title: Task 1: Add trigger/description to schema and create-trigger
design:
## Goal
Add :trigger/description attribute to schema and pass it through the trigger creation pipeline so triggers on the stack include their description.

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- db/schema.cljs:64-72 - trigger schema attributes
- engine/triggers.cljs:14-31 - create-trigger function (receives data, returns trigger map)
- engine/triggers.cljs:240-246 - fire-matching-triggers (calls create-trigger, only passes :effects)
- engine/triggers.cljs:296-302 - fire-and-resolve-triggers (same pattern, also needs update)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create test in test/fizzle/engine/triggers_test.cljs:
- test_trigger_with_description_includes_description_in_data
  - Create trigger with {:trigger/description "deals 1 damage"}
  - Verify trigger map has :trigger/description in :trigger/data
- test_trigger_without_description_still_works
  - Create trigger without :trigger/description
  - Verify trigger map created successfully (backwards compatible)

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] db/schema.cljs:72 - Add `:trigger/description {}` after :trigger/data
- [ ] engine/triggers.cljs:31 - Add :trigger/description to create-trigger return map:
      ```clojure
      {:trigger/id (random-uuid)
       :trigger/type trigger-type
       :trigger/source source-id
       :trigger/controller controller-id
       :trigger/data data
       :trigger/description (:description data)}  ;; NEW: extract from data
      ```
- [ ] engine/triggers.cljs:245 - Update fire-matching-triggers to pass description:
      ```clojure
      {:effects (:trigger/effects trigger)
       :description (:trigger/description trigger)}  ;; NEW
      ```
- [ ] engine/triggers.cljs:301 - Update fire-and-resolve-triggers same way:
      ```clojure
      {:effects (:trigger/effects trigger)
       :description (:trigger/description trigger)}  ;; NEW
      ```
- [ ] Run `make test` - verify existing tests pass
- [ ] Run `make validate` - verify lint/format pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :trigger/description attribute exists in schema (db/schema.cljs)
- [ ] create-trigger extracts description from data map and includes in return value
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers passes :trigger/description in data map
- [ ] fire-and-resolve-triggers passes :trigger/description in data map
- [ ] Test: trigger with description has it in :trigger/description
- [ ] Test: trigger without description still works (nil description OK)
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Data Flow Understanding (CRITICAL)**:
The description flows: card definition → fire-matching-triggers → create-trigger → trigger on stack
- Card has `:card/triggers [{:trigger/type :x :trigger/description "..." :trigger/effects [...]}]`
- fire-matching-triggers reads the trigger from card, must pass description in data
- create-trigger receives data map, extracts description to top-level attribute
- Trigger entity stored in Datascript with :trigger/description

**Backwards Compatibility**:
- Triggers without :trigger/description must still work
- (:trigger/description trigger) returns nil when not present - this is OK
- Do NOT require description, just support it

**Two Call Sites**:
- fire-matching-triggers (line 240-246) - for stack-based triggers
- fire-and-resolve-triggers (line 296-302) - for immediate triggers (mana abilities)
- BOTH must be updated to pass description

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO adding description as required field (must be optional for backwards compatibility)
- ❌ NO modifying create-trigger signature (keep 4 args, pass description in data map)
- ❌ NO forgetting fire-and-resolve-triggers (both call sites must be updated)

title: Task 5: Implement Mental Note card
design:
## Goal
Implement Mental Note instant card that mills 2 from own library and draws 1.

## Context
Epic fizzle-6vv requires Mental Note as one of the Phase 2 cards. All other cards (Island, Swamp, Lotus Petal, LED, Careful Study) are complete.

Existing patterns in iggy_pop.cljs show:
- :mill effect with :effect/target :opponent works for Brain Freeze
- :draw effect works with :effect/amount
- For self-mill, omit :effect/target or use explicit player targeting

## Implementation
1. Add mental-note card definition to iggy_pop.cljs:
   - Cost: U (1 blue)
   - Type: Instant  
   - Text: 'Put the top two cards of your library into your graveyard, then draw a card.'
   - Effects: [{:effect/type :mill :effect/amount 2} {:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 1}]

2. Add to all-cards vector

3. Write test verifying Mental Note works correctly

## Verification Commands
- make test
- make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Mental Note card defined with correct cost/type/effects
- [ ] Card added to all-cards vector
- [ ] Test verifies mill 2 + draw 1 behavior
- [ ] make validate passes
close_reason: Mental Note card implemented with tests

title: Implement storm trigger resolution and cast-spell integration
design:
## Goal

Implement the storm keyword behavior: when a spell with :storm is cast, create a storm trigger on the stack. When that trigger resolves, create N copies of the spell (where N = spells cast before this turn).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context

Completed fizzle-21w: Trigger infrastructure in place (create-trigger, add-trigger-to-stack, resolve-trigger multimethod, remove-trigger, get-stack-items).

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

Reference implementations:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs - resolve-trigger multimethod pattern
- src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:38-53 - cast-spell flow
- src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:56-69 - resolve-spell flow
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:31-39 - get-storm-count

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/storm_test.cljs

Test cases with bug-catching rationale:
- [ ] test-cast-storm-spell-creates-trigger: Cast spell with :storm keyword creates trigger on stack (catches: missing trigger creation)
- [ ] test-storm-trigger-on-top-of-spell: Storm trigger has higher stack-order than spell (catches: wrong resolution order)
- [ ] test-resolve-storm-creates-copies: Storm trigger resolution creates N copies where N = storm count BEFORE cast (catches: off-by-one error)
- [ ] test-storm-copies-have-is-copy-flag: Copies have :object/is-copy true (catches: missing copy flag)
- [ ] test-storm-copies-do-not-increment-storm: Casting 1 spell, then storm with 1 copy, storm count still 2 (catches: copies incrementing count)
- [ ] test-storm-count-zero-creates-no-copies: Storm as first spell creates 0 copies (catches: off-by-one at boundary)
- [ ] test-storm-count-one-creates-one-copy: Storm after 1 spell creates 1 copy (catches: calculation error)
- [ ] test-storm-count-two-creates-two-copies: Storm after 2 spells creates 2 copies (catches: calculation error)
- [ ] test-storm-source-missing-returns-unchanged-db: If source object gone, resolve-trigger returns db unchanged (catches: nil reference crash)

### 3. Implementation checklist

Schema addition (src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs):
- [ ] Add :object/is-copy {} - Boolean, marks spell copies (default: false/nil)

Add to src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs:
- [ ] defmethod resolve-trigger :storm [db trigger]
  - Get source-id from (:trigger/source trigger)
  - Get copy-count from (:count (:trigger/data trigger))
  - If source object not found, return db unchanged (defensive)
  - Loop copy-count times, calling create-spell-copy for each
  - Return final db with all copies on stack
- [ ] create-spell-copy [db source-object-id controller-id] -> db with copy on stack
  - Query source object to get :object/card ref
  - Create new object with :object/id (random-uuid), :object/card same-ref, :object/zone :stack, :object/owner/controller from controller-id, :object/is-copy true
  - Transact and return db
  - Note: Copy inherits card but :object/is-copy true prevents storm cascade

Modify src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:
- [ ] Add require for fizzle.engine.triggers
- [ ] cast-spell: After increment-storm, check for :storm keyword in card
  - (when (contains? (:card/keywords card) :storm) ...)
  - Get current storm count (after increment)
  - Create trigger with {:count (dec storm-count)} - copies = spells BEFORE this one
  - Call (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack db (triggers/create-trigger :storm object-id player-id {:count (dec storm-count)}))
  - Note: Storm count is AFTER increment, so dec gives spells cast before

Modify src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs (if needed):
- [ ] Update get-stack-items to include both triggers AND spell objects, ordered by position
  - Current: Only returns triggers
  - Needed: Return [{:type :trigger :item trigger} {:type :spell :item object}] for full stack view

### 4. Key invariants

- Storm count for copies = current storm count - 1 (not including the storm spell itself)
- Copies are created by trigger resolution, NOT by cast-spell
- Copies do not trigger storm (checked via :object/is-copy flag, NOT by removing keyword)
- Copies have :object/is-copy true
- If source object missing at resolution time, return db unchanged (no crash)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Casting spell with :storm creates storm trigger on top of spell
- [ ] Storm trigger resolves to create N copies (N = spells cast BEFORE storm spell)
- [ ] Copies have :object/is-copy true
- [ ] Copies do not increment storm count (verify: cast 1 spell, cast storm, count = 2 not 3)
- [ ] Copies do not trigger storm (verify: :object/is-copy prevents cascade check)
- [ ] Edge case: Storm as first spell (count 0) creates 0 copies
- [ ] Edge case: Source object missing at resolution returns db unchanged
- [ ] 9 unit tests pass (8 functional + 1 edge case)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass: make test
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: make validate

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Storm Count Zero**
- Storm as first spell has storm count 1 after increment
- Copies = 1 - 1 = 0
- MUST handle this gracefully (no copies created, no error)
- Test: test-storm-count-zero-creates-no-copies

**Edge Case: Source Object Missing**
- Source spell might be exiled or otherwise removed before trigger resolves
- resolve-trigger :storm MUST check if source exists
- If nil, return db unchanged (defensive, no crash)
- Test: test-storm-source-missing-returns-unchanged-db

**Preventing Storm Cascade**
- Copies must NOT trigger storm themselves
- Two options: (a) Remove :storm keyword from copy, (b) Check :object/is-copy in cast-spell
- CHOSEN: Option (b) - Don't modify card keywords, check flag instead
- Rationale: Card keywords are immutable; flag is cleaner
- Implementation: In cast-spell, only create storm trigger if NOT :object/is-copy

**Off-by-One Risk**
- Storm count is incremented BEFORE trigger creation
- So count after cast = total spells including this one
- Copies needed = count - 1 = spells BEFORE this one
- CRITICAL: Use (dec storm-count) not storm-count
- Test: test-storm-count-one-creates-one-copy verifies this

**Stack Ordering**
- Trigger must resolve BEFORE spell (copies resolve first)
- Trigger has higher stack-order than spell
- LIFO: Higher stack-order = resolves first
- Verified by test-storm-trigger-on-top-of-spell

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO incrementing storm count for copies (copies are not cast)
- ❌ NO storm cascade (copies must not trigger storm)
- ❌ NO creating copies during cast-spell (copies created by trigger resolution)
- ❌ NO removing :storm keyword from copies (check :object/is-copy flag instead)
- ❌ NO crashing on nil source (defensive check, return unchanged db)
- ❌ NO off-by-one in copy count (use dec of post-increment storm count)

title: Task 1: Set up re-frame infrastructure and entry point
design:
## Goal Create the foundational infrastructure for the UI: add re-frame dependency, create index.html, create core.cljs entry point that initializes re-frame and mounts a minimal root component. ## Effort Estimate 2-3 hours ## Implementation ### 1. Add re-frame dependency Edit shadow-cljs.edn to add re-frame to dependencies: ```clojure :dependencies [[datascript "1.7.3"] [reagent "1.2.0"] [re-frame "1.4.3"]] ``` ### 2. Create resources/public directory and index.html ```bash mkdir -p resources/public ``` ```html Fizzle
``` ### 3. Create src/main/fizzle/core.cljs ```clojure (ns fizzle.core (:require [reagent.dom :as rdom] [re-frame.core :as rf])) ;; Placeholder app-db (will add game events in next task) (rf/reg-event-db ::init-db (fn [_ _] {:message "Fizzle loading..."})) (rf/reg-sub ::message (fn [db _] (:message db))) (defn app [] (let [msg @(rf/subscribe [::message])] [:div {:style {:padding "20px"}} [:h1 "Fizzle"] [:p msg]])) (defn ^:dev/after-load mount-root [] (rf/clear-subscription-cache!) (rdom/render [app] (.getElementById js/document "app"))) (defn init [] (rf/dispatch-sync [::init-db]) (mount-root)) ``` ### 4. Verify it works Run `make dev` and open http://localhost:8080 Should see "Fizzle" heading and "Fizzle loading..." message ## Success Criteria - [ ] `make dev` starts without errors (watch for "Build completed" in terminal) - [ ] Browser at http://localhost:8080 shows "Fizzle" heading and "Fizzle loading..." text - [ ] Hot reload works: edit message string in core.cljs, save, browser updates without refresh - [ ] `make test` passes (all 75 existing tests still green) - [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format-check + tests) ## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN) - ❌ NO game logic in core.cljs (this is just infrastructure - game events come in next task) - ❌ NO direct DOM manipulation (use Reagent's rdom/render, not js interop) - ❌ NO hardcoded port numbers in code (shadow-cljs.edn handles this) ## Key Considerations (Edge Cases & Troubleshooting) **If shadow-cljs fails to compile:** - Check re-frame version compatibility (1.4.3 works with Reagent 1.2.0) - Run `npm install` to ensure shadow-cljs is installed - Check for typos in namespace requires **If port 8080 in use:** - shadow-cljs will show error with port conflict - Either kill the process using 8080 or edit shadow-cljs.edn :dev-http port - Use `lsof -i :8080` to find conflicting process **If hot reload doesn't work:** - Ensure `^:dev/after-load` metadata is on mount-root function - Check browser console for errors - Hard refresh (Cmd+Shift+R) to clear cache **Reference:** - shadow-cljs.edn already configured for :browser target with fizzle.core/init entry point - Existing source at src/main/fizzle/ (db/, engine/, cards/)
close_reason: Re-frame infrastructure set up. shadow-cljs.edn updated with re-frame dep, package.json updated with react/react-dom deps, index.html created, core.cljs entry point with placeholder app working. make dev serves at localhost:8080, all 75 tests pass, make validate passes.

title: Task 1: Implement basic lands (Island, Swamp)
design:
## Goal
Add Island and Swamp basic land cards following existing land patterns.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
Existing patterns in iggy_pop.cljs show land structure (City of Brass, Gemstone Mine).
Basic lands are simpler - just tap for single color, no triggers or counters.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:57-76 (City of Brass pattern)
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:82-105 (Gemstone Mine pattern)
- Note: basics have :card/supertypes #{:basic} and :card/subtypes #{:island}

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Add tests to: src/test/fizzle/cards/basic_lands_test.cljs (new file)

Test cases:
```clojure
;; test_island_taps_for_blue_mana
;; Setup: Island on battlefield, untapped
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :blue
;; Verify: mana pool has {:blue 1}, island is tapped

;; test_swamp_taps_for_black_mana
;; Setup: Swamp on battlefield, untapped
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :black
;; Verify: mana pool has {:black 1}, swamp is tapped

;; test_basic_land_played_from_hand
;; Setup: Island in hand, land plays remaining
;; Action: play-land
;; Verify: Island on battlefield, land plays decremented

;; test_tapped_land_cannot_tap_again
;; Setup: Island on battlefield, already tapped
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability
;; Verify: returns unchanged db (can-activate? false)

;; test_land_in_hand_cannot_activate_mana
;; Setup: Island in hand (not battlefield)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability
;; Verify: returns unchanged db (zone check fails)
```

### 3. Add card definitions
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
(def island
  {:card/id :island
   :card/name "Island"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/subtypes #{:island}
   :card/supertypes #{:basic}
   :card/text "{T}: Add {U}."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true}
                     :ability/produces {:blue 1}}]})

(def swamp
  {:card/id :swamp
   :card/name "Swamp"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/subtypes #{:swamp}
   :card/supertypes #{:basic}
   :card/text "{T}: Add {B}."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true}
                     :ability/produces {:black 1}}]})
```

### 4. Update all-cards vector
Add island and swamp to the all-cards def at bottom of iggy_pop.cljs:
```clojure
(def all-cards
  [dark-ritual brain-freeze cabal-ritual city-of-brass gemstone-mine island swamp])
```

### 5. Run validation
- make test
- make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Island definition exists in iggy_pop.cljs with :card/supertypes #{:basic}
- [ ] Swamp definition exists in iggy_pop.cljs with :card/supertypes #{:basic}
- [ ] Island has :card/subtypes #{:island}
- [ ] Swamp has :card/subtypes #{:swamp}
- [ ] Both included in all-cards vector
- [ ] 5 tests in basic_lands_test.cljs all pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in engine code (data-over-code: cards are pure data)
- ❌ NO skipping TDD (write failing tests first, then implement)
- ❌ NO modifying activate-mana-ability for basic lands (should work generically)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Already Tapped**
- Existing can-activate? checks :object/tapped via costs/can-pay?
- Verify this works for basic lands (test_tapped_land_cannot_tap_again)

**Edge Case: Wrong Zone**
- Mana abilities only work on battlefield
- activate-mana-ability checks :object/zone in validation
- Verify with test_land_in_hand_cannot_activate_mana

**Pattern Consistency**
- Follow exact structure from City of Brass / Gemstone Mine
- Use :ability/produces for UI display (shows mana symbol on land)
- Use :ability/cost {:tap true} not {:cost/tap true}

**Reference Implementation**
- City of Brass at iggy_pop.cljs:57-76 is closest pattern
- Note: basic lands don't need :card/triggers (no damage on tap)

title: Add missing corner cases for triggers module
description:
## Goal
Add missing corner case tests for engine/triggers module.

## Corner Cases to Add

### Trigger validation
- test-trigger-with-nil-controller-graceful
  Verify trigger with nil controller doesn't crash
  Should return unchanged db or handle gracefully

### Replacement effects

  Between trigger creation and resolution

Follow established patterns (add-permanent, tap-permanent, etc.)

## Success Criteria

close_reason: Added 3 corner case tests: test-trigger-with-nil-controller-graceful (nil controller handling), test-trigger-referencing-destroyed-permanent (destroyed source in check-trigger), test-storm-trigger-with-destroyed-source (storm with missing source). All tests pass.

title: Task 1: Create Makefile with Core Commands
design:
## Goal

Create Makefile with core development commands: repl, dev, test, clean. This establishes the primary interface before adding linting/formatting.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create Makefile

```makefile
.PHONY: repl dev test clean help

# Detect Java - try common locations
JAVA_HOME ?= $(shell \
  if [ -d "/usr/local/opt/openjdk" ]; then echo "/usr/local/opt/openjdk"; \
  elif [ -d "/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk" ]; then echo "/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk"; \
  elif [ -n "$$JAVA_HOME" ]; then echo "$$JAVA_HOME"; \
  else echo ""; fi)

ifneq ($(JAVA_HOME),)
  export PATH := $(JAVA_HOME)/bin:$(PATH)
endif

help:
	@echo "Fizzle Development Commands"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make repl     - Start ClojureScript REPL (node)"
	@echo "  make dev      - Start browser dev server"
	@echo "  make test     - Run all tests"
	@echo "  make clean    - Remove build artifacts"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Validation (after linting added):"
	@echo "  make validate - Run all checks (lint + fmt + test)"

repl:
	npx shadow-cljs node-repl

dev:
	npx shadow-cljs watch app

test:
	npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js

clean:
	rm -rf out/ .shadow-cljs/ resources/public/js/
```

### 2. Update CLAUDE.md

Add this section after Build Commands:

```markdown
## Development Commands

Use these make commands instead of hand-rolling shadow-cljs calls:

\`\`\`bash
# Development
make repl      # Start node REPL - ready to eval immediately
make dev       # Start browser dev server with hot reload
make test      # Run all tests
make clean     # Remove build artifacts (out/, .shadow-cljs/)

# Validation (after Phase 2)
make validate  # Run lint + format-check + tests
\`\`\`

**IMPORTANT**: Always use \`make test\` instead of raw shadow-cljs commands.
```

### 3. Verify all commands work

- [ ] `make help` shows available commands
- [ ] `make repl` starts REPL, can eval `(+ 1 2)` and `(require '[fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]])`
- [ ] `make test` runs 40 tests, all pass
- [ ] `make dev` starts watch (ctrl-c to exit)
- [ ] `make clean` removes out/ and .shadow-cljs/

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Makefile exists at project root with repl, dev, test, clean, help targets
- [ ] `make repl` starts REPL in one command without PATH export
- [ ] `make test` runs all 40 tests, outputs pass count
- [ ] `make help` displays all available commands
- [ ] `make clean` removes out/, .shadow-cljs/, resources/public/js/
- [ ] CLAUDE.md has "Development Commands" section with make commands
- [ ] CLAUDE.md instructs agent to use make commands
- [ ] Changes committed

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Java Not Installed**
- Makefile attempts to detect Java in common locations
- If Java not found, shadow-cljs will fail with clear error message
- User must install Java separately (documented in README)

**Edge Case: Different JAVA_HOME Locations**
- macOS Intel Homebrew: /usr/local/opt/openjdk
- macOS ARM Homebrew: /opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk
- Linux: Typically in PATH already or JAVA_HOME set
- Makefile checks multiple locations, falls back to existing JAVA_HOME/PATH

**Edge Case: npm not installed**
- npx requires Node.js/npm
- Error message will be clear: "npx: command not found"
- Prerequisite, not something Makefile can fix

**Portability**
- Makefile uses POSIX shell commands (works on macOS/Linux)
- Windows users need WSL or alternative (out of scope)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO hard-coded absolute paths without fallbacks (portability)
- ❌ NO manual PATH exports required before running make (usability)
- ❌ NO npm scripts duplicating Makefile commands (single source of truth)
- ❌ NO undocumented commands (help target lists all)

title: Add missing corner case tests for engine/rules
description:
Add P0 corner case tests for critical game logic:

1. test-resolve-spell-not-on-stack - Verify graceful handling when resolving spell in wrong zone
2. test-cast-negative-cost - Handle edge case of malformed card data

title: Add trigger infrastructure to stack system
design:
## Goal

Implement the foundational trigger system that allows triggered abilities (like storm) to exist as objects on the stack. This is the foundation for storm copies and future triggered abilities.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

Reference implementations:
- db/schema.cljs:40-60 - Object schema pattern (follow this for trigger schema)
- engine/rules.cljs:56-69 - resolve-spell pattern (follow for resolve-trigger)
- engine/zones.cljs:1-30 - Zone transition pattern

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/triggers_test.cljs

Test cases with specific bug prevention:
- [ ] test-create-trigger-structure: Verify create-trigger returns map with all required keys (:trigger/id, :trigger/type, :trigger/source, :trigger/controller, :trigger/data) - catches missing fields bug
- [ ] test-create-trigger-unique-id: Verify each call to create-trigger returns unique :trigger/id - catches duplicate ID bug
- [ ] test-add-trigger-to-stack-transacts: Verify trigger appears in db after add-trigger-to-stack - catches silent failure bug
- [ ] test-add-trigger-with-nil-source-fails: Verify add-trigger-to-stack with nil source returns error/unchanged db - catches nil reference bug
- [ ] test-resolve-trigger-dispatches-storm: Verify (resolve-trigger db {:trigger/type :storm ...}) calls storm handler - catches dispatch bug
- [ ] test-resolve-trigger-default-noop: Verify unknown :trigger/type returns db unchanged - catches mutation on unknown type bug
- [ ] test-remove-trigger-retracts: Verify trigger not in db after remove-trigger - catches incomplete cleanup bug
- [ ] test-get-stack-items-empty: Verify get-stack-items returns [] on empty stack - catches nil-vs-empty bug
- [ ] test-get-stack-items-ordered: Verify triggers added later appear first (LIFO stack order) - catches ordering bug
- [ ] test-get-trigger-not-found: Verify get-trigger with invalid ID returns nil - catches exception on missing bug

### 3. Implementation checklist

Schema changes (db/schema.cljs):
- [ ] Add :trigger/id {:db/unique :db.unique/identity} - UUID, unique identifier
- [ ] Add :trigger/type {:db/valueType :db.type/keyword} - e.g. :storm, :etb
- [ ] Add :trigger/source {:db/valueType :db.type/ref} - ref to source object
- [ ] Add :trigger/controller {:db/valueType :db.type/ref} - ref to player
- [ ] Add :trigger/data {} - map for trigger-specific data (no special db type)
- [ ] Add :trigger/stack-order {:db/valueType :db.type/long} - integer for LIFO ordering

New file engine/triggers.cljs:
- [ ] create-trigger [trigger-type source-id controller-id data] -> {:trigger/id (random-uuid) :trigger/type trigger-type :trigger/source source-id :trigger/controller controller-id :trigger/data data}
- [ ] add-trigger-to-stack [db trigger] -> db with trigger transacted, :trigger/stack-order set to max+1
- [ ] resolve-trigger multimethod (fn [db trigger] ...) dispatches on (:trigger/type trigger)
- [ ] defmethod resolve-trigger :default [db trigger] -> db (no-op, logs warning)
- [ ] remove-trigger [db trigger-id] -> db with trigger entity retracted

Query additions (db/queries.cljs):
- [ ] get-stack-items [db] -> vector of maps {:type :spell|:trigger :item <object-or-trigger>} ordered by stack position (most recent first)
- [ ] get-trigger [db trigger-id] -> trigger map or nil
- [ ] get-next-stack-order [db] -> integer (current max + 1, or 0 if empty)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Trigger schema in db/schema.cljs with 6 attributes (:trigger/id, :trigger/type, :trigger/source, :trigger/controller, :trigger/data, :trigger/stack-order)
- [ ] create-trigger returns map with all 5 core keys (not stack-order, that's set by add-trigger-to-stack)
- [ ] Each create-trigger call produces unique UUID
- [ ] add-trigger-to-stack transacts trigger to db with correct stack-order
- [ ] get-stack-items returns triggers and spells in LIFO order
- [ ] resolve-trigger :default returns db unchanged (no mutation)
- [ ] remove-trigger fully retracts trigger from db
- [ ] 10 unit tests pass covering structure, ordering, dispatch, cleanup, edge cases
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (make test)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO trigger without :trigger/id (every trigger must have unique ID)
- ❌ NO add-trigger-to-stack with nil source-id (must validate source exists)
- ❌ NO resolve-trigger mutating db for unknown types (default must be pure no-op)
- ❌ NO hardcoded stack ordering (use :trigger/stack-order for proper LIFO)
- ❌ NO triggers leaking after resolution (remove-trigger must fully retract)
- ❌ NO mixing trigger logic with spell logic in rules.cljs (triggers.cljs is separate)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases & Failure Modes)

**Edge Case: Nil Source Reference**
- What if trigger created with nil or invalid source-id?
- MUST validate source exists before transacting
- Return unchanged db (or throw) rather than creating orphan trigger

**Edge Case: Unknown Trigger Type**
- What if resolve-trigger called with :trigger/type not registered?
- MUST fall through to :default method
- :default returns db unchanged, logs warning for debugging
- Do NOT throw exception (breaks stack resolution)

**Edge Case: Empty Stack**
- get-stack-items on empty stack MUST return [] not nil
- Callers should be able to iterate without nil check

**Edge Case: Trigger Already Resolved**
- What if resolve-trigger called on trigger not in db?
- get-trigger returns nil, caller should check before resolving
- resolve-trigger should handle gracefully (no-op if trigger missing)

**Edge Case: Stack Order Overflow**
- :trigger/stack-order is long, but what if millions of triggers?
- Not a real concern for MTG (stack rarely exceeds 100)
- Document: stack-order is per-game, resets on new game

**Performance Consideration**
- get-stack-items query should be efficient (single Datalog query)
- Avoid N+1 queries when resolving stack

**Reference Implementation Pattern**
- Follow db/schema.cljs :object/* pattern for :trigger/* schema
- Follow engine/rules.cljs resolve-spell for resolve-trigger structure

## Notes

This task does NOT implement:
- Storm trigger specifically (next task)
- Integration with cast-spell (next task)
- Any specific trigger types beyond :default

Focus on clean, extensible infrastructure that future triggers can build on.

title: Implement flashback system and Deep Analysis
design:
## Goal
Implement the flashback mechanic and Deep Analysis card to complete Phase 5 of the epic.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
Completed: Tutor system (Merchant Scroll), Land-entered triggers (City of Traitors), Fetchlands (Polluted Delta), Event system migration (City of Brass). Missing: Flashback system and Deep Analysis card.

## Implementation Checklist

### Step 1: Add flashback cost property schema
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- Add :card/flashback-cost property to card definitions
- Structure: {:mana {:blue 1 :colorless 1} :life 3} for Deep Analysis
- Reference: Study existing cost structures in lion's-eye-diamond, polluted-delta

### Step 2: Extend can-cast? for graveyard zone
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs
- Current code (line 28): `(= :hand (:object/zone obj))` - hardcoded hand check
- Extend to: Check hand OR (graveyard AND has flashback-cost)
- When in graveyard with flashback: use :card/flashback-cost for mana check
- When in graveyard with flashback: check life cost if present (player life >= cost)
```clojure
;; Pseudocode for can-cast? extension:
(let [zone (:object/zone obj)
      has-flashback (:card/flashback-cost card)]
  (cond
    (= :hand zone) (mana/can-pay? db player-id (:card/mana-cost card))
    (and (= :graveyard zone) has-flashback)
    (let [fb-cost (:card/flashback-cost card)]
      (and (mana/can-pay? db player-id (:mana fb-cost))
           (or (nil? (:life fb-cost))
               (>= (q/get-player-life db player-id) (:life fb-cost)))))
    :else false))
```

### Step 3: Extend cast-spell for flashback
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs
- Detect if casting from graveyard (check :object/zone before cast)
- If graveyard + has flashback: pay flashback cost (mana + life), not normal cost
- If flashback has life cost: deduct life using existing drain/life-loss pattern
- Mark object with :object/cast-via-flashback true for resolution tracking
```clojure
;; Add to object before moving to stack:
[:db/add obj-eid :object/cast-via-flashback true]
```

### Step 4: Handle exile-on-resolution in resolve-spell
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs (resolve-spell, lines 127-149)
- Current code sends instants/sorceries to :graveyard (line 144)
- Add check: if :object/cast-via-flashback true, destination = :exile
- This is a replacement effect - applies even if spell would otherwise go to graveyard
```clojure
;; Modified destination logic:
(let [cast-via-flashback (:object/cast-via-flashback obj)
      destination (cond
                    cast-via-flashback :exile
                    (permanent-type? card-types) :battlefield
                    :else :graveyard)]
  ...)
```

### Step 5: Implement Deep Analysis card
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- Card ID: :deep-analysis
- Name: "Deep Analysis"
- Mana cost: {:colorless 3 :blue 1} (normal cast)
- Flashback cost: {:mana {:colorless 1 :blue 1} :life 3}
- Type: #{:sorcery}
- Colors: #{:blue}
- Effect: Draw 2 cards (targeting caster only for simplicity)
  - Use existing :draw effect type
  - Note: Original card says "Target player draws two cards and loses 2 life"
  - For Iggy Pop practice, self-target only is acceptable (always draws for caster)
- Life loss is PART OF THE EFFECT (2 life on normal cast), not the cost
- Flashback life cost (3 life) is ADDITIONAL to the effect's 2 life loss
```clojure
(def deep-analysis
  {:card/id :deep-analysis
   :card/name "Deep Analysis"
   :card/cmc 4
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 3 :blue 1}
   :card/flashback-cost {:mana {:colorless 1 :blue 1} :life 3}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:sorcery}
   :card/text "Target player draws two cards and loses 2 life. Flashback {1}{U}, Pay 3 life."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 2}
                  {:effect/type :drain :effect/amount 2}]}) ;; drain targets self = lose life
```

### Step 6: Add flashback tests
File: src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs (NEW FILE)
Reference: src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs for test patterns

Tests to implement:
1. test-deep-analysis-in-hand-castable-with-mana
   - Card in hand, player has 3UU mana pool
   - can-cast? returns true
   - Bug caught: Ensures normal casting path still works

2. test-deep-analysis-in-graveyard-castable-with-flashback-mana-and-life
   - Card in graveyard, player has 1U mana and 3+ life
   - can-cast? returns true
   - Bug caught: Verifies flashback path enables casting from graveyard

3. test-deep-analysis-in-graveyard-not-castable-insufficient-life
   - Card in graveyard, player has 1U mana but only 2 life
   - can-cast? returns false
   - Bug caught: Ensures life cost is checked, prevents death-before-resolution

4. test-deep-analysis-in-graveyard-not-castable-insufficient-mana
   - Card in graveyard, player has 3 life but 0 mana
   - can-cast? returns false
   - Bug caught: Ensures mana cost still required

5. test-flashback-cast-pays-correct-costs
   - Cast from graveyard, verify 1U mana deducted, 3 life deducted
   - Bug caught: Ensures flashback cost (not normal cost) is paid

6. test-flashback-spell-exiles-on-resolution
   - Cast from graveyard, resolve
   - Object ends up in :exile zone, not :graveyard
   - Bug caught: Core flashback behavior - exile replacement effect

7. test-normal-cast-spell-goes-to-graveyard
   - Cast from hand, resolve
   - Object ends up in :graveyard zone (normal behavior preserved)
   - Bug caught: Regression - flashback changes don't break normal path

8. test-deep-analysis-draws-two-loses-two
   - Cast and resolve
   - Player hand +2 cards, life -2
   - Bug caught: Effect implementation correct

9. test-flashback-not-castable-from-exile
   - Card in exile zone with flashback
   - can-cast? returns false
   - Bug caught: Flashback only works from graveyard

### Step 7: Update all-cards in iggy_pop.cljs
- Add deep-analysis to all-cards vector

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Deep Analysis card defined with :card/flashback-cost {:mana {:colorless 1 :blue 1} :life 3}
- [ ] can-cast? returns true for flashback card in graveyard when player has mana AND life
- [ ] can-cast? returns false for flashback card in graveyard when player lacks life (< 3)
- [ ] cast-spell deducts flashback cost (not normal cost) when casting from graveyard
- [ ] cast-spell deducts life when flashback cost includes life
- [ ] Flashback cast marks object with :object/cast-via-flashback true
- [ ] resolve-spell sends :object/cast-via-flashback cards to :exile (not :graveyard)
- [ ] Normal cast (from hand) still sends to :graveyard (no regression)
- [ ] Deep Analysis effect: draw 2, lose 2 life
- [ ] All 9 flashback tests pass
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make test)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Life Cost Payment Timing**
- Flashback life cost is paid as part of casting (like mana)
- Must check life >= cost BEFORE allowing cast
- If player at 3 life casts flashback costing 3 life: allowed (goes to 0)
- If player at 2 life: cannot cast (insufficient resources)

**Deep Analysis Has Two Life Costs**
- Effect causes 2 life loss (target player loses 2 life)
- Flashback cost includes 3 life payment
- Total life loss when flashback casting: 5 life (3 cost + 2 effect)
- This is correct per Oracle text

**Flashback Only From Graveyard**
- Cards in exile with flashback: NOT castable
- Cards in hand with flashback: use normal cost
- Cards in library: not castable (can't cast from library)
- Only graveyard zone enables flashback alternate cost

**Replacement Effect for Destination**
- "If this spell would go to graveyard, exile it instead"
- This applies regardless of HOW it would go to graveyard:
  - Normal resolution: effect happens, then exile
  - Countered: counter tries to send to GY, replacement exiles
- Current code: resolve-spell handles resolution destination
- Counter implementation (if exists): may need separate handling
- Note: Counter system not implemented yet, so only resolution path needed

**No Flashback Without Cost**
- Card without :card/flashback-cost cannot be cast from graveyard
- Ensure can-cast? returns false for non-flashback cards in graveyard

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Deep Analysis specifically - flashback system must be generic
- ❌ NO checking flashback in resolve-spell by card name - use :object/cast-via-flashback flag
- ❌ NO allowing cast from graveyard without checking life cost (if present)
- ❌ NO breaking normal cast path for flashback cards (must still cast from hand normally)
- ❌ NO sending flashback spells to graveyard on resolution
close_reason: Deferred to future epic - scope too large for current batch. Epic delivered tutor system, land-entered triggers, fetchlands, and event system migration. Flashback system well-documented for future implementation.

title: Create engine/costs.cljs with multimethod for :tap and :remove-counter
design:
## Goal
Implement engine/costs.cljs with multimethod dispatch for cost payment. Support :tap and :remove-counter cost types.

## Context
Completed fizzle-isp: Schema fields and effect types in place. Now need cost payment system that abilities.cljs will use.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Implementation Steps

### 1. RED: Write tests for pay-cost multimethod
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/costs_test.cljs (new file)

Follow pattern from effects_test.cljs - use add-permanent helper.

**:tap cost tests (6 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-pay-tap-cost-untapped-permanent
  (testing "pay-cost with :tap on untapped permanent taps it and returns db"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent on battlefield
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:tap true})
    ;; Assert: returned db has permanent tapped, not nil
    ))

(deftest test-pay-tap-cost-already-tapped
  (testing "pay-cost with :tap on already tapped permanent returns nil"
    ;; Setup: tapped permanent on battlefield
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:tap true})
    ;; Assert: returns nil (cost cannot be paid)
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-tap-untapped
  (testing "can-pay? :tap returns true for untapped permanent"
    ;; Catches: missing can-pay? implementation
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-tap-already-tapped
  (testing "can-pay? :tap returns false for tapped permanent"
    ;; Catches: can-pay? not checking tapped state
    ))

(deftest test-pay-tap-invalid-object-id
  (testing "pay-cost with invalid object-id returns nil, no crash"
    ;; Catches: missing nil guard on object lookup
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-tap-invalid-object-id
  (testing "can-pay? with invalid object-id returns false, no crash"
    ;; Catches: missing nil guard
    ))
```

**:remove-counter cost tests (6 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-pay-remove-counter-has-counters
  (testing "pay-cost removes counter from permanent with sufficient counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: permanent now has {:mining 2}
    ;; Catches: counter not decremented
    ))

(deftest test-pay-remove-counter-exact-amount
  (testing "pay-cost removes last counter (edge case: counter goes to 0)"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 1}
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: permanent has {:mining 0} (counter exists with 0 value)
    ;; Catches: off-by-one error
    ))

(deftest test-pay-remove-counter-insufficient
  (testing "pay-cost returns nil when insufficient counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 1}
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:remove-counter {:mining 2}})
    ;; Assert: returns nil, counters unchanged
    ;; Catches: paying cost even when can't afford
    ))

(deftest test-pay-remove-counter-no-counters-at-all
  (testing "pay-cost returns nil when permanent has no counters (nil :object/counters)"
    ;; Setup: permanent with nil :object/counters
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: returns nil
    ;; Catches: nil pointer when counters map missing
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-remove-counter-has-counters
  (testing "can-pay? returns true when counters sufficient"
    ;; Catches: can-pay? not checking counter count
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-remove-counter-insufficient
  (testing "can-pay? returns false when counters insufficient"
    ;; Catches: can-pay? returning true when can't pay
    ))
```

### 2. GREEN: Implement engine/costs.cljs

Create new file: src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.costs
  "Cost payment system for abilities.
   
   Dispatches on cost type to pay costs.
   All functions are pure: (db, object-id, cost) -> db or nil."
  (:require
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]))


(defmulti pay-cost
  "Pay a single cost on an object.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent paying the cost
     cost - Map with single cost type key, e.g. {:tap true} or {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}
   
   Returns:
     New db with cost paid, or nil if cost cannot be paid."
  (fn [_db _object-id cost] (first (keys cost))))


(defmulti can-pay?
  "Check if a cost can be paid.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent
     cost - Map with single cost type key
   
   Returns:
     Boolean - true if cost can be paid, false otherwise."
  (fn [_db _object-id cost] (first (keys cost))))


;; Default: unknown cost types cannot be paid
(defmethod pay-cost :default [_db _object-id _cost] nil)
(defmethod can-pay? :default [_db _object-id _cost] false)
```

### 3. Implement :tap cost type

```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :tap [db object-id _cost]
  ;; Can pay tap cost if:
  ;; 1. Object exists
  ;; 2. Object is not already tapped
  (when-let [obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (not (d/q '[:find ?tapped .
                :in $ ?e
                :where [?e :object/tapped ?tapped]]
              db obj-eid))))


(defmethod pay-cost :tap [db object-id cost]
  ;; Guard: check can pay first
  (when (can-pay? db object-id cost)
    (let [obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/tapped true]]))))
```

Note: May need to add q/get-object-eid to queries.cljs if it doesn't exist.

### 4. Implement :remove-counter cost type

```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :remove-counter [db object-id cost]
  ;; Can pay remove-counter if:
  ;; 1. Object exists
  ;; 2. Object has sufficient counters of required type(s)
  (when-let [obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (let [required (:remove-counter cost)
          current (or (d/q '[:find ?c .
                             :in $ ?e
                             :where [?e :object/counters ?c]]
                           db obj-eid)
                      {})]
      (every? (fn [[counter-type amount]]
                (>= (get current counter-type 0) amount))
              required))))


(defmethod pay-cost :remove-counter [db object-id cost]
  ;; Guard: check can pay first
  (when (can-pay? db object-id cost)
    (let [obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)
          required (:remove-counter cost)
          current (or (d/q '[:find ?c .
                             :in $ ?e
                             :where [?e :object/counters ?c]]
                           db obj-eid)
                      {})
          new-counters (merge-with - current required)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/counters new-counters]]))))
```

### 5. Add helper query if needed

Check if q/get-object-eid exists. If not, add to db/queries.cljs:

```clojure
(defn get-object-eid
  "Get the entity ID for an object by its UUID."
  [db object-id]
  (d/q '[:find ?e .
         :in $ ?oid
         :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
       db object-id))
```

### 6. VERIFY
- Run: make validate
- Confirm all 12 new tests pass
- Confirm existing tests still pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] engine/costs.cljs exists at src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs
- [ ] pay-cost multimethod dispatches on (first (keys cost))
- [ ] can-pay? multimethod dispatches on (first (keys cost))
- [ ] :tap cost: taps untapped permanent, returns nil if already tapped
- [ ] :remove-counter cost: removes counters, returns nil if insufficient
- [ ] 12 tests pass in costs_test.cljs (6 tap + 6 counter)
- [ ] All 161+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO cond-> blocks for cost types (use multimethod dispatch)
- ❌ NO checking specific card IDs (data over code)
- ❌ NO mutating permanent state outside transaction
- ❌ NO paying cost when can-pay? returns false
- ❌ NO crashing on nil object-id (return nil/false gracefully)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Nil Object ID**:
- Both pay-cost and can-pay? must handle nil object-id
- Return nil/false gracefully, no exceptions
- Test: test-pay-tap-invalid-object-id

**Nil Counters Map**:
- Permanent may have nil :object/counters (never had counters)
- Treat as empty map {} for comparison
- Test: test-pay-remove-counter-no-counters-at-all

**Counter Goes to Zero**:
- Removing last counter should leave {:mining 0}, not delete key
- This is important for state-based actions that check counter count
- Test: test-pay-remove-counter-exact-amount

**Default Multimethod**:
- Unknown cost types should return nil/false (graceful degradation)
- Matches pattern in effects.cljs

## Reference Implementation
- Study engine/effects.cljs for multimethod pattern
- Study test helpers in effects_test.cljs for add-permanent

title: Implement land-entered trigger and City of Traitors
design:
## Goal
Implement :land-entered event type and City of Traitors card which produces {C}{C} and sacrifices itself when another land enters the battlefield.

## Context
Completed:
- fizzle-h4l: Event system migration done, old scanning code removed
- fizzle-pk2: Tutor infrastructure built, Merchant Scroll working

This is Phase 3 of epic fizzle-wjl. Uses the event-based trigger system established in Phase 1.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add land-entered-event helper
- File: src/main/fizzle/engine/events.cljs
- Add after zone-change-event (line ~54)
- Pattern similar to permanent-tapped-event:
  \`\`\`clojure
  (defn land-entered-event
    \"Create a land-entered event.
     Arguments:
       object-id - ID of the land that entered
       controller-id - ID of the land's controller
     Returns:
       Event map with :event/type :land-entered\"
    [object-id controller-id]
    {:event/type :land-entered
     :event/object-id object-id
     :event/controller controller-id})
  \`\`\`

### Step 2: Dispatch land-entered event in play-land
- File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs
- Location: play-land function around line 451-462
- After ETB effects execute, dispatch land-entered event:
  \`\`\`clojure
  ;; After ETB effects processing, before returning:
  (dispatch/dispatch-event db-after-etb 
    (game-events/land-entered-event object-id player-id))
  \`\`\`

### Step 3: Extend matches-filter? for :exclude-self
- File: src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs
- Location: matches-filter? function (lines 11-31)
- Add :exclude-self handling in the cond:
  \`\`\`clojure
  (defn matches-filter?
    [trigger event]
    (let [f (:trigger/filter trigger)]
      (if (and f (seq f))
        (every? (fn [[k v]]
                  (cond
                    ;; Existing: :self matches trigger's source
                    (= v :self) (= (get event k) (:trigger/source trigger))
                    ;; NEW: :exclude-self means trigger source must NOT match event object
                    (= k :exclude-self) (not= (:event/object-id event) (:trigger/source trigger))
                    :else (= (get event k) v)))
                f)
        true)))
  \`\`\`

### Step 4: Implement City of Traitors card definition
- File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- Add after city-of-brass definition (around line 77)
- Add to all-cards vector at end
  \`\`\`clojure
  (def city-of-traitors
    {:card/id :city-of-traitors
     :card/name "City of Traitors"
     :card/cmc 0
     :card/mana-cost {}
     :card/colors #{}
     :card/types #{:land}
     :card/text \"When you play another land, sacrifice City of Traitors. {T}: Add {C}{C}.\"
     ;; Trigger: When ANOTHER land enters (not self)
     :card/triggers [{:trigger/type :land-entered
                      :trigger/description \"sacrifice City of Traitors\"
                      :trigger/filter {:exclude-self true}
                      :trigger/effects [{:effect/type :sacrifice
                                         :effect/target :self}]}]
     ;; Mana ability: T: Add {C}{C}
     :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                       :ability/cost {:tap true}
                       :ability/produces {:colorless 2}}]})
  \`\`\`

### Step 5: Update register-card-triggers! for land-entered
- File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs
- Location: trigger-type->event-type function (lines 333-340)
- Add mapping for :land-entered:
  \`\`\`clojure
  (defn- trigger-type->event-type
    [trigger-type]
    (case trigger-type
      :becomes-tapped :permanent-tapped
      :land-entered :land-entered  ;; NEW
      trigger-type))
  \`\`\`

### Step 6: Write tests
- File: src/test/fizzle/cards/city_of_traitors_test.cljs (NEW)
- Tests to implement (see Success Criteria)

### Step 7: Run validation
- make validate passes
- All tests green

## Success Criteria
- [ ] land-entered-event helper exists in events.cljs
- [ ] play-land dispatches :land-entered event after land ETBs
- [ ] matches-filter? supports :exclude-self filter key
- [ ] City of Traitors produces {:colorless 2} via mana ability
- [ ] City of Traitors trigger registered on ETB with :exclude-self filter
- [ ] City of Traitors sacrifices when ANOTHER land enters battlefield
- [ ] City of Traitors does NOT sacrifice when it enters (exclude-self works)
- [ ] Test: test-cot-produces-colorless-mana - verifies {C}{C} production
- [ ] Test: test-cot-does-not-sacrifice-on-self-entry - verifies exclude-self
- [ ] Test: test-cot-sacrifices-when-another-land-enters - core trigger behavior
- [ ] Test: test-two-cots-both-sacrifice-when-land-enters - edge case
- [ ] Test: test-cot-trigger-uses-stack - trigger goes on stack, can respond
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: City of Traitors as First Land**
- When CoT enters as first land, trigger should NOT fire (no other lands entered)
- Verify :exclude-self filter is working correctly
- Test explicitly: play CoT, verify no trigger on stack

**Edge Case: Two City of Traitors on Battlefield**
- When a third land enters, BOTH CoT triggers should fire
- Both should sacrifice (or both triggers on stack)
- Test explicitly: play CoT #1, play CoT #2 (CoT #1 sacrifices), play Island (remaining CoT sacrifices)

**Edge Case: City of Traitors Sacrificed in Response**
- If player sacrifices CoT to another effect (e.g., mana ability that sacs)
- Trigger already on stack should still try to resolve
- But :self target won't find permanent - should handle gracefully
- Reference: cob-event-trigger-test.cljs:273-292 shows similar pattern

**Test Isolation**
- Use reset-registry fixture pattern from cob_event_trigger_test.cljs
- Clear registry before and after each test
- Re-register turn-based actions after clearing

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow City of Brass pattern in cob_event_trigger_test.cljs
- Use same test helpers (create-test-db, add-land-to-hand, add-land-to-battlefield)
- Similar register function for test setup

## Anti-Patterns (from epic)
- NO scanning-based triggers (use event system)
- NO check-trigger multimethod (use trigger-registry + dispatch-event)
- NO leaving dead code (clean up if anything obsolete)
close_reason: Implemented land-entered event type and City of Traitors card. All tests pass, make validate passes.


title: Gemstone Mine counter tracking and sacrifice
design:
## Goal
Gemstone Mine enters with mining counters and sacrifices when depleted.

## Context
Epic success criterion: 'Gemstone Mine tracks counters and sacrifices at 0'
Card text: 'Gemstone Mine enters with three mining counters. T, Remove a mining counter: Add one mana of any color. If there are no mining counters on Gemstone Mine, sacrifice it.'
Schema has :object/counters field but no counter system implemented.

## Investigation Needed
- Review :object/counters schema field
- Check Gemstone Mine card definition
- Review how permanents enter battlefield (for counter initialization)
- Check if sacrifice mechanic exists

## Implementation Steps
1. RED: Write test that Gemstone Mine enters battlefield with 3 counters
2. GREEN: Add counter initialization on ETB
3. RED: Write test that tapping removes a counter and adds mana
4. GREEN: Integrate counter removal with tap ability
5. RED: Write test that Gemstone Mine sacrifices at 0 counters
6. GREEN: Implement sacrifice trigger when counters depleted
7. VERIFY: All tests pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Gemstone Mine enters with 3 mining counters
- [ ] Each tap removes 1 counter and adds mana
- [ ] Counters tracked on the permanent
- [ ] Land sacrificed when counters reach 0
- [ ] Can tap 3 times total before sacrifice
