title: Phase 1.5: Full Game Loop
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Full MTG turn structure: Untap → Upkeep → Draw → Main 1 → Combat → Main 2 → End → Cleanup
- Player library with 60-card shuffled deck at game start
- Draw effect moves top card of library to hand
- Player draws 7 cards as opening hand
- Land cards (City of Brass, Gemstone Mine) can be played from hand to battlefield
- Land plays limited to 1 per turn, tracked via :player/land-plays-left
- Lands tap to produce mana (click to tap, pick color)
- City of Brass deals 1 damage to controller when tapped
- Gemstone Mine uses counters, sacrifices when depleted
- Both players have life totals starting at 20
- :lose-life and :gain-life effects modify life totals
- Loss condition: life ≤ 0
- Loss condition: forced to draw from empty library
- Win condition: opponent life ≤ 0 OR opponent must draw from empty library
- Test deck: 12x each of City of Brass, Gemstone Mine, Dark Ritual, Cabal Ritual, Brain Freeze

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Player can take multiple turns (untap, draw, play lands, cast spells, end turn)
- [ ] Player starts with 7-card hand drawn from shuffled 60-card library
- [ ] Lands can be played (1 per turn) and tapped for mana
- [ ] City of Brass deals 1 damage when tapped for mana
- [ ] Gemstone Mine tracks counters and sacrifices at 0
- [ ] Life totals displayed and updated correctly
- [ ] Game ends when life ≤ 0 or library empty on draw
- [ ] Can goldfish a storm combo across multiple turns
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO hardcoded starting hands (must draw from shuffled library)
- ❌ NO skipping untap step (lands must untap each turn)
- ❌ NO auto-tapping lands (player clicks to tap, explicit mana decisions)
- ❌ NO infinite mana pools (mana empties at phase transitions per MTG rules)
- ❌ NO fetch lands in Phase 1.5 (deferred - adds library search complexity)
- ❌ NO combat phase implementation beyond placeholder (storm doesn't attack)
- ❌ NO mocking game state in integration tests (test real turn flow)

## Approach

Extend the existing Phase 1 engine with turn structure and land permanents. The core architecture (Datascript, re-frame events, effect multimethods) remains unchanged. Add new effect types (:draw, :lose-life, :gain-life, :tap, :untap), phase advancement events, and land card definitions. UI gets a 'Next Phase' button and life total display.

## Architecture

**New Effects (engine/effects.cljs):**
- :draw - move top N cards from library to hand
- :lose-life - reduce player life by N
- :gain-life - increase player life by N

**New Events (events/game.cljs):**
- ::advance-phase - move to next phase/step
- ::start-turn - untap, reset land plays, reset storm count
- ::play-land - move land from hand to battlefield
- ::activate-mana-ability - tap land, add mana, apply costs

**New Cards (cards/iggy_pop.cljs):**
- City of Brass - land with damaging mana ability
- Gemstone Mine - land with counter-based mana ability

**New Queries (db/queries.cljs):**
- get-life-total
- get-library-size
- get-battlefield (permanents in play)

**New Subscriptions (subs/game.cljs):**
- ::life-total
- ::library-size
- ::current-phase
- ::battlefield

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Phase 1 demonstrates a single storm turn with infinite mana. Cannot practice actual games: no drawing cards, no land drops, no life tracking, no turn progression. Storm practice requires managing resources across multiple turns.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- db/schema.cljs:48-49 - :player/life and :player/land-plays-left already in schema
- db/schema.cljs:54-57 - :game/turn, :game/phase, :game/step already defined
- engine/effects.cljs:38-59 - Mill effect pattern to follow for draw
- events/game.cljs:64-68 - Library creation pattern exists for opponent

**External:**
- MTG Comprehensive Rules 500-514 - Turn structure reference
- Existing Phase 1 patterns sufficient, no new libraries needed

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Extend existing architecture ✓

**What it is:** Add new effect types, events, and card definitions following established patterns. No architectural changes.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed effects.cljs - multimethod pattern extensible
- Reviewed events/game.cljs - event pattern clear
- Schema already has life/phase/land-plays fields

**Pros:**
- Consistent with Phase 1 patterns
- Minimal new concepts
- Tests follow existing patterns

**Cons:**
- None significant

**Chosen because:** Architecture already supports this; just needs content.

#### 2. Phase-based state machine ❌

**What it is:** Formal state machine for phase transitions with explicit allowed actions per phase.

**Why we looked at this:** Could prevent illegal actions (cast during untap step).

**Investigation:**
- Evaluated complexity vs benefit
- Storm rarely has phase-specific timing issues

**Pros:**
- Rigorous phase enforcement

**Cons:**
- Over-engineering for goldfish practice
- Adds complexity without clear benefit

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Goldfish practice doesn't need strict phase enforcement. Player is trusted to follow rules.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Adding multiplayer or competitive play.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Full turn structure (all phases, simplified combat)
- Land permanents with mana abilities
- Life totals and loss conditions
- Test deck with lands and storm cards

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Fetch lands (Phase 2 - needs library search UI)
- Combat damage (future - storm doesn't attack)
- Upkeep/end step triggers (future - no cards need them yet)
- Mulligan decisions (Phase 4 - hand sculpting)

### Open Questions
- Mana pool clearing: at phase change or end of step? (default: end of each phase per modern rules)
- Cleanup discard: enforce 7-card limit or trust player? (default: trust player for now)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Turn structure depth? | Full MTG structure | Implement all phases, can simplify combat |
| Library initialization? | Hardcoded test deck with lands | 60-card deck with City/Gemstone/rituals/Brain Freeze |
| Win/loss conditions? | Full life + decking | Track both players' life, check on each action |
| Mana ability activation? | Click to tap | Manual tapping, no auto-tap |
| Fetch land complexity? | Skip for now | Only City of Brass and Gemstone Mine |
| Test deck contents? | Minimal variety | 12x each of 5 card types |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Turn Structure Implementation
**Question explored:** How to implement phase advancement?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/game.cljs - existing event patterns
- db/schema.cljs:54-57 - phase/step fields exist

**Findings:**
- Schema already has :game/phase and :game/step
- Events update :game/db atomically
- Can use simple phase list and advance index

**Conclusion:** Add ::advance-phase event, cycle through phase list

#### Land Permanents
**Question explored:** How to handle lands differently from spells?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/zones.cljs - zone transition logic
- engine/rules.cljs - casting rules

**Findings:**
- Zones already support :battlefield
- Casting uses stack; lands bypass stack
- Need separate ::play-land event

**Conclusion:** Lands go hand → battlefield directly (not via stack)

### Dead-End Paths

#### Auto-Tap Mana System
**Why explored:** Could simplify UI
**Investigation:**
- Reviewed MTGO-style auto-tap complexity
- Considered edge cases (City of Brass damage, Gemstone counters)

**Why abandoned:** User explicitly chose manual tap. Auto-tap hides decisions important for storm practice (life management from City of Brass).

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Gemstone Mine counter tracking?' → Use :object/counters attribute (add to schema if needed)
- 'Mana pool clearing timing?' → Default to phase transitions, matches modern rules
close_reason: Phase 1.5 complete: Turn structure, library/hand, land mechanics, life tracking, loss conditions all working. All tests passing (143 tests). Gemstone Mine counter logic deferred to fizzle-dg4 (Data-Driven Ability System) as that epic specifically addresses removing hardcoded card logic.

title: Task 2: Implement Lotus Petal sacrifice-for-mana
design:
## Goal
Add Lotus Petal card that sacrifices itself to produce 1 mana of any color.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1 (Island, Swamp). Lotus Petal requires :sacrifice-self cost type.
Existing costs.cljs has :tap and :remove-counter. Need to add :sacrifice-self.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing cost patterns
- src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs - Review can-pay? and pay-costs functions
- Pattern: multimethod dispatch on cost type key
- Need to add :sacrifice-self cost type that moves permanent to graveyard

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Add tests to: src/test/fizzle/cards/lotus_petal_test.cljs (new file)

Test cases (8 tests):
```clojure
;; test_lotus_petal_sacrifice_for_black_mana
;; Setup: Lotus Petal on battlefield, untapped
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :black
;; Verify: mana pool has {:black 1}, Lotus Petal zone is :graveyard

;; test_lotus_petal_sacrifice_for_blue_mana
;; Same as above but :blue - verify any-color works

;; test_lotus_petal_sacrifice_for_white_mana
;; Same pattern - :white

;; test_lotus_petal_sacrifice_for_red_mana
;; Same pattern - :red

;; test_lotus_petal_sacrifice_for_green_mana
;; Same pattern - :green

;; test_lotus_petal_cannot_activate_from_graveyard
;; Setup: Lotus Petal in graveyard (simulating after sacrifice)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability with :black
;; Verify: returns unchanged db, mana pool unchanged

;; test_lotus_petal_in_hand_cannot_activate
;; Setup: Lotus Petal in hand (not battlefield)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability with :black
;; Verify: returns unchanged db, mana pool unchanged

;; test_lotus_petal_is_artifact_type
;; Verify card definition has :card/types #{:artifact}
```

### 3. Add :sacrifice-self cost type
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

Add can-pay? multimethod for :sacrifice-self:
```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :sacrifice-self [db object-id _cost]
  ;; Can sacrifice-self if:
  ;; 1. Object exists
  ;; 2. Object is on battlefield
  (if-let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (= :battlefield (d/q '[:find ?z .
                           :in $ ?e
                           :where [?e :object/zone ?z]]
                         db obj-eid))
    false))
```

Add pay-cost multimethod for :sacrifice-self:
```clojure
(defmethod pay-cost :sacrifice-self [db object-id cost]
  ;; Guard: check can pay first
  (when (can-pay? db object-id cost)
    (let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/zone :graveyard]]))))
```

### 4. Add Lotus Petal card definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
;; Lotus Petal - Zero-cost artifact mana acceleration
;; {T}, Sacrifice Lotus Petal: Add one mana of any color.
(def lotus-petal
  {:card/id :lotus-petal
   :card/name "Lotus Petal"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:artifact}
   :card/text "{T}, Sacrifice Lotus Petal: Add one mana of any color."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true
                                    :sacrifice-self true}
                     :ability/produces {:any 1}}]})
```

### 5. Update all-cards vector
Add lotus-petal to all-cards:
```clojure
(def all-cards
  [dark-ritual brain-freeze cabal-ritual city-of-brass gemstone-mine island swamp lotus-petal])
```

### 6. Run validation
- make test (expect 8 new tests pass)
- make validate (lint + fmt + tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] can-pay? :sacrifice-self returns true only for battlefield objects
- [ ] pay-cost :sacrifice-self moves object to :graveyard zone
- [ ] Lotus Petal definition exists with :card/types #{:artifact}
- [ ] Lotus Petal mana ability has both :tap and :sacrifice-self costs
- [ ] activate-mana-ability works for all 5 colors (white, blue, black, red, green)
- [ ] Cannot activate from graveyard (already sacrificed)
- [ ] Cannot activate from hand (not on battlefield)
- [ ] 8 tests in lotus_petal_test.cljs all pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in costs.cljs (sacrifice-self is generic for all cards)
- ❌ NO skipping TDD (write failing tests first)
- ❌ NO checking card ID in can-pay?/pay-cost (must be generic)
- ❌ NO implementing sacrifice without zone check (must be on battlefield)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Cost Payment Order**
- Both :tap and :sacrifice-self costs must be paid
- activate-mana-ability should iterate through ability costs and pay each
- Verify existing pay-costs handles multiple cost types

**Zone Change for Sacrifice**
- Sacrifice = move to graveyard (not exile)
- Use :object/zone :graveyard (same as spell resolution)
- Verify zone update triggers any on-death effects (future concern)

**Edge Case: Double Activation**
- After sacrifice, object is in graveyard
- can-pay? :sacrifice-self returns false (zone check fails)
- Second activation returns unchanged db (safe, no error)

**Edge Case: Tap Cost Already Paid**
- If somehow object is tapped before sacrifice, tap cost fails
- can-pay? :tap returns false for tapped objects (existing behavior)
- Unlikely scenario but correctly handled

**Reference Implementation**
- Study existing :remove-counter cost pattern in costs.cljs
- Same structure: can-pay? checks, pay-cost executes
- :sacrifice-self is simpler (no counter tracking, just zone change)
close_reason: Implemented :sacrifice-self cost type and Lotus Petal card. All 8 tests passing.

title: Create engine/abilities.cljs with generic mana ability activation
design:
## Goal
Implement engine/abilities.cljs with generic ability activation that reads card data and delegates to costs.cljs for payment.

## Context
Completed fizzle-31d: engine/costs.cljs now handles :tap and :remove-counter costs via multimethod dispatch. Now need the abilities layer that reads :card/abilities from card data and uses costs.cljs to pay costs.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Implementation Steps

### 1. RED: Write tests for abilities.cljs
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/abilities_test.cljs (new file)

Follow pattern from costs_test.cljs - use add-permanent helper.

**can-activate? tests (6 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-can-activate-tap-ability
  (testing "can-activate? returns true for untapped permanent with :tap cost"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent on battlefield
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true}}
    ;; Assert: (abilities/can-activate? db object-id ability) => true
    ;; Catches: Missing delegation to costs/can-pay?
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-already-tapped
  (testing "can-activate? returns false for tapped permanent with :tap cost"
    ;; Setup: tapped permanent on battlefield
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true}}
    ;; Assert: (abilities/can-activate? db object-id ability) => false
    ;; Catches: Not checking cost payability
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-counter-ability
  (testing "can-activate? returns true when permanent has sufficient counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}
    ;; Assert: true
    ;; Catches: Not delegating counter check to costs.cljs
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-counter-ability-insufficient
  (testing "can-activate? returns false when insufficient counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 0}
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}
    ;; Assert: false
    ;; Catches: Not checking counter count
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-nil-cost
  (testing "can-activate? returns true for ability with nil cost (free activation)"
    ;; Setup: any permanent
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost nil}  ;; or ability without :ability/cost key
    ;; Assert: true
    ;; Catches: NPE on nil cost map, treating nil as unpayable
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-invalid-object-id
  (testing "can-activate? returns false for non-existent permanent"
    ;; Setup: db with no matching object
    ;; Action: (abilities/can-activate? db (random-uuid) ability)
    ;; Assert: false (not crash)
    ;; Catches: NPE on object lookup
    ))
```

**pay-all-costs tests (4 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-pay-all-costs-single
  (testing "pay-all-costs with single :tap cost taps permanent"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent
    ;; Action: (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id {:tap true})
    ;; Assert: returns db with permanent tapped
    ;; Catches: Not calling costs/pay-cost
    ))

(deftest test-pay-all-costs-multiple
  (testing "pay-all-costs with :tap and :remove-counter pays both"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Action: (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: permanent tapped AND counters at {:mining 2}
    ;; Catches: Only paying first cost
    ))

(deftest test-pay-all-costs-fails-partial
  (testing "pay-all-costs returns nil if any cost fails, no partial payment"
    ;; Setup: tapped permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Action: (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: returns nil, counters still {:mining 3} (no change)
    ;; Catches: Paying counter cost even when tap fails
    ))

(deftest test-pay-all-costs-empty
  (testing "pay-all-costs with empty/nil costs returns db unchanged"
    ;; Setup: any permanent
    ;; Action: (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id {})
    ;; Assert: returns original db unchanged
    ;; Catches: NPE on empty map, returning nil for free costs
    ))
```

**activate-ability tests (4 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-activate-tap-ability
  (testing "activate-ability pays :tap cost and applies mana effect"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true} :ability/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 1}}]}
    ;; Action: (abilities/activate-ability db object-id ability :player-1)
    ;; Assert: permanent tapped AND player has 1 black mana
    ;; Catches: Not applying effects after paying costs
    ))

(deftest test-activate-tap-already-tapped
  (testing "activate-ability returns nil when cost cannot be paid"
    ;; Setup: tapped permanent
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true} :ability/effects [...]}
    ;; Action: (abilities/activate-ability db object-id ability :player-1)
    ;; Assert: nil, mana pool unchanged
    ;; Catches: Applying effects even when costs fail
    ))

(deftest test-activate-counter-ability
  (testing "activate-ability removes counter and applies mana effect"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}} :ability/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:any 1}}]}
    ;; Action: (abilities/activate-ability db object-id ability :player-1)
    ;; Assert: counters at {:mining 2}, mana in pool
    ;; Catches: Counter not removed, effect not applied
    ))

(deftest test-activate-ability-empty-effects
  (testing "activate-ability with no effects still pays costs (for triggered abilities later)"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true} :ability/effects []}
    ;; Action: (abilities/activate-ability db object-id ability :player-1)
    ;; Assert: returns db with permanent tapped (not nil)
    ;; Catches: Returning nil when effects list empty
    ))
```

### 2. GREEN: Implement engine/abilities.cljs

Create new file: src/main/fizzle/engine/abilities.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.abilities
  "Generic ability activation for Fizzle.

   Provides can-activate?, pay-all-costs, and activate-ability functions
   that work with any ability defined as data."
  (:require
    [fizzle.engine.costs :as costs]
    [fizzle.engine.effects :as effects]))
```

### 3. Implement can-activate?

```clojure
(defn can-activate?
  "Check if an ability can be activated.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent
     ability - Map with :ability/cost and optionally :ability/effects
   
   Returns:
     Boolean - true if all costs can be paid (or no costs)."
  [db object-id ability]
  (let [cost (:ability/cost ability)]
    (if (or (nil? cost) (empty? cost))
      true  ;; Free activation
      (every? (fn [[cost-type cost-value]]
                (costs/can-pay? db object-id {cost-type cost-value}))
              cost))))
```

### 4. Implement pay-all-costs

```clojure
(defn pay-all-costs
  "Pay all costs for an ability.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent
     costs - Map of costs {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}}
   
   Returns:
     New db with all costs paid, or nil if any cost cannot be paid.
     Empty/nil costs return db unchanged."
  [db object-id costs]
  (if (or (nil? costs) (empty? costs))
    db  ;; No costs to pay
    (reduce (fn [db' [cost-type cost-value]]
              (if-let [new-db (costs/pay-cost db' object-id {cost-type cost-value})]
                new-db
                (reduced nil)))
            db
            costs)))
```

### 5. Implement activate-ability

```clojure
(defn activate-ability
  "Activate an ability on a permanent.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent
     ability - Map with :ability/cost and :ability/effects
     player-id - The player activating
   
   Returns:
     New db with costs paid and effects applied, or nil if cannot activate."
  [db object-id ability player-id]
  (when (can-activate? db object-id ability)
    (let [cost (:ability/cost ability)
          db-after-costs (pay-all-costs db object-id cost)]
      (when db-after-costs
        ;; Apply effects (empty list is fine)
        (reduce (fn [db' effect]
                  (effects/execute-effect db' player-id effect))
                db-after-costs
                (:ability/effects ability []))))))
```

### 6. VERIFY
- Run: make validate
- Confirm all 14 new tests pass
- Confirm all 175+ existing tests still pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] engine/abilities.cljs exists at src/main/fizzle/engine/abilities.cljs
- [ ] can-activate? with :tap cost on untapped permanent returns true
- [ ] can-activate? with :tap cost on tapped permanent returns false
- [ ] can-activate? with nil/empty cost returns true (free activation)
- [ ] can-activate? with invalid object-id returns false (not crash)
- [ ] pay-all-costs with single cost pays it and returns db
- [ ] pay-all-costs with multiple costs pays all or returns nil
- [ ] pay-all-costs with nil/empty costs returns db unchanged
- [ ] activate-ability pays costs, applies effects, returns db
- [ ] activate-ability with unpayable cost returns nil, no side effects
- [ ] activate-ability with empty effects list still works (returns db)
- [ ] 14 tests pass in abilities_test.cljs
- [ ] All 175+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO card ID checks (data over code)
- ❌ NO duplicating cost logic (delegate to costs.cljs)
- ❌ NO hardcoded ability types
- ❌ NO partial cost payment (all-or-nothing)
- ❌ NO crashing on nil/empty costs or effects (handle gracefully)
- ❌ NO applying effects if costs cannot be paid

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Nil/Empty Cost Map**:
- Ability with no cost (nil :ability/cost or {}) should be activatable
- pay-all-costs with {} or nil should return db unchanged
- Test: test-can-activate-nil-cost, test-pay-all-costs-empty

**Multiple Costs - All-or-Nothing**:
- If :tap paid but :remove-counter fails, entire activation fails
- reduce returns nil on first failure via (reduced nil)
- Original db is never modified (immutable)
- Test: test-pay-all-costs-fails-partial

**Empty Effects List**:
- Ability with :ability/effects [] or nil is valid
- Should still pay costs and return db (not nil)
- Test: test-activate-ability-empty-effects

**Invalid Object ID**:
- can-activate? with non-existent object-id should return false
- Should not crash or throw exception
- Test: test-can-activate-invalid-object-id

**Effect Integration**:
- Effects use existing effects/execute-effect multimethod
- Mana abilities produce {:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 1}}
- Unknown effect types are no-op (graceful degradation)

**NOTE: Trigger firing is OUT OF SCOPE**:
- :becomes-tapped triggers will be handled by the trigger system
- This task focuses on ability activation only
- Triggers will be added in a separate task when we implement trigger firing

## Reference Implementation
- Study engine/costs.cljs for cost payment patterns
- Study engine/effects.cljs for effect execution pattern
- Study test helpers in costs_test.cljs for add-permanent helper

title: Task 1: Datascript Schema and Initial Game State
design:
## Goal

Create the Datascript schema and initial game state with Dark Ritual in hand, providing the foundation for all engine functions.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Project Setup (if needed)

Configure shadow-cljs.edn for test build:
```clojure
;; shadow-cljs.edn - add test build if not present
{:source-paths ["src/main" "src/test"]
 :dependencies [[datascript "1.6.5"]
                [reagent "1.2.0"]]
 :builds
 {:app {...}
  :test {:target :node-test
         :output-to "out/test.js"
         :ns-regexp ".*-test$"
         :autorun true}}}
```

### 2. Study existing patterns

Reference design doc schema (fizzle-design.md lines 231-274):
- Card definitions with :card/id, :card/name, :card/effects
- Game objects with :object/id, :object/zone, :object/card
- Players with :player/id, :player/mana-pool, :player/storm-count
- Game state with :game/phase, :game/active-player

### 3. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/db/schema_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.db.schema-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]))

;; Schema validity tests
(deftest schema-creates-valid-connection-test
  (testing "schema creates connection that accepts transactions"
    (let [conn (d/create-conn schema)]
      (is (some? conn))
      ;; Actually verify we can transact - not just that conn exists
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :test-player
                         :player/life 20}])
      (is (= 20 (-> @conn
                    (d/q '[:find ?life .
                           :where [?e :player/id :test-player]
                                  [?e :player/life ?life]])))))))

;; Initial state tests
(deftest initial-state-has-player-with-empty-mana-pool-test
  (testing "player mana pool starts with all zeros"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (= {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
             (q/get-mana-pool db :player-1))))))

(deftest initial-state-has-dark-ritual-in-hand-test
  (testing "Dark Ritual is in player's hand"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)]
      (is (= 1 (count hand)))
      (is (= "Dark Ritual" (:card/name (first hand)))))))

(deftest initial-state-has-zero-storm-count-test
  (testing "storm count starts at 0"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (= 0 (q/get-storm-count db :player-1))))))

;; Query edge case tests
(deftest get-mana-pool-nonexistent-player-test
  (testing "get-mana-pool returns nil for nonexistent player"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (nil? (q/get-mana-pool db :nonexistent-player))))))

(deftest get-hand-empty-returns-empty-vector-test
  (testing "get-hand returns empty vector when no cards in hand"
    (let [conn (d/create-conn schema)]
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :empty-player
                         :player/life 20
                         :player/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 
                                            :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
                         :player/storm-count 0}])
      (is (= [] (q/get-hand @conn :empty-player))))))

(deftest get-card-by-object-id-test
  (testing "get-card retrieves card definition from game object"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          obj-id (:object/id (first hand))]
      (is (= "Dark Ritual" (:card/name (q/get-card db obj-id)))))))
```

### 4. Implementation checklist

- [ ] shadow-cljs.edn - Add :test build and datascript dependency
  - Target: :node-test for fast CLI testing
  - Dependencies: datascript 1.6.5
  
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs - Datascript schema
  ```clojure
  (def schema
    {;; Card definitions (templates)
     :card/id         {:db/unique :db.unique/identity}
     :card/name       {:db/index true}
     :card/cmc        {}
     :card/mana-cost  {}  ; {:black 1} for B cost
     :card/colors     {:db/cardinality :db.cardinality/many}
     :card/types      {:db/cardinality :db.cardinality/many}
     :card/text       {}
     :card/effects    {}  ; EDN vector of effect maps
     :card/keywords   {:db/cardinality :db.cardinality/many}
     
     ;; Game objects (instances of cards in a game)
     :object/id       {:db/unique :db.unique/identity}
     :object/card     {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}
     :object/zone     {}  ; :hand, :stack, :graveyard, :battlefield, :library, :exile
     :object/owner    {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}
     :object/controller {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}
     :object/tapped   {}
     
     ;; Players
     :player/id       {:db/unique :db.unique/identity}
     :player/name     {}
     :player/life     {}
     :player/mana-pool {}  ; {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
     :player/storm-count {}
     
     ;; Game state (singleton)
     :game/id         {:db/unique :db.unique/identity}
     :game/turn       {}
     :game/phase      {}  ; :main1, :main2, etc.
     :game/active-player {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}})
  ```

- [ ] src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Dark Ritual definition
  ```clojure
  (def dark-ritual
    {:card/id :dark-ritual
     :card/name "Dark Ritual"
     :card/cmc 1
     :card/mana-cost {:black 1}
     :card/colors #{:black}
     :card/types #{:instant}
     :card/text "Add {B}{B}{B}."
     :card/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana
                     :effect/mana {:black 3}}]})
  ```

- [ ] src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs - Query helpers
  ```clojure
  (defn get-mana-pool [db player-id]
    (d/q '[:find ?pool .
           :in $ ?pid
           :where [?e :player/id ?pid]
                  [?e :player/mana-pool ?pool]]
         db player-id))
  
  (defn get-hand [db player-id]
    (let [player-eid (d/q '[:find ?e .
                           :in $ ?pid
                           :where [?e :player/id ?pid]]
                         db player-id)]
      (when player-eid
        (->> (d/q '[:find [(pull ?obj [* {:object/card [*]}]) ...]
                   :in $ ?owner
                   :where [?obj :object/owner ?owner]
                          [?obj :object/zone :hand]]
                 db player-eid)
             (vec)))))
  
  (defn get-storm-count [db player-id]
    (d/q '[:find ?count .
           :in $ ?pid
           :where [?e :player/id ?pid]
                  [?e :player/storm-count ?count]]
         db player-id))
  
  (defn get-card [db object-id]
    (d/q '[:find (pull ?card [*]) .
           :in $ ?oid
           :where [?obj :object/id ?oid]
                  [?obj :object/card ?card]]
         db object-id))
  ```

- [ ] src/main/fizzle/db/init.cljs - Initial state creation
  ```clojure
  (defn init-game-state []
    (let [conn (d/create-conn schema)]
      ;; Transact card definition
      (d/transact! conn [dark-ritual])
      
      ;; Transact player
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :player-1
                         :player/name "Player"
                         :player/life 20
                         :player/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 
                                            :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
                         :player/storm-count 0}])
      
      ;; Get entity IDs for refs
      (let [player-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :player/id :player-1]] @conn)
            card-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :card/id :dark-ritual]] @conn)]
        
        ;; Transact game object (Dark Ritual in hand)
        (d/transact! conn [{:object/id (random-uuid)
                           :object/card card-eid
                           :object/zone :hand
                           :object/owner player-eid
                           :object/controller player-eid}])
        
        ;; Transact game state
        (d/transact! conn [{:game/id :game-1
                           :game/turn 1
                           :game/phase :main1
                           :game/active-player player-eid}]))
      
      ;; Return db value (immutable snapshot)
      @conn))
  ```

- [ ] test/fizzle/db/schema_test.cljs - Unit tests (see above)

### 5. Verification

```bash
# Run tests
npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js

# Or from REPL:
npx shadow-cljs cljs-repl app
(require '[fizzle.db.init :as init])
(def db (init/init-game-state))
(require '[fizzle.db.queries :as q])
(q/get-hand db :player-1)  ;; => [{:card/name "Dark Ritual" ...}]
(q/get-mana-pool db :player-1)  ;; => {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 ...}
(q/get-storm-count db :player-1)  ;; => 0
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `npx shadow-cljs compile test` completes without errors
- [ ] `node out/test.js` shows all 7 tests passing
- [ ] init-game-state returns Datascript db value (verified: `(d/db? db)` is true)
- [ ] `(q/get-hand db :player-1)` returns vector with one Dark Ritual object
- [ ] `(q/get-mana-pool db :player-1)` returns `{:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}`
- [ ] `(q/get-storm-count db :player-1)` returns `0`
- [ ] `(q/get-card db obj-id)` returns Dark Ritual card definition
- [ ] `(q/get-mana-pool db :nonexistent)` returns `nil` (edge case)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO hardcoded entity IDs (use queries to find entities by :db/unique attributes)
- ❌ NO mutable state outside Datascript conn (all state in one place)
- ❌ NO inline card definitions in init.cljs (cards live in cards/ namespace)
- ❌ NO ignoring nil returns from queries (handle missing entities gracefully)
- ❌ NO skipping tests for edge cases (nonexistent players, empty hands)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Nonexistent Player**
- Queries must handle player-id that doesn't exist
- Return nil or empty collection, don't throw
- Test: `get-mana-pool` with `:nonexistent-player` returns nil

**Edge Case: Empty Hand**
- `get-hand` should return empty vector `[]`, not nil
- Ensures consistent type for consumers
- Test: Player with no objects in hand returns `[]`

**Edge Case: Object Without Card Reference**
- Shouldn't happen in normal flow, but `get-card` should handle
- Return nil if object has no :object/card ref
- Defensive programming for corrupted state

**Schema Evolution**
- Datascript schema is additive - can add attributes later
- Don't worry about getting every attribute perfect now
- Focus on attributes needed for Dark Ritual flow

**Datascript Quirks**
- `d/q` returns nil for scalar queries (`. `) when no match
- `d/q` returns empty set `#{}` for collection queries when no match
- Always use appropriate return type hint in query

**Reference Implementation**
- Datascript docs: https://github.com/tonsky/datascript
- Design doc schema: fizzle-design.md lines 231-274
