title: Polluted Delta fetch ability should use the stack
design:
## Goal
Polluted Delta's activated ability should go on the stack using existing trigger infrastructure.

## Context
Current `activate-ability` (game.cljs:602-663) executes effects immediately after paying costs. Should instead use the same stack system as triggered abilities.

## Root Cause
The code at lines 640-644 executes effects directly:
```clojure
;; No selection needed - execute all effects
{:db (reduce (fn [db' effect]
               (effects/execute-effect db' player-id effect))
             db-after-costs
             effects-list)
 :pending-selection nil}
```

Should instead add to stack like triggered abilities do.

## Existing Infrastructure (triggers.cljs)
- `create-trigger` - creates trigger entity with type, source, player, data
- `add-trigger-to-stack` - adds trigger with stack-order
- `resolve-trigger` multimethod - dispatches on `:trigger/type`

Activated abilities can use this same system:
- `:trigger/type` → `:activated-ability`
- `:trigger/source` → the permanent's object-id
- `:trigger/effects` → the ability's effects list
- `:trigger/uses-stack?` → true

## Implementation
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs (activate-ability function)

### Step 1: Modify activate-ability to add to stack instead of executing
```clojure
;; Instead of executing effects directly:
(let [ability-trigger (triggers/create-trigger
                        :activated-ability
                        object-id
                        player-id
                        {:effects effects-list
                         :remaining-effects effects-after})]  ;; for tutor case
  {:db (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack db-after-costs ability-trigger)
   :pending-selection nil})
```

### Step 2: Add resolve-trigger method for :activated-ability
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs

```clojure
(defmethod resolve-trigger :activated-ability
  [db trigger]
  (let [player-id (:trigger/player trigger)
        effects (get-in trigger [:trigger/data :effects])]
    (reduce (fn [d effect]
              (effects/execute-effect d player-id effect))
            db
            effects)))
```

### Step 3: Handle selection effects on resolution
For tutor effects, the selection modal should appear on RESOLUTION, not activation.
This matches MTG rules - you search when the ability resolves.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Polluted Delta ability creates trigger on stack when activated
- [ ] Sacrifice happens immediately (as cost), not on resolution
- [ ] Tutor selection appears when ability RESOLVES from stack
- [ ] Can respond to ability (priority passes after activation)
- [ ] Existing mana abilities still resolve immediately (no stack)
- [ ] Storm trigger, City of Brass trigger still work correctly

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO creating new stack system - use existing trigger infrastructure
- ❌ NO executing effects on activation (except mana abilities)
- ❌ NO breaking mana ability immediate resolution
close_reason: Implemented activated ability stack usage. Costs paid on activation, effects resolve from stack. Added 5 tests. All tests pass.

title: Phase 2: Card Batch + Selection UI
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Basic lands (Island, Swamp) produce typed mana when tapped
- Lotus Petal sacrifices to produce 1 mana of any color
- Lion's Eye Diamond sacrifices + discards hand to produce 3 mana of any color (timing restriction deferred)
- Careful Study draws 2, then player selects 2 cards to discard
- Mental Note mills 2 from self, then draws 1
- Player can select specific cards from hand via modal UI
- Graveyard contents visible with card count for threshold tracking

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Island taps for blue mana
- [ ] Swamp taps for black mana
- [ ] Lotus Petal can be sacrificed for any color mana
- [ ] LED can be activated (sacrifice + discard hand) for 3 mana of chosen color
- [ ] Careful Study draws 2, shows selection modal, discards player's 2 chosen cards
- [ ] Mental Note mills 2 from own library, draws 1
- [ ] Selection modal appears when effect requires player choice
- [ ] Selection modal enforces exact count (must select N cards)
- [ ] Graveyard view shows all cards in graveyard zone
- [ ] Graveyard count visible for threshold tracking (7+ indicator)
- [ ] All new cards added to iggy_pop.cljs all-cards vector
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card IDs in engine code (data-over-code: engine interprets card data generically)
- ❌ NO auto-selecting discard targets (user agency: player must choose which cards to discard)
- ❌ NO blocking UI during selection (UX: modal must be dismissable/cancelable)
- ❌ NO skipping selection for 'obvious' cases (consistency: always show modal when :selection :player)

## Approach
Implement cards as pure EDN data following existing patterns. Add new cost types (:sacrifice-self, :discard-hand) to costs.cljs. Add :discard effect type to effects.cljs with :selection :player support. Create modal component for card selection that pauses game resolution until player confirms. Add graveyard-view component showing zone contents with threshold indicator.

## Architecture
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Add 5 new card definitions
- src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs - Add :sacrifice-self and :discard-hand cost types
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Add :discard effect with selection support
- src/main/fizzle/core.cljs - Add selection-modal and graveyard-view components
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs - Add selection state management events
- src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs - Add :game/pending-selection schema if needed

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Phase 1 has only 5 cards (Dark Ritual, Cabal Ritual, Brain Freeze, City of Brass, Gemstone Mine). Phase 2 needs the full Iggy Pop deck playable. This batch adds core mana acceleration (Lotus Petal, LED) and card filtering (Careful Study, Mental Note) plus basic lands.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Existing card pattern with :card/abilities, :card/effects
- engine/costs.cljs - Has :tap and :remove-counter costs, needs :sacrifice-self and :discard-hand
- engine/effects.cljs - Has :draw, :mill, :add-mana, needs :discard with selection
- core.cljs - Reagent components, no modal system yet

**External:**
- MTG rules: LED 'activate only as an instant' - deferred to future task per user decision

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Modal overlay for card selection ✓
**What it is:** Popup component showing hand contents, player clicks cards to select, confirm button submits choice.

**Chosen because:** Reusable for Intuition (select from 3), IGG (select from graveyard), Cunning Wish (select from sideboard). User selected this approach.

#### 2. Inline hand selection ❌
**What it is:** Multi-select mode in existing hand view with highlight states.

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** More complex state management, harder to reuse for non-hand selections.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Modal approach proves too intrusive for gameplay flow.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- 5 cards (Island, Swamp, Lotus Petal, LED, Careful Study, Mental Note)
- Selection modal for discard
- Graveyard view with threshold indicator

**Out of scope:**
- LED timing restriction (deferred - separate task)
- Other Iggy Pop cards (future batches)
- Graveyard interaction (return cards, flashback)

### Open Questions
- Should selection modal be cancelable? (default: yes, returns to pre-cast state)
- Graveyard card ordering? (default: most recent on top)

## Design Discovery

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Which cards first? | All 5 (lands, Petal, LED, draw spells) | Batch implementation |
| LED timing restriction? | Defer - just add discard cost | Simpler now, timing task later |
| Discard selection? | Player selects cards | Need selection UI |
| Selection UI style? | Modal overlay | Reusable component |
| Graveyard view? | Yes, include | Track threshold progress |

### Dead-End Paths
#### Random discard
**Why explored:** Simplest implementation
**Why abandoned:** User wants player agency in discard decisions

title: Feature: Event-Driven Trigger System
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Game engine produces events when game state changes (phase entry, tapping, zone changes)
- Cards register triggers dynamically when entering zones where triggers are active
- Cards unregister triggers when leaving those zones
- Triggers match against events and fire when conditions are met
- Turn-based actions (untap, draw, cleanup) are modeled as game-rule triggers
- Turn-based actions execute immediately (no stack), card triggers use the stack
- Draw step automatically draws a card (skip turn 1)
- Triggers can be active from any zone (battlefield, graveyard, library, etc.)
- Existing untap logic refactored to use new system

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Event bus produces events for phase entry, permanent tapping, zone changes
- [ ] Triggers register/unregister dynamically on zone entry/exit
- [ ] Turn-based actions fire before card triggers
- [ ] Draw step draws a card automatically (skipped on turn 1)
- [ ] Untap step untaps permanents via new trigger system (refactored from start-turn)
- [ ] City of Brass trigger migrated to event-driven system
- [ ] Card triggers go on stack, turn-based actions don't
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] New tests for event bus and trigger registration
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO scanning all battlefield cards after every action (use event subscription)
- ❌ NO hardcoded phase logic in advance-phase/start-turn (use registered triggers)
- ❌ NO battlefield-only trigger assumption (must support graveyard, library, etc.)
- ❌ NO breaking existing functionality (City of Brass must still work)

## Approach
Replace current scan-based trigger system with event-driven pub/sub architecture. Game actions produce events, triggers subscribe to event types they care about. Turn-based actions and card triggers unified under same event system with different resolution modes (immediate vs stack).

## Architecture
### New Files
- src/main/fizzle/engine/events.cljs - Event bus, event production
- src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger-registry.cljs - Dynamic trigger registration

### Modified Files
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs - Refactor to use event matching
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs - Produce events on phase change, call event bus
- src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs - Register/unregister triggers on zone change

### Event Structure
```clojure
{:event/type :phase-entered
 :event/phase :draw
 :event/turn 2
 :event/player :player-1}

{:event/type :permanent-tapped
 :event/object-id <uuid>
 :event/controller :player-1}

{:event/type :zone-change
 :event/object-id <uuid>
 :event/from-zone :library
 :event/to-zone :graveyard}
```

### Trigger Structure
```clojure
{:trigger/id <uuid>
 :trigger/source :game-rule | <object-id>
 :trigger/event-type :phase-entered
 :trigger/filter {:phase :draw}
 :trigger/effects [...]
 :trigger/uses-stack? false
 :trigger/active-zones #{:battlefield}}
```

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Current trigger system scans all battlefield cards after every action. This doesn't scale and can't handle triggers from other zones (Gaea's Blessing in graveyard). Also, turn-based actions are scattered across functions rather than unified.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- engine/triggers.cljs:54-114 - Current multimethod check/resolve pattern
- engine/triggers.cljs:179-211 - fire-matching-triggers scans battlefield
- events/game.cljs:188-200 - advance-phase just changes phase value
- events/game.cljs:221-236 - start-turn has inline untap logic
- engine/state-based.cljs - Registry pattern example we can follow

**Trigger Types Needed:**
- :phase-entered - for upkeep/draw/end step triggers
- :permanent-tapped - for City of Brass style triggers
- :land-tapped-for-mana - for Mana Flare style triggers
- :zone-change - for Gaea's Blessing, Emrakul style triggers

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Event-Driven Pub/Sub System ✓

**What it is:** Cards register triggers to an event bus when entering active zones. Game produces events, triggers subscribe and fire when matching events occur.

**Pros:**
- Scalable (no scanning)
- Supports any-zone triggers
- Unifies turn-based actions and card triggers
- Matches MTG's event-driven nature

**Cons:**
- Larger refactor than minimal fix
- Need to migrate existing triggers

**Chosen because:** Correct architecture for MTG trigger system, enables future cards

#### 2. Enhanced Scan-Based System ❌

**What it is:** Keep scanning but add event matching instead of state diff.

**Why we looked at this:** Less invasive change

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Still O(n) scan after every action, can't handle non-battlefield triggers cleanly

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Performance profiling shows event registration is actually slower

#### 3. Minimal Draw Step Fix ❌

**What it is:** Just add draw logic inline to advance-phase, deal with proper system later.

**Why we looked at this:** Quick fix for immediate need

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Would be thrown away immediately when building proper system

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Never - user explicitly rejected this approach

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Event bus and production
- Trigger registry with zone-aware registration
- Phase-entered, permanent-tapped, zone-change events
- Turn-based actions (draw, untap, cleanup)
- City of Brass migration to new system

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Mana Flare style global land triggers (add when card implemented)
- Combat damage triggers (add when combat implemented)
- Death triggers (add when destroy effects implemented)

### Open Questions
- Should event history be retained for debugging/replay? (decide during implementation)
- How to handle replacement effects that modify events? (defer until needed)

## Design Discovery

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Automatic or manual draw? | Automatic | Draw fires as turn-based action on phase entry |
| Skip turn 1 draw? | Yes, matches MTG rules | Turn-based actions need access to turn number |
| Full system or minimal fix? | Full system now | Build event-driven architecture, not a hack |
| Turn-based before or after card triggers? | Turn-based first | Two-phase resolution: immediate actions, then stack |
| Refactor untap? | Yes, unify everything | All phase actions go through same system |
| Dynamic registration? | Yes, zone-aware | Register on zone entry, unregister on zone exit |
| Battlefield only? | No, any zone | Gaea's Blessing needs library/graveyard triggers |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Current Trigger System Analysis
**Question explored:** How do triggers currently work?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/triggers.cljs - Full file review

**Findings:**
- Multimethod dispatch on :trigger/type for check and resolve
- fire-matching-triggers scans ALL battlefield objects
- Two functions: fire-matching-triggers (stack) vs fire-and-resolve-triggers (immediate)
- :becomes-tapped compares old-db vs new-db state

**Conclusion:** Good pattern with multimethod dispatch, but scan-based approach won't scale

#### State-Based Actions Registry Pattern
**Question explored:** Is there a registry pattern to follow?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/state-based.cljs

**Findings:**
- Uses atom-based registry: (defonce sbas (atom {}))
- register-sba! adds handlers
- check-and-execute-sbas iterates registry

**Conclusion:** Can use similar pattern for trigger registry
close_reason: All success criteria met, automated review passed, LIFO stack bug fixed

title: Create player library on game init
design:
## Goal
Initialize player's 60-card shuffled library at game start so draw effect works across multiple turns.

## Context
Epic success criterion: 'Player starts with 7-card hand drawn from shuffled 60-card library'
Currently ::init-game creates opponent library (40 cards) but NOT player library.
Player starts with hardcoded 3-card hand instead of drawing from library.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (TDD cycle with 6 tests + implementation)

## Files to Modify
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs - Modify ::init-game event handler
- src/test/fizzle/events/init_game_test.cljs - NEW: Test file for game initialization

## Implementation Checklist
1. [ ] Create test file src/test/fizzle/events/init_game_test.cljs
2. [ ] RED: Write test_player_library_has_60_cards_after_init
   - Call init-game, query player's library zone
   - Assert count = 60
3. [ ] GREEN: Add library creation to ::init-game
   - Create make-test-deck function: 12x each of dark-ritual, cabal-ritual, brain-freeze, city-of-brass, gemstone-mine
   - Use (shuffle ...) to randomize order
   - Create objects with :object/zone :library and :object/position 0-59
4. [ ] RED: Write test_player_library_cards_have_correct_distribution
   - Query library cards by type
   - Assert 12x each of 5 card types
5. [ ] GREEN: Verify deck composition is correct
6. [ ] RED: Write test_player_starts_with_7_card_hand
   - Call init-game, query player's hand zone
   - Assert count = 7
7. [ ] GREEN: After creating library, call draw effect 7 times OR create 7 hand objects from top of library
8. [ ] RED: Write test_library_has_53_cards_after_initial_draw
   - Assert library count = 53 (60 - 7)
9. [ ] REFACTOR: Remove hardcoded 3-card hand creation (lines 59-65 in game.cljs)
10. [ ] VERIFY: make test passes, no hardcoded starting hands remain

## Pattern to Follow
Opponent library creation in game.cljs lines 66-70:
```clojure
;; Create opponent library (40 cards so mill has targets)
(d/transact! conn (vec (for [i (range 40)]
                         {:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card dr-eid :object/zone :library
                          :object/owner opp-eid :object/controller opp-eid
                          :object/tapped false :object/position i})))
```

## Test Deck Composition
Per epic requirements:
- 12x City of Brass (land)
- 12x Gemstone Mine (land)
- 12x Dark Ritual (instant)
- 12x Cabal Ritual (instant)
- 12x Brain Freeze (instant)
Total: 60 cards

## Key Considerations

### Shuffle Testing Approach
ClojureScript's shuffle uses Math.random() which is non-deterministic.
- Do NOT test for specific shuffled order (flaky)
- DO test that library contains correct cards (order-independent)
- OPTIONALLY: Test two shuffles produce different orders (statistically likely but not guaranteed)
- Acceptable: Trust ClojureScript's shuffle implementation

### Drawing Initial Hand
Two approaches:
1. Use existing draw effect 7 times (preferred - exercises existing code)
2. Create hand objects directly during init (faster but bypasses draw)

Recommendation: Create library first, then use draw effect loop for initial 7 cards.
This ensures draw effect is tested and works correctly.

### Code to Remove
MUST remove hardcoded hand creation (game.cljs lines 59-65):
```clojure
;; Create game objects (3 cards in hand) - REMOVE THIS
(d/transact! conn [{:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card dr-eid :object/zone :hand ...}
                   {:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card cr-eid :object/zone :hand ...}
                   {:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card bf-eid :object/zone :hand ...}])
```

### Card Entity ID Lookup
Need to look up all 5 card entity IDs, not just 3:
```clojure
(let [dr-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :dark-ritual)
      cr-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :cabal-ritual)
      bf-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :brain-freeze)
      cob-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :city-of-brass)
      gm-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :gemstone-mine)]
  ...)
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Player library created with 60 cards on game init
- [ ] Library contains correct distribution: 12x each of 5 card types
- [ ] Player starts with 7-card hand drawn from library
- [ ] Library has 53 cards remaining after initial draw
- [ ] No hardcoded starting hands in codebase
- [ ] All tests in init_game_test.cljs pass
- [ ] make test passes (all project tests)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded starting hands (epic anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO deterministic deck order (must shuffle)
- ❌ NO skipping the draw step (hand comes from library via draw)
- ❌ NO tests that depend on specific shuffle order (flaky)
- ❌ NO leaving old 3-card hand code in place
close_reason: Player library now created with 60 shuffled cards (12x each of 5 card types). 7-card opening hand drawn from library. Tests verify total deck = 60, correct distribution, hand = 7, library = 53.

title: Add turn-bar component to core.cljs
design:
## Goal
Add a turn-bar component that displays turn counter, phase strip, and Next Phase/New Turn button.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing patterns
   - src/fizzle/core.cljs:45-64 - mana-pool-view pattern (subscribe, render with Tailwind)
   - src/fizzle/core.cljs:145-169 - life-view pattern
   - src/fizzle/events/game.cljs:173 - phases vector for phase list

2. Add phase-indicator component
   ```clojure
   (defn phase-indicator [phase current-phase]
     [:span {:class (if (= phase current-phase)
                      "px-2 py-1 bg-blue-500 text-white rounded"
                      "px-2 py-1 text-gray-500")}
      (name phase)])
   ```

3. Add turn-bar component
   ```clojure
   (defn turn-bar []
     (let [current-phase @(rf/subscribe [::subs/current-phase])
           current-turn @(rf/subscribe [::subs/current-turn])
           at-cleanup? (= current-phase :cleanup)]
       [:div {:class "flex items-center gap-4 p-2 bg-gray-100 border-b"}
        [:span {:class "font-bold"} (str "Turn " (or current-turn 1))]
        [:div {:class "flex gap-1"}
         (for [phase events/phases]
           ^{:key phase} [phase-indicator phase current-phase])]
        [:button {:class "px-3 py-1 bg-blue-600 text-white rounded"
                  :on-click #(rf/dispatch [(if at-cleanup? ::events/start-turn ::events/advance-phase)])}
         (if at-cleanup? "New Turn" "Next Phase")]]))
   ```

4. Insert turn-bar at top of app component
   - Find app component (around line 233)
   - Add [turn-bar] before battlefield-view

5. Add require for events/phases vector if not already imported

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Turn number displays (verify: load app, see 'Turn 1')
- [ ] All 8 phases visible in strip (count: untap, upkeep, draw, main1, combat, main2, end, cleanup)
- [ ] Current phase has blue background (main1 on startup)
- [ ] Click 'Next Phase' changes highlight to next phase
- [ ] At cleanup phase, button text shows 'New Turn'
- [ ] Click 'New Turn' resets to Turn 2, untap phase
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Nil Subscription Values:**
- current-phase or current-turn may be nil before game initializes
- Use `(or current-turn 1)` for turn display
- Use `(or current-phase :main1)` or handle nil in phase-indicator

**Phase List Import:**
- events/phases is defined in events/game.cljs:173
- May need to require `[fizzle.events.game :as events]` in core.cljs
- If already required, verify the alias used

**Styling Consistency:**
- Follow existing Tailwind patterns in core.cljs
- Keep POC simple - no hover states or animations needed

## Anti-patterns (Task-specific)
- ❌ NO hardcoded phase list (use events/phases vector)
- ❌ NO complex conditional styling (simple bg-blue-500 for current is sufficient)
- ❌ NO error throwing on nil (gracefully handle with defaults)
close_reason: Implemented turn-bar component with phase-indicator, turn counter, and Next Phase/New Turn button. Commit a861b40.

title: Implement Polluted Delta fetchland
description: Implement Polluted Delta fetchland using existing tutor infrastructure. Card sacrifices self, searches library for Island or Swamp (by subtype), puts it onto battlefield tapped.
design:
## Goal
Implement Polluted Delta - a fetchland that sacrifices itself and tutors for a land with Island or Swamp subtype, putting it directly onto the battlefield tapped.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context (VERIFIED)
- Tutor infrastructure exists from Merchant Scroll (fizzle-pk2)
- :tutor effect uses :effect/target-zone (currently only :hand supported for movement)
- :sacrifice-self cost exists in costs.cljs (used by Lotus Petal, LED)
- Basic lands have :card/subtypes #{:island} or #{:swamp} (verified in iggy_pop.cljs:142,159)
- query-library-by-criteria already supports OR logic for :card/subtypes (queries.cljs:225-226)

## Key Technical Findings

**REQUIRES CODE CHANGE:**
The current execute-tutor-selection (game.cljs:810-826) moves card to target-zone but does NOT support:
1. :battlefield as target zone (move-to-zone supports it, but no tapped state handling)
2. Setting tapped state on ETB

**Solution:** Modify execute-tutor-selection to check target-zone and set :object/tapped true when zone is :battlefield and effect specifies tapped.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Extend execute-tutor-selection for battlefield + tapped (game.cljs)

Modify execute-tutor-selection around line 820-826:

BEFORE:
```clojure
(let [card-id (first selected)
      db-after-move (zones/move-to-zone db card-id target-zone)]
  ...)
```

AFTER:
```clojure
(let [card-id (first selected)
      db-after-move (zones/move-to-zone db card-id target-zone)
      ;; If entering battlefield tapped, set tapped state
      enters-tapped? (and (= target-zone :battlefield)
                         (:selection/enters-tapped selection))
      db-with-tapped (if enters-tapped?
                       (let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
                                         db-after-move card-id)]
                         (d/db-with db-after-move [[:db/add obj-eid :object/tapped true]]))
                       db-after-move)]
  ...)
```

Also update build-tutor-selection (line 631-649) to pass :selection/enters-tapped from effect:
```clojure
:selection/enters-tapped (:effect/enters-tapped tutor-effect)
```

### Step 2: Implement Polluted Delta card definition (iggy_pop.cljs)

Add after merchant-scroll definition (~line 250):

```clojure
;; Polluted Delta - Fetchland
;; Land - Tap, Pay 1 life, Sacrifice: Search your library for an Island or Swamp card,
;; put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle. (Premodern: no life payment)
(def polluted-delta
  {:card/id :polluted-delta
   :card/name "Polluted Delta"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "{T}, Sacrifice Polluted Delta: Search your library for an Island or Swamp card, put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :activated
                     :ability/costs [{:cost/type :tap}
                                     {:cost/type :sacrifice-self}]
                     :ability/effects [{:effect/type :tutor
                                        :effect/criteria {:card/subtypes #{:island :swamp}}
                                        :effect/target-zone :battlefield
                                        :effect/enters-tapped true
                                        :effect/shuffle? true}]}]})
```

Add to all-cards vector.

### Step 3: Write tests (src/test/fizzle/cards/polluted_delta_test.cljs)

Create new test file:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.polluted-delta-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer [deftest is testing]]
            [fizzle.test-helpers :as h]))

(deftest polluted-delta-fetches-island
  (testing "Polluted Delta can fetch an Island to battlefield tapped"
    (let [game (-> (h/new-game)
                   (h/put-on-battlefield :polluted-delta)
                   (h/put-in-library :island)
                   (h/put-in-library :dark-ritual)  ; Non-target
                   (h/activate-ability :polluted-delta 0)
                   (h/select-card :island))]
      ;; Island on battlefield, tapped
      (is (h/on-battlefield? game :island))
      (is (h/tapped? game :island))
      ;; Polluted Delta sacrificed (in graveyard)
      (is (h/in-graveyard? game :polluted-delta))
      ;; Dark Ritual still in library (not selected)
      (is (h/in-library? game :dark-ritual)))))

(deftest polluted-delta-fetches-swamp
  (testing "Polluted Delta can fetch a Swamp to battlefield tapped"
    (let [game (-> (h/new-game)
                   (h/put-on-battlefield :polluted-delta)
                   (h/put-in-library :swamp)
                   (h/activate-ability :polluted-delta 0)
                   (h/select-card :swamp))]
      (is (h/on-battlefield? game :swamp))
      (is (h/tapped? game :swamp))
      (is (h/in-graveyard? game :polluted-delta)))))

(deftest polluted-delta-fail-to-find
  (testing "Polluted Delta allows fail-to-find when no valid targets"
    (let [game (-> (h/new-game)
                   (h/put-on-battlefield :polluted-delta)
                   (h/put-in-library :dark-ritual)  ; No valid targets
                   (h/activate-ability :polluted-delta 0)
                   (h/select-nothing))]  ; Fail to find
      ;; Delta still sacrificed (cost paid)
      (is (h/in-graveyard? game :polluted-delta))
      ;; Library unchanged (only shuffled)
      (is (h/in-library? game :dark-ritual)))))

(deftest polluted-delta-shuffles-library
  (testing "Library is shuffled after search (even on fail-to-find)"
    ;; This tests shuffle happened - verify library order changed
    ;; Implementation: check shuffle was called via library positions
    ))

(deftest polluted-delta-selection-shows-only-valid-targets
  (testing "Selection modal only shows Island/Swamp, not other cards"
    (let [game (-> (h/new-game)
                   (h/put-on-battlefield :polluted-delta)
                   (h/put-in-library :island)
                   (h/put-in-library :swamp)
                   (h/put-in-library :dark-ritual)
                   (h/activate-ability :polluted-delta 0))
          candidates (h/get-selection-candidates game)]
      ;; Only Island and Swamp should be candidates
      (is (= 2 (count candidates)))
      (is (every? #{:island :swamp} (map :card/id candidates))))))
```

### Step 4: Verify and commit

```bash
make validate   # Must pass
# Manual test in UI: play Polluted Delta, activate, verify selection modal, verify land enters tapped
git add -A && git commit -m "Add Polluted Delta fetchland

- Extend tutor effect to support battlefield target zone with tapped state
- Add Polluted Delta card definition with sacrifice-self + tutor ability
- Add tests for fetch, fail-to-find, and selection filtering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Polluted Delta card definition exists in iggy_pop.cljs
- [ ] Polluted Delta added to all-cards vector
- [ ] Activation requires tap + sacrifice (ability costs verified)
- [ ] Selection modal shows only Island/Swamp subtype cards
- [ ] Selected land enters battlefield TAPPED (not untapped)
- [ ] Polluted Delta is in graveyard after activation (sacrificed)
- [ ] Library shuffled after search (verify shuffle-library called)
- [ ] Fail-to-find option available and works (sacrifice still happens)
- [ ] 5+ tests pass in polluted_delta_test.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-select for tutors (rules: fail-to-find must always be available)
- ❌ NO putting land onto battlefield untapped (Polluted Delta enters tapped per MTG rules)
- ❌ NO fetching non-land cards (criteria must filter by subtype)
- ❌ NO skipping sacrifice cost (sacrifice happens even if fail-to-find)
- ❌ NO hardcoding card IDs in engine code (effect system is generic)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty Library After Fetch**
- If library has 1 card (the target), after fetch library is empty
- Shuffle of empty library should be no-op (verified: shuffle-library handles this)

**Edge Case: No Valid Targets in Library**
- User must be shown fail-to-find option
- Sacrifice cost already paid (on stack resolution, not selection)
- Anti-pattern: auto-selecting is forbidden

**Edge Case: Polluted Delta Destroyed While Ability on Stack**
- Ability on stack still resolves (source independence)
- No special handling needed - standard MTG rules

**Edge Case: Multiple Valid Targets**
- Selection modal shows all valid options
- Player chooses exactly 1

**Testing Bug Risk: Tapped State**
- Must verify land enters TAPPED, not untapped
- Regression risk: future changes to move-to-zone could lose tapped state

**Testing Bug Risk: Sacrifice Timing**
- Sacrifice is COST, happens before effect resolves
- Delta should be in graveyard by the time selection modal appears
close_reason: Polluted Delta fetchland implemented with battlefield tutor support and enters-tapped handling

title: Task 2: Mana System (add, pay, can-pay?)
design:
## Goal

Implement the mana system: add mana to pool, pay costs, and check if costs can be paid. This is foundational for casting spells.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1: Datascript schema and initial state with Dark Ritual in hand.
Discovery: Datascript pull queries return nested data under attribute key.

## Implementation

### 1. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.engine.mana-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state empty-mana-pool]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]))

;; === add-mana tests ===

(deftest add-mana-adds-black-mana-test
  (testing "add-mana adds black mana to empty pool"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          db' (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:black 3})]
      (is (= {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 3 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
             (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))))

(deftest add-mana-accumulates-test
  (testing "add-mana accumulates with existing mana"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          db' (-> db
                  (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 2})
                  (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))]
      (is (= 3 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest add-mana-multiple-colors-test
  (testing "add-mana handles multiple colors at once"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          db' (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:black 2 :blue 1})]
      (is (= 2 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))
      (is (= 1 (:blue (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest add-mana-empty-map-test
  (testing "add-mana with empty map returns unchanged db"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          db' (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {})]
      (is (= (q/get-mana-pool db :player-1)
             (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))))

;; === can-pay? tests ===

(deftest can-pay-empty-pool-test
  (testing "can-pay? returns false with empty pool"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (false? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {:black 1}))))))

(deftest can-pay-exact-mana-test
  (testing "can-pay? returns true with exact mana"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))]
      (is (true? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {:black 1}))))))

(deftest can-pay-excess-mana-test
  (testing "can-pay? returns true with excess mana"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 3}))]
      (is (true? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {:black 1}))))))

(deftest can-pay-wrong-color-test
  (testing "can-pay? returns false with wrong color mana"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:blue 5}))]
      (is (false? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {:black 1}))))))

(deftest can-pay-empty-cost-test
  (testing "can-pay? returns true for empty cost (free spell)"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (true? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {}))))))

(deftest can-pay-zero-amount-test
  (testing "can-pay? returns true for zero amount cost"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (true? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {:black 0}))))))

;; === pay-mana tests ===

(deftest pay-mana-removes-mana-test
  (testing "pay-mana removes mana from pool"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 3}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:black 1})]
      (is (= 2 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest pay-mana-exact-payment-test
  (testing "pay-mana handles exact payment (pool goes to zero)"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:black 1})]
      (is (= 0 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest pay-mana-empty-cost-test
  (testing "pay-mana with empty cost returns unchanged pool"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 3}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {})]
      (is (= 3 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))
```

### 2. Implementation checklist

- [ ] mkdir -p src/main/fizzle/engine
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs - Mana operations
  - (add-mana db player-id mana-map) -> db with updated pool
  - (can-pay? db player-id cost-map) -> boolean
  - (pay-mana db player-id cost-map) -> db with reduced pool
- [ ] mkdir -p src/test/fizzle/engine  
- [ ] test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs - Unit tests (13 tests)

### 3. Key implementation details

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.mana
  "Mana pool operations for Fizzle.
   
   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db
   Caller must check can-pay? before calling pay-mana."
  (:require [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]))

(defn add-mana
  "Add mana to a player's pool. Pure function: (db, args) -> db
   
   mana-to-add is a map like {:black 3} or {:black 2 :blue 1}.
   Empty map {} is a no-op."
  [db player-id mana-to-add]
  (if (empty? mana-to-add)
    db
    (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)
          current-pool (q/get-mana-pool db player-id)
          new-pool (merge-with + current-pool mana-to-add)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add player-eid :player/mana-pool new-pool]]))))

(defn can-pay?
  "Check if player can pay a mana cost.
   
   Returns true if player has sufficient mana of each color.
   Empty cost {} or zero amounts {:black 0} always return true."
  [db player-id cost]
  (if (empty? cost)
    true
    (let [pool (q/get-mana-pool db player-id)]
      (every? (fn [[color amount]]
                (>= (get pool color 0) amount))
              cost))))

(defn pay-mana
  "Remove mana from player's pool to pay a cost.
   
   IMPORTANT: Caller MUST verify can-pay? first.
   Calling pay-mana without sufficient mana will result in negative pool values.
   Empty cost {} is a no-op."
  [db player-id cost]
  (if (empty? cost)
    db
    (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)
          current-pool (q/get-mana-pool db player-id)
          new-pool (merge-with - current-pool cost)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add player-eid :player/mana-pool new-pool]]))))
```

### 4. Verification

```bash
export PATH="/usr/local/opt/openjdk/bin:$PATH"
npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] add-mana adds mana to player's pool correctly
- [ ] add-mana accumulates with existing mana
- [ ] add-mana handles multiple colors
- [ ] add-mana with empty map is no-op
- [ ] can-pay? returns false with empty pool
- [ ] can-pay? returns true with sufficient mana
- [ ] can-pay? returns false with wrong color
- [ ] can-pay? returns true for empty cost {}
- [ ] can-pay? returns true for zero amount {:black 0}
- [ ] pay-mana removes mana from pool
- [ ] pay-mana with empty cost is no-op
- [ ] All 13 mana tests pass
- [ ] Functions are pure (db in, db out)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO mutation outside d/db-with (purity: all changes via transactions)
- ❌ NO throwing on insufficient mana (caller should check can-pay? first)
- ❌ NO hardcoded player IDs (parameterized)
- ❌ NO handling generic mana yet (defer to later task - Dark Ritual has no generic cost)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty Cost**
- {} means free spell (like Lotus Petal activation cost)
- {:black 0} also means no black needed
- Both should return true for can-pay? and be no-ops for pay-mana

**Edge Case: Nonexistent Player**
- add-mana/pay-mana will throw if player doesn't exist (q/get-player-eid returns nil)
- This is acceptable - invalid player-id is a programming error
- Tests assume valid player-id (covered by schema tests)

**Edge Case: Negative Mana**
- pay-mana could produce negative values if called without can-pay? check
- Documented in docstring: caller MUST verify can-pay? first
- Not defensive - trusts caller (engine code, not user input)

**Not Implemented Yet (Out of Scope)**
- Generic mana costs (e.g., {2}{B} where 2 can be any color)
- Dark Ritual costs just {:black 1}, no generic needed yet
- Will add when implementing cards with generic costs

**Reference Implementation**
- Design doc mana section: fizzle-design.md lines 347-363

title: Task 4: Effect Interpreter (execute-effect add-mana)
design:
## Goal

Implement the effect interpreter that executes card effects. Starting with :add-mana effect type, which is needed for Dark Ritual's resolution.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed Task 3: Zone transitions (move-to-zone).
Discovery: Datascript d/db-with works well for zone changes. q/get-object pulls full entity with card reference.

We need an effect interpreter that:
- Takes a db and an effect map
- Dispatches on :effect/type
- Returns new db with effect applied

## Implementation

### 1. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.engine.effects-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.effects :as fx]))

;; === execute-effect :add-mana tests ===

(deftest execute-effect-add-mana-test
  (testing "execute-effect with :add-mana adds mana to player's pool"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          effect {:effect/type :add-mana
                  :effect/mana {:black 3}}
          db' (fx/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
      (is (= 3 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest execute-effect-add-mana-multiple-colors-test
  (testing "execute-effect with :add-mana handles multiple colors"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          effect {:effect/type :add-mana
                  :effect/mana {:black 2 :red 1}}
          db' (fx/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
      (is (= 2 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))
      (is (= 1 (:red (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest execute-effect-add-mana-accumulates-test
  (testing "execute-effect with :add-mana accumulates with existing mana"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          effect {:effect/type :add-mana
                  :effect/mana {:black 3}}
          db' (-> db
                  (fx/execute-effect :player-1 effect)
                  (fx/execute-effect :player-1 effect))]
      (is (= 6 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest execute-effect-unknown-type-test
  (testing "execute-effect with unknown type returns db unchanged"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          effect {:effect/type :unknown-effect}
          db' (fx/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
      (is (= (q/get-mana-pool db :player-1)
             (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))))

(deftest execute-effect-nil-type-test
  (testing "execute-effect with nil type (missing key) returns db unchanged"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          effect {}  ;; no :effect/type key
          db' (fx/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
      (is (= (q/get-mana-pool db :player-1)
             (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))))
```

### 2. Implementation checklist

- [ ] Create src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs - Unit tests (5 tests)
- [ ] Run tests RED (namespace not found)
- [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Effect interpreter
  - (execute-effect db player-id effect) -> db with effect applied
  - defmulti dispatching on :effect/type
  - defmethod for :add-mana
  - defmethod :default returns db unchanged (unknown effects are no-ops)
- [ ] Run tests GREEN (all 5 pass)
- [ ] Commit changes

### 3. Key implementation details

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.effects
  "Effect interpreter for Fizzle.

   Dispatches on :effect/type to execute card effects.
   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db"
  (:require [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]))

(defmulti execute-effect
  "Execute an effect on the game state.

   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     player-id - The player who controls this effect
     effect - Map with :effect/type and effect-specific keys

   Returns:
     New db with effect applied.

   Unknown effect types return db unchanged (no-op)."
  (fn [_db _player-id effect] (:effect/type effect)))

(defmethod execute-effect :default
  [db _player-id _effect]
  ;; Unknown effect types are no-ops
  db)

(defmethod execute-effect :add-mana
  [db player-id effect]
  (mana/add-mana db player-id (:effect/mana effect)))
```

### 4. Verification

```bash
export PATH="/usr/local/opt/openjdk/bin:$PATH"
npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] execute-effect with :add-mana adds mana to player's pool
- [ ] execute-effect handles multiple colors in one effect
- [ ] execute-effect accumulates with existing mana
- [ ] execute-effect with unknown type returns db unchanged
- [ ] execute-effect with nil/missing type returns db unchanged
- [ ] All 5 effect interpreter tests pass
- [ ] Verification command passes: `npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js`

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Missing :effect/mana**
- If :add-mana effect has no :effect/mana key, passes nil to add-mana
- mana/add-mana uses (empty? mana-to-add) which returns true for nil
- Result: no-op, which is acceptable behavior

**Edge Case: Unknown Effect Type**
- Unknown types return db unchanged (silent no-op)
- This is per anti-patterns: engine trusts caller, rules layer validates
- defmulti :default method handles both unknown keywords and nil

**Edge Case: nil Effect Type (Missing Key)**
- If effect map has no :effect/type key, dispatch value is nil
- defmulti dispatches to :default method (no-op)
- Added explicit test to verify this behavior

**Architecture Note**
- Using defmulti for extensibility: adding new effect types = new defmethod
- Matches design doc's effect interpreter pattern
- player-id passed for effects that need to know who controls

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO throwing on unknown effect types (silent no-op per design)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in interpreter (data-driven: card defines effect data)
- ❌ NO side effects (purity: interpreter transforms db)

Tests deleted (not replaced) since they tested Clojure language features, not production code.

title: Feature: Card Implementation Skill with Scryfall Validation
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Skill at .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md provides complete card implementation workflow
- DSL reference at docs/card-dsl.md documents all effect/ability/cost/trigger types with examples
- Skill fetches card data from Scryfall API before implementation
- Skill fetches rulings from Scryfall to inform test cases
- Skeleton generation includes Scryfall-verified attributes marked with comments
- Tests must be derived from oracle text and rulings only - no hallucinated rules
- Consultation checkpoints when DSL doesn't support oracle text behavior
- Verification phase cross-checks final implementation against Scryfall data
- Skill blocks completion until all required tests pass

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md exists with complete workflow
- [ ] docs/card-dsl.md exists with comprehensive DSL reference
- [ ] Skill workflow: Scryfall lookup → skeleton → implement → test → verify
- [ ] DSL docs cover: effects, abilities, costs, triggers, conditions, gotchas
- [ ] Skill includes consultation checkpoint format for simplifications
- [ ] Skill references comprehensive rules URL
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hallucinated MTG rules (validation: all behavior must trace to oracle text, rulings, or comprehensive rules)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card data in skill (maintainability: must fetch from Scryfall at runtime)
- ❌ NO tests that assume rules not in oracle/rulings (correctness: prevents mistakes like 'cannot activate without targets')
- ❌ NO skipping consultation for unsupported behaviors (alignment: user decides simplifications)
- ❌ NO DSL docs embedded in skill file (separation: docs/card-dsl.md is single source of truth)
- ❌ NO completion without passing tests (quality: tests are blocking requirement)

## Approach
Create two deliverables: (1) comprehensive DSL documentation at docs/card-dsl.md covering all current effect types, ability types, cost types, and trigger types with examples from existing cards; (2) card implementation skill at .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md that orchestrates the workflow from Scryfall lookup through verification.

The skill enforces Scryfall validation at both ends - generating skeleton with verified attributes upfront, and cross-checking the final implementation. Tests are derived solely from oracle text and Scryfall rulings to prevent rule hallucination.

## Architecture
- docs/card-dsl.md - Living documentation, updated as DSL evolves
- .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md - Workflow skill with 5 phases
- Scryfall API integration via WebFetch in skill
- Comprehensive rules reference: https://media.wizards.com/2026/downloads/MagicCompRules%2020260116.txt

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Card implementations have had errors in basic attributes (mana costs) and effect DSL usage. No systematic validation against canonical card data. Tests have included hallucinated rules that don't match actual MTG behavior.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - All card definitions, pure EDN data
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Effect interpreter with multimethod dispatch
- src/main/fizzle/engine/abilities.cljs - Mana and activated ability handling
- src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs - Cost payment system
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs - Trigger system for events
- src/test/fizzle/cards/ - Card-specific test files

**External:**
- Scryfall API (api.scryfall.com) - Canonical card data, oracle text, rulings
- Comprehensive Rules - Official game rules document

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Standalone local skill with separate DSL docs ✓

**What it is:** Skill at .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md handles workflow. Comprehensive DSL reference at docs/card-dsl.md can evolve with codebase.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed existing cards for DSL patterns
- Identified all effect/ability/cost/trigger types in use
- Confirmed Scryfall API provides needed data

**Pros:**
- Clean separation of workflow vs reference
- DSL docs can be updated as system evolves
- Local to repo (project-specific)

**Cons:**
- Two files to maintain

**Chosen because:** DSL reference needs to evolve with codebase; skill workflow is relatively stable.

#### 2. Extend hyperpowers:brainstorming directly ❌

**What it is:** Add card-specific sections to the brainstorming skill in the hyperpowers plugin.

**Why we looked at this:** Avoids creating new skill file

**Pros:**
- Single skill file

**Cons:**
- Bloats generic brainstorming skill
- Card-specific logic mixed with general workflow
- Not local to Fizzle repo

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Card implementation is Fizzle-specific; doesn't belong in generic hyperpowers plugin.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We're building a generic MTG card tool that others would use.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Skill workflow for implementing new cards
- Comprehensive DSL documentation
- Scryfall integration for validation
- Test derivation from oracle/rulings

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Automated effect implementation from oracle text (deferred - would require NLP)
- Bulk card import (deferred - do one card at a time first)
- Card legality checking beyond Premodern (not needed for Iggy Pop focus)

### Open Questions
- Should DSL docs include effect implementation code examples? (decide during task 1)
- How detailed should consultation checkpoint prompts be? (decide during skill writing)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Scryfall timing? | Both before and after | Skeleton generation + final verification |
| Pain points? | Attributes, DSL syntax, test gaps | Focus areas for docs and skill |
| Code generation? | Yes, skeleton with TODOs | Skill generates card def template |
| DSL docs location? | Separate file in repo | docs/card-dsl.md, skill references it |
| Integration? | Extension to brainstorming | Brainstorming calls skill for card tasks |
| Test enforcement? | Mandatory, derived from oracle/rulings only | No hallucinated rules in tests |
| Consultation? | Required for simplifications | User decides where Fizzle deviates from rules |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Card DSL Structure
**Question explored:** How are cards defined in Fizzle?
**Sources consulted:**
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Full card definitions
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Effect multimethod
- Recent commits for card additions

**Findings:**
- Cards are pure EDN maps with :card/* namespaced keys
- Effects dispatch on :effect/type keyword
- Abilities are :mana (don't use stack) or :activated (use stack)
- Triggers have :trigger/type and :trigger/effects

**Conclusion:** DSL is well-structured; needs documentation to prevent misuse

#### Scryfall API
**Question explored:** What data does Scryfall provide?
**Sources consulted:**
- Scryfall API documentation
- Sample card responses

**Findings:**
- /cards/named?exact=X returns full card data
- rulings_uri provides separate rulings endpoint
- All needed fields available: mana_cost, cmc, colors, type_line, oracle_text

**Conclusion:** Scryfall is canonical source for card data and rulings

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Example tests were wrong (rules hallucination)' → Tests must derive from oracle/rulings only, not assumed behavior
- 'Fizzle is a model, not the full game' → Consultation checkpoints for intentional simplifications

title: Epic: Phase 1 Core Engine - Dark Ritual End-to-End
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Dark Ritual can be cast from hand when player has B available
- Casting Dark Ritual moves it from hand → stack → graveyard
- Resolving Dark Ritual adds BBB to player's mana pool
- Mana pool tracks all five colors plus colorless
- Storm count increments when spells are cast
- Game state is queryable via Datascript
- All engine functions are pure: (db, args) -> db
- No UI required - all interaction via REPL

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Can create initial game state with Dark Ritual in hand
- [ ] can-cast? returns true when player has B, false otherwise
- [ ] cast-spell moves card to stack and pays mana
- [ ] resolve-spell executes add-mana effect and moves to graveyard
- [ ] Mana pool correctly shows BBB after resolution
- [ ] Storm count is 1 after casting
- [ ] All functions testable via REPL with direct calls
- [ ] Unit tests pass for mana system
- [ ] Unit tests pass for zone transitions
- [ ] Unit tests pass for effect interpreter (add-mana)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO internal randomness in engine functions (determinism: fork/replay requires all RNG as parameters)
- ❌ NO re-frame dependency in engine namespace (isolation: engine must be pure, re-frame wraps later)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card logic in engine (data-driven: cards are data, engine interprets)
- ❌ NO mutable state outside Datascript (simplicity: single source of truth)
- ❌ NO UI code in this phase (scope: REPL-only validates engine before UI work)
- ❌ NO skipping Datascript for 'simpler' atoms (architecture: Datascript enables declarative queries)

## Approach

Engine-first development with deferred re-frame integration. Build pure functions that transform Datascript state, validate via REPL and unit tests. The engine functions will later be wrapped by re-frame events, but remain independently testable.

All randomness passed as parameters to enable deterministic replay. Action granularity matches user intent (cast-spell is one action) even though engine does multiple db updates internally.

Start with Dark Ritual only - the simplest spell that exercises the full cast→resolve→effect pipeline.

## Architecture

```
src/fizzle/
├── db/
│   ├── schema.cljs     # Datascript schema for cards, objects, players, game
│   └── queries.cljs    # Common Datalog queries
├── engine/
│   ├── mana.cljs       # Mana pool: add, pay, can-pay?
│   ├── zones.cljs      # Zone transitions: move-to-zone
│   ├── rules.cljs      # Core rules: can-cast?, cast-spell, resolve-spell
│   └── effects.cljs    # Effect interpreter: execute-effect multimethod
└── cards/
    └── iggy_pop.cljs   # Card definitions (starting with Dark Ritual)
```

Key data flow:
1. Initial state created with Datascript schema + Dark Ritual in hand
2. can-cast? queries db for card in hand + sufficient mana
3. cast-spell: pay-mana → move-to-zone :stack → increment storm
4. resolve-spell: execute-effects → move-to-zone :graveyard
5. Effect interpreter dispatches on :effect/type, executes :add-mana

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Need a working game engine foundation before building UI, fork/replay, or additional cards. Dark Ritual is the simplest spell that validates the entire pipeline.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- Project bootstrapped with shadow-cljs but no source files yet
- Design doc at fizzle-design.md defines schema, effect system, architecture
- src/main/ and src/test/ directories exist but are empty

**Design Doc Key Patterns:**
- Datascript schema (fizzle-design.md lines 231-274)
- Effect interpreter multimethod (lines 480-528)
- Card definition format (lines 277-345)
- Engine function signatures (lines 435-475)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Engine-First with Deferred re-frame ✓

**What it is:** Build pure engine functions first, drive from REPL, add re-frame wrapper later.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed design doc - engine functions are pure (db, args) -> db
- re-frame layer is thin wrapper around engine calls
- Testing pure functions simpler than testing through re-frame

**Pros:**
- Maximum testability
- Faster iteration on engine design
- Clear separation of pure logic vs app wiring

**Cons:**
- Delays validating re-frame integration

**Chosen because:** Testing is priority; re-frame wrapper is mechanical given clear engine API.

#### 2. Full re-frame from Start ❌

**What it is:** Wire events, subscriptions, and effects immediately alongside engine.

**Why we looked at this:** Validates integration early, events become action log.

**Investigation:**
- More ceremony for REPL-driven development
- Tests need re-frame test helpers OR test pure functions anyway
- Design doc already specifies event structure clearly

**Pros:**
- Validates event design early
- No integration surprises

**Cons:**
- More upfront wiring
- Tests more complex
- Slower iteration

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Given detailed design doc, integration risk is low. Pure engine functions are the hard work; re-frame wrapper is mechanical.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We discover engine APIs that don't compose well with re-frame's event model.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Datascript schema for cards, objects, players, mana
- Mana system (pool, payment, generation)
- Zone transitions (hand → stack → graveyard)
- Effect interpreter (add-mana only)
- Dark Ritual card definition
- Unit tests for all engine functions

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- re-frame events/subscriptions - Phase 1b
- UI components - Phase 1c
- Additional cards (Cabal Ritual, Brain Freeze) - after Dark Ritual works
- Storm copies - after basic storm count works
- Fork/replay - Phase 3

### Open Questions

- Exact Datascript entity ID generation strategy (random UUID vs sequential?)
- Should card definitions live in Datascript or separate registry? (design doc shows both)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| MVP scope? | Single card (Dark Ritual) | Smallest vertical slice through full pipeline |
| re-frame/Datascript integration? | Datascript inside app-db | Single atom when re-frame added |
| UI priority? | REPL-only first | No UI code in Phase 1 |
| re-frame timing? | Defer to later | Engine functions pure, testable in isolation |

### Constraints for Future Integration

1. **All randomness as parameters** - no internal rand calls
2. **Functions are (db, explicit-args) -> db** - no hidden state
3. **Granularity matches user intent** - cast-spell is one conceptual action

### Dead-End Paths

None yet - this is greenfield implementation.

### Open Concerns Raised

- Integration risk when adding re-frame later → Mitigated by constraints above and clear design doc

title: Update Gemstone Mine and remove zero-counters SBA
design:
## Goal
Update Gemstone Mine card definition to include conditional sacrifice as part of mana ability effects, and remove the zero-counters SBA that incorrectly fires on any counter removal.

## Context
Completed fizzle-l0l: Effect condition system and :sacrifice effect now exist.
- check-condition multimethod with :no-counters implementation
- execute-effect wrapper checks condition before running effect-impl  
- :sacrifice effect moves target to graveyard

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study current implementations
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:79-100 - Current Gemstone Mine definition (needs :ability/effects update)
- engine/state_based.cljs:60-94 - Zero-counters SBA to remove entirely
- events/game.cljs:431-478 - activate-mana-ability (NEEDS MODIFICATION to process :ability/effects)
- engine/abilities.cljs:52-72 - activate-ability already processes :ability/effects (reference pattern)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**File: src/test/fizzle/events/game_test.cljs** (or appropriate test file)

**Test 1: test-gemstone-mine-sacrifices-on-last-counter**
- Setup: Play Gemstone Mine (enters with 3 counters), activate mana ability 3 times
- After 3rd activation: Assert Gemstone Mine is in graveyard
- Bug caught: Sacrifice not firing when counters depleted via ability

**Test 2: test-gemstone-mine-stays-with-counters-remaining**
- Setup: Play Gemstone Mine (3 counters)
- Execute: Activate mana ability once
- Assert: Gemstone Mine still on battlefield with {:mining 2} counters
- Bug caught: Premature sacrifice

**Test 3: test-gemstone-mine-no-sacrifice-on-manual-counter-removal**
- Setup: Play Gemstone Mine, then directly transact counter removal (simulate external effect)
- Use: `(d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/counters {:mining 0}]])`
- Assert: Gemstone Mine still on battlefield (no SBA triggers sacrifice)
- Bug caught: Old SBA still active or incorrectly sacrificing

**Test 4: test-mana-ability-executes-ability-effects**
- Setup: Create card with mana ability that has :ability/effects (e.g., gain 1 life)
- Execute: Activate mana ability
- Assert: Effect executed (life gained)
- Bug caught: activate-mana-ability not processing :ability/effects

### 3. Update activate-mana-ability to process :ability/effects

**File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs**

Current flow (lines 465-475) adds mana directly without processing :ability/effects.
MUST add effect processing after mana addition:

```clojure
;; After line 470 (db-after-mana)
;; Step 2b: Execute ability effects (e.g., conditional sacrifice)
db-after-effects (reduce (fn [db' effect]
                           (let [resolved-effect (if (= :self (:effect/target effect))
                                                   (assoc effect :effect/target object-id)
                                                   effect)]
                             (effects/execute-effect db' player-id resolved-effect)))
                         db-after-mana
                         (:ability/effects mana-ability []))
```

Then use db-after-effects for trigger/SBA steps.

### 4. Update Gemstone Mine card definition

**File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:96-100**

Change from:
```clojure
:card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                  :ability/cost {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}}
                  :ability/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana
                                     :effect/mana {:any 1}}]}]
```

To:
```clojure
:card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                  :ability/cost {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}}
                  :ability/produces {:any 1}
                  :ability/effects [{:effect/type :sacrifice
                                     :effect/target :self
                                     :effect/condition {:condition/type :no-counters
                                                        :condition/counter-type :mining}}]}]
```

Note: Mana production happens via :ability/produces (interpreted by activate-mana-ability), effects are separate.

### 5. Remove zero-counters SBA

**File: src/main/fizzle/engine/state_based.cljs**

Delete lines 60-94:
- `check-zero-counters` function
- `execute-sba :zero-counters` defmethod
- `(register-sba! :zero-counters check-zero-counters)`

Keep the registry infrastructure (other SBAs may use it).

### 6. Update any tests referencing SBA behavior

Search for tests that might expect zero-counters SBA to fire and update them.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Gemstone Mine sacrifices itself ONLY when mana ability activates AND counters = 0
- [ ] Gemstone Mine does NOT sacrifice when counters removed by non-ability means
- [ ] activate-mana-ability processes :ability/effects with :self resolution
- [ ] Zero-counters SBA code completely removed from state_based.cljs
- [ ] 4+ new tests pass covering sacrifice and effect processing
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make test)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Critical: activate-mana-ability Gap**
The current activate-mana-ability in game.cljs does NOT process :ability/effects.
It hardcodes mana addition instead of using the card's effect list.
MUST modify to process effects, similar to how abilities.cljs:activate-ability does.

**:self Resolution**
Effects with :effect/target :self MUST be resolved to object-id before execute-effect.
Pattern exists in game.cljs:385-388 (play-land ETB effects).
Copy this pattern when processing :ability/effects.

**Test Isolation**
Test 3 (no SBA sacrifice) MUST use direct datascript transaction to set counters to 0.
This simulates an external effect that didn't come from mana ability.
Do NOT use activate-mana-ability for this test.

**Existing Test Impact**
Check if any existing tests rely on zero-counters SBA behavior.
If so, update them to reflect correct rules (ability-based sacrifice).

**Reference Implementation**
Study abilities.cljs:52-72 (activate-ability) for effect processing pattern.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO leaving dead code (commented SBA code must be deleted, not commented)
- ❌ NO forgetting :self resolution (effects will fail silently)
- ❌ NO modifying City of Brass (only Gemstone Mine needs this fix)
- ❌ NO creating new test files (add to existing game_test.cljs or abilities_test.cljs)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Gemstone Mine checks in activate-mana-ability (generic effect processing)

title: Add integration test: storm combo scenario
design:
## Goal
Prove storm functionality works end-to-end with a realistic combo scenario.

## Context
Epic success criterion requires: 'Cast 2 rituals + Brain Freeze = 3 mill resolutions (9 cards)'

This tests:
- Storm count increments correctly (2 rituals = storm count 2)
- Brain Freeze with storm creates 2 copies (spells before = 2)
- Total 3 Brain Freeze resolutions (original + 2 copies)
- Each mills 3 cards = 9 cards total milled

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Add test to src/test/fizzle/engine/storm_test.cljs
- [ ] Create helper: add-brain-freeze-to-hand (follow add-cabal-ritual-to-hand pattern from rules_test.cljs:152-170)
- [ ] Create helper: add-opponent (copy from effects_test.cljs:62-73)
- [ ] Create helper: add-library-cards (copy from effects_test.cljs:76-96)
- [ ] Write test-storm-combo-two-rituals-brain-freeze:
  1. init-storm-test-state (provides 2 non-storm spells + storm spell in hand)
  2. add-opponent
  3. add-library-cards :player-2 with 12 card-ids (need buffer above 9)
  4. Cast non-storm-obj-1 (storm count = 1)
  5. Cast non-storm-obj-2 (storm count = 2)
  6. Cast storm-obj-1 (Brain Freeze with storm)
  7. Get storm trigger from stack (filter by :trigger/type :storm)
  8. Resolve trigger (creates 2 copies)
  9. Resolve all 3 Brain Freeze spells (original + 2 copies)
  10. Assert opponent graveyard count = 9
- [ ] Run make test to verify

## Key Code References
- init-storm-test-state: storm_test.cljs:42-96 (sets up player with storm/non-storm spells)
- triggers/resolve-trigger: triggers.cljs:53-65 (resolves :storm trigger)
- rules/resolve-spell: rules.cljs:108-122 (resolves spell, executes effects)
- add-library-cards pattern: effects_test.cljs:76-96

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Test function named test-storm-combo-two-rituals-brain-freeze
- [ ] Asserts storm count = 2 after two ritual casts
- [ ] Asserts storm count = 3 after Brain Freeze cast
- [ ] Asserts 2 copies created (count objects with :object/is-copy true)
- [ ] Asserts opponent graveyard = 9 cards after all resolutions
- [ ] Test passes: make test shows 0 failures

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO hardcoded entity IDs (use queries to find objects)
- ❌ NO skipping intermediate assertions (verify state at each step)
- ❌ NO relying on test execution order (test must be independent)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Test Independence**:
- Test must work standalone, not depend on other test state
- Use init-storm-test-state for clean starting state
- Add opponent and library cards fresh each test

**Resolution Order**:
- Storm trigger resolves FIRST (top of stack, LIFO)
- After trigger resolution: 3 Brain Freeze spells on stack
- Resolve each spell individually to simulate real game flow
- Cannot resolve all at once - must loop through stack items

**Edge Case Awareness**:
- Test uses 12 cards in library (buffer above 9 needed)
- If test fails with fewer cards, would be partial mill (not a bug)
- This integration test is happy path - edge cases covered elsewhere

**Existing Helper Reuse**:
- init-storm-test-state already creates storm spell with :storm keyword
- BUT it uses generic storm-spell, not brain-freeze specifically
- Option A: Use existing storm-spell (it has empty effects, add mill effect)
- Option B: Create brain-freeze-specific setup
- RECOMMENDED: Option A - modify storm-spell to have mill effect for this test

**Mill Target**:
- Brain Freeze targets opponent (:effect/target :opponent)
- MUST have opponent player in game state
- MUST have opponent library cards to mill
close_reason: Integration test added: test-storm-combo-two-rituals-brain-freeze verifies 2 rituals + Brain Freeze = 9 cards milled (3 resolutions x 3 cards each)

title: Update City of Brass and Gemstone Mine card definitions
design:
## Goal
Update City of Brass and Gemstone Mine to use data-driven :card/triggers, :card/abilities, and :card/etb-effects fields.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## File Path
src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

## Implementation

### 1. Update City of Brass

Current definition (text-only):
```clojure
(def city-of-brass
  {:card/id :city-of-brass
   :card/name "City of Brass"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Whenever City of Brass becomes tapped, it deals 1 damage to you. {T}: Add one mana of any color."})
```

New definition (data-driven):
```clojure
(def city-of-brass
  {:card/id :city-of-brass
   :card/name "City of Brass"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Whenever City of Brass becomes tapped, it deals 1 damage to you. {T}: Add one mana of any color."
   
   ;; Trigger: fires when this permanent becomes tapped (ANY tap, not just mana ability)
   :card/triggers [{:trigger/type :becomes-tapped
                    :trigger/effects [{:effect/type :deal-damage
                                       :effect/amount 1
                                       :effect/target :controller}]}]
   
   ;; Mana ability: T: Add one mana of any color
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true}
                     :ability/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana
                                        :effect/mana {:any 1}}]}]})
```

**Key decisions:**
- :effect/target :controller (not :self) - damage goes to player, not land
- :trigger/type :becomes-tapped - matches engine/triggers.cljs implementation
- :ability/type :mana - identifies as mana ability (doesn't use stack)
- :ability/cost {:tap true} - uses costs.cljs :tap multimethod

### 2. Update Gemstone Mine

Current definition (text-only):
```clojure
(def gemstone-mine
  {:card/id :gemstone-mine
   :card/name "Gemstone Mine"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Gemstone Mine enters the battlefield with three mining counters on it. {T}, Remove a mining counter from Gemstone Mine: Add one mana of any color. If there are no mining counters on Gemstone Mine, sacrifice it."})
```

New definition (data-driven):
```clojure
(def gemstone-mine
  {:card/id :gemstone-mine
   :card/name "Gemstone Mine"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Gemstone Mine enters the battlefield with three mining counters on it. {T}, Remove a mining counter from Gemstone Mine: Add one mana of any color. If there are no mining counters on Gemstone Mine, sacrifice it."
   
   ;; ETB effect: add 3 mining counters to this permanent
   :card/etb-effects [{:effect/type :add-counters
                       :effect/counters {:mining 3}
                       :effect/target :self}]
   
   ;; Mana ability: T, remove mining counter: Add one mana of any color
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true
                                    :remove-counter {:mining 1}}
                     :ability/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana
                                        :effect/mana {:any 1}}]}]})
   ;; Note: Sacrifice when 0 counters is handled by state-based action, not card ability
```

**Key decisions:**
- :card/etb-effects is separate from :card/abilities (ETB fires on entry, not activation)
- :effect/target :self for ETB - counters go on the permanent entering
- :remove-counter {:mining 1} in ability cost - uses costs.cljs multimethod
- Sacrifice at 0 counters NOT in card data - handled by SBA system

## Success Criteria
- [ ] City of Brass has :card/triggers field with :becomes-tapped trigger
- [ ] City of Brass has :card/abilities field with :tap cost
- [ ] City of Brass trigger effect uses :deal-damage (not :lose-life)
- [ ] City of Brass trigger target is :controller (player, not permanent)
- [ ] Gemstone Mine has :card/etb-effects with :add-counters
- [ ] Gemstone Mine has :card/abilities with {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}}
- [ ] Gemstone Mine ETB target is :self
- [ ] Both cards still in all-cards vector
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (card definitions backward compatible)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO hardcoded sacrifice logic in Gemstone Mine (use SBA)
- NO damage logic in City of Brass abilities (use trigger)
- NO :effect/target :self for damage (damage goes to controller, not land)
- NO mixing ETB effects in :card/abilities

## Edge Cases Handled
- :effect/target :controller vs :self - damage to player, counters to permanent
- Empty :card/triggers on Gemstone Mine - no triggered abilities
- Empty :card/etb-effects on City of Brass - no ETB effects

title: Integrate data-driven abilities into card definitions and remove hardcoded checks
design:
## Goal

## Subtasks

2. fizzle-91z: Update City of Brass and Gemstone Mine card definitions (~2-3h)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Card definitions use :card/triggers, :card/abilities, :card/etb-effects
- [ ] events/game.cljs has no hardcoded card ID checks

## Execution Order
