title: Create engine/state_based.cljs with registry pattern
design:
## Goal
Create the state-based actions module with registry pattern for extensibility.

## Effort Estimate
4 hours

## File Path
src/main/fizzle/engine/state_based.cljs
src/test/fizzle/engine/state_based_test.cljs

## Implementation

### 1. Create state_based.cljs with registry pattern

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.state-based
  "State-based actions for Fizzle.
   
   SBAs are checked after every game action. Uses registry pattern
   for extensibility - add new SBA types without modifying existing code.
   
   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db"
  (:require
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]))

;; Registry of SBA check functions
;; Each check-fn: (db) -> seq of {:sba/type keyword :sba/target object-id}
(defonce sba-registry (atom {}))

(defn register-sba!
  "Register a state-based action check function.
   
   Arguments:
     sba-type - Keyword identifying the SBA (e.g. :zero-counters)
     check-fn - Function (db) -> seq of SBA events to execute
   
   Returns:
     nil (side effect: updates registry)"
  [sba-type check-fn]
  (swap! sba-registry assoc sba-type check-fn)
  nil)

(defn check-all-sbas
  "Run all registered SBA checks.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
   
   Returns:
     Seq of SBA events: {:sba/type keyword :sba/target object-id}"
  [db]
  (mapcat (fn [[_type check-fn]] (check-fn db))
          @sba-registry))

(defmulti execute-sba
  "Execute a single state-based action.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     sba - Map with :sba/type and :sba/target
   
   Returns:
     New db after SBA executed."
  (fn [_db sba] (:sba/type sba)))

(defmethod execute-sba :default [db _sba] db)

;; === Zero Counters SBA (Gemstone Mine) ===

(defn- check-zero-counters
  "Find permanents that have counter requirements but 0 counters.
   
   Checks :object/counters map - if any counter type has 0 or negative value
   and the card requires counters (has :card/abilities with :remove-counter cost),
   the permanent should be sacrificed."
  [db]
  (let [battlefield-objects (d/q '[:find [(pull ?obj [* {:object/card [*]}]) ...]
                                   :where [?obj :object/zone :battlefield]]
                                 db)]
    (for [obj battlefield-objects
          :let [counters (:object/counters obj)
                card (:object/card obj)
                abilities (:card/abilities card)
                ;; Check if any ability has remove-counter cost
                has-counter-cost? (some #(get-in % [:ability/cost :remove-counter]) abilities)]
          ;; Only sacrifice if: has counter-using ability AND all counters depleted
          :when (and has-counter-cost?
                     counters
                     (every? (fn [[_type amt]] (<= amt 0)) counters))]
      {:sba/type :zero-counters
       :sba/target (:object/id obj)})))

(defmethod execute-sba :zero-counters
  [db sba]
  (let [object-id (:sba/target sba)]
    ;; Move to graveyard (sacrifice)
    (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :graveyard)))

;; Register built-in SBAs
(register-sba! :zero-counters check-zero-counters)

(defn check-and-execute-sbas
  "Check all SBAs and execute any that apply.
   
   Runs checks repeatedly until no more SBAs fire (loop handles cascading SBAs).
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
   
   Returns:
     New db after all SBAs executed."
  [db]
  (loop [db' db]
    (let [sbas (check-all-sbas db')]
      (if (empty? sbas)
        db'
        (recur (reduce execute-sba db' sbas))))))
```

### 2. Create tests

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.state-based-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer [deftest testing is]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.engine.state-based :as sba]))

(deftest test-register-sba
  (testing "can register custom SBA check"
    (let [called (atom false)
          check-fn (fn [_db] (reset! called true) [])]
      (sba/register-sba! :test-sba check-fn)
      (sba/check-all-sbas {})
      (is @called "Custom SBA check should be called"))))

(deftest test-check-all-sbas-empty
  (testing "returns empty when no SBAs apply"
    ;; Create db with permanent that has counters
    (let [db (d/db-with (d/empty-db)
               [{:object/id (random-uuid)
                 :object/zone :battlefield
                 :object/counters {:mining 3}
                 :object/card {:card/abilities [{:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}]}}])
          sbas (sba/check-all-sbas db)]
      (is (empty? sbas) "No SBAs when counters > 0"))))

(deftest test-zero-counters-detected
  (testing "finds permanents with 0 counters that require counters"
    (let [obj-id (random-uuid)
          db (d/db-with (d/empty-db)
               [{:object/id obj-id
                 :object/zone :battlefield
                 :object/counters {:mining 0}
                 :object/card {:card/abilities [{:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}]}}])
          sbas (sba/check-all-sbas db)]
      (is (= 1 (count sbas)))
      (is (= :zero-counters (:sba/type (first sbas))))
      (is (= obj-id (:sba/target (first sbas)))))))

(deftest test-zero-counters-sacrifice
  (testing "executes sacrifice for zero-counter permanent"
    (let [obj-id (random-uuid)
          db (d/db-with (d/empty-db)
               [{:object/id obj-id
                 :object/zone :battlefield
                 :object/counters {:mining 0}
                 :object/card {:card/abilities [{:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}]}}])
          db-after (sba/check-and-execute-sbas db)
          obj-after (d/q '[:find ?zone .
                           :in $ ?oid
                           :where [?e :object/id ?oid]
                                  [?e :object/zone ?zone]]
                         db-after obj-id)]
      (is (= :graveyard obj-after) "Object should be in graveyard after sacrifice"))))

(deftest test-non-zero-counters-ignored
  (testing "permanents with counters > 0 not sacrificed"
    (let [obj-id (random-uuid)
          db (d/db-with (d/empty-db)
               [{:object/id obj-id
                 :object/zone :battlefield
                 :object/counters {:mining 1}
                 :object/card {:card/abilities [{:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}]}}])
          db-after (sba/check-and-execute-sbas db)
          obj-zone (d/q '[:find ?zone .
                          :in $ ?oid
                          :where [?e :object/id ?oid]
                                 [?e :object/zone ?zone]]
                        db-after obj-id)]
      (is (= :battlefield obj-zone) "Object should still be on battlefield"))))
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/engine/state_based.cljs exists
- [ ] register-sba! function works (verified by test)
- [ ] check-all-sbas returns seq of SBA events
- [ ] execute-sba multimethod dispatches on :sba/type
- [ ] :zero-counters SBA detects permanents with 0 counters
- [ ] :zero-counters SBA moves permanent to graveyard
- [ ] check-and-execute-sbas loops until no more SBAs
- [ ] 5 tests in state_based_test.cljs all pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO card ID checks (check :card/abilities data, not :card/id)
- NO hardcoded Gemstone Mine references
- NO mutable state outside sba-registry atom
- NO blocking/looping without termination condition

## Edge Cases Handled
- Empty counters map: Treated as 0 for all counter types
- Negative counters: Treated same as 0 (sacrifice)
- No counter-using abilities: Not sacrificed even if counters exist
- Cascading SBAs: Loop handles multiple SBAs firing

title: Refactor activate-mana-ability to use generic engine
design:
## Goal
Remove hardcoded card ID checks from activate-mana-ability and use the generic engine modules (abilities.cljs, triggers.cljs, state_based.cljs).

## Effort Estimate
4 hours

## File Path
src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

## Current Implementation (lines 355-394)

```clojure
(defn activate-mana-ability
  [db player-id object-id mana-color]
  (let [obj (queries/get-object db object-id)
        player-eid (queries/get-player-eid db player-id)]
    (if (and obj
             player-eid
             (= (:object/zone obj) :battlefield)
             (= (:db/id (:object/controller obj)) player-eid)
             (not (:object/tapped obj)))
      (let [card (:object/card obj)
            card-id (:card/id card)  ;; <-- VIOLATION: reading card-id
            db-after-tap (-> db
                             (tap-permanent object-id)
                             (mana/add-mana player-id {mana-color 1}))]
        ;; City of Brass deals 1 damage to controller when tapped
        (if (= card-id :city-of-brass)  ;; <-- VIOLATION: hardcoded card check
          (effects/execute-effect db-after-tap player-id
                                  {:effect/type :lose-life  ;; <-- VIOLATION: should be :deal-damage via trigger
                                   :effect/amount 1
                                   :effect/target player-id})
          db-after-tap))
      db)))
```

## New Implementation

```clojure
(defn activate-mana-ability
  "Activate a mana ability on a land.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     player-id - The player activating the ability
     object-id - The land to tap
     mana-color - The color of mana to produce (:white :blue :black :red :green)
   
   Flow:
     1. Find mana ability from card data
     2. Check can-activate? via abilities module
     3. Pay costs via abilities module (includes tap)
     4. Add chosen mana to pool
     5. Fire matching triggers (e.g., City of Brass :becomes-tapped)
     6. Check state-based actions (e.g., Gemstone Mine sacrifice)
   
   Returns unchanged db if any step fails."
  [db player-id object-id mana-color]
  (let [obj (queries/get-object db object-id)
        player-eid (queries/get-player-eid db player-id)]
    ;; Basic validation
    (if (and obj
             player-eid
             (= (:object/zone obj) :battlefield)
             (= (:db/id (:object/controller obj)) player-eid))
      ;; Find mana ability from card data
      (let [card (:object/card obj)
            abilities (:card/abilities card)
            mana-ability (first (filter #(= (:ability/type %) :mana) abilities))]
        (if (and mana-ability
                 (abilities/can-activate? db object-id mana-ability))
          ;; Execute ability
          (let [db-before-tap db
                ;; Step 1: Pay costs (tap, remove counters, etc.)
                db-after-costs (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id (:ability/cost mana-ability))]
            (when db-after-costs
              (let [;; Step 2: Add mana (mana-color is player's choice for {:any 1})
                    db-after-mana (mana/add-mana db-after-costs player-id {mana-color 1})
                    ;; Step 3: Fire matching triggers (City of Brass damage)
                    db-after-triggers (triggers/fire-matching-triggers db-before-tap db-after-mana)
                    ;; Step 4: Check state-based actions (Gemstone Mine sacrifice)
                    db-after-sbas (state-based/check-and-execute-sbas db-after-triggers)]
                db-after-sbas)))
          db))
      db)))
```

## Changes Required

### 1. Add require for state-based module
```clojure
(ns fizzle.events.game
  (:require
    ...
    [fizzle.engine.abilities :as abilities]  ;; NEW
    [fizzle.engine.state-based :as state-based]  ;; NEW
    ...))
```

### 2. Replace activate-mana-ability function (lines 355-394)
- Remove card-id variable
- Remove (= card-id :city-of-brass) check
- Use abilities/can-activate? for validation
- Use abilities/pay-all-costs for cost payment
- Use triggers/fire-matching-triggers after state change
- Use state-based/check-and-execute-sbas at end

### 3. Update play-land to fire ETB effects
```clojure
(defn play-land
  [db player-id object-id]
  ;; ... existing validation ...
  (if valid?
    (let [db-after-move (-> db
                            (zones/move-to-zone object-id :battlefield)
                            (d/db-with [[:db/add player-eid :player/land-plays-left (dec land-plays)]]))
          ;; NEW: Fire ETB effects
          obj (queries/get-object db-after-move object-id)
          card (:object/card obj)
          etb-effects (:card/etb-effects card)]
      (if (seq etb-effects)
        (reduce (fn [db' effect]
                  (let [;; Resolve :self target to object-id
                        resolved-effect (if (= :self (:effect/target effect))
                                          (assoc effect :effect/target object-id)
                                          effect)]
                    (effects/execute-effect db' player-id resolved-effect)))
                db-after-move
                etb-effects)
        db-after-move))
    db))
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] activate-mana-ability has no references to card-id or :city-of-brass
- [ ] activate-mana-ability has no references to :gemstone-mine
- [ ] `grep -n "city-of-brass\|gemstone-mine" src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs` returns nothing
- [ ] activate-mana-ability uses abilities/can-activate?
- [ ] activate-mana-ability uses abilities/pay-all-costs
- [ ] activate-mana-ability uses triggers/fire-matching-triggers
- [ ] activate-mana-ability uses state-based/check-and-execute-sbas
- [ ] play-land fires :card/etb-effects when land enters
- [ ] City of Brass damage fires via trigger (verified by test)
- [ ] Gemstone Mine counters added on ETB (verified by test)
- [ ] Gemstone Mine counters decremented on activation (verified by test)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO card-id checks in activate-mana-ability
- NO hardcoded card names anywhere in events/game.cljs
- NO bypassing abilities module for cost payment
- NO bypassing triggers module for state change notifications
- NO bypassing state-based module for SBA checks

## Edge Cases Handled
- Object has no mana ability: Return db unchanged
- Ability can't be activated (already tapped, no counters): Return db unchanged
- No ETB effects on card: Skip ETB processing
- No triggers match: fire-matching-triggers returns db unchanged
- No SBAs apply: check-and-execute-sbas returns db unchanged

## Test Updates Needed

### src/test/fizzle/events/tap_land_test.cljs

```clojure
(deftest test-city-of-brass-damage-via-trigger
  (testing "City of Brass damage fires via trigger, not hardcoded"
    ;; Setup: City of Brass on battlefield
    ;; Action: Activate mana ability
    ;; Verify: Player took 1 damage
    ;; Verify: Damage came from trigger resolution, not direct effect
    ))

(deftest test-gemstone-mine-etb-counters
  (testing "Gemstone Mine enters with 3 mining counters"
    ;; Setup: Gemstone Mine in hand
    ;; Action: Play land
    ;; Verify: Object has {:mining 3} counters
    ))

(deftest test-gemstone-mine-counter-cost
  (testing "Gemstone Mine activation removes mining counter"
    ;; Setup: Gemstone Mine on battlefield with 3 counters
    ;; Action: Activate mana ability
    ;; Verify: Object has {:mining 2} counters
    ))

(deftest test-gemstone-mine-sacrifice-at-zero
  (testing "Gemstone Mine sacrificed when counters reach 0"
    ;; Setup: Gemstone Mine on battlefield with 1 counter
    ;; Action: Activate mana ability
    ;; Verify: Object is in graveyard (sacrificed via SBA)
    ))
```

title: Task 1: Create event bus and trigger registry infrastructure
design:
## Goal
Build the foundational event bus and trigger registry that all phase/card triggers will use.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- src/main/fizzle/engine/state-based.cljs:13-42 - Registry pattern with atom
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs:54-94 - Multimethod dispatch pattern
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs:82-113 - resolve-trigger multimethod (reuse for trigger resolution)

### 2. Create src/main/fizzle/engine/events.cljs
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.events
  "Event creation helpers for the event-driven trigger system.
   Events are immutable maps describing game state changes.")

;; Event creation helpers
(defn phase-entered-event [phase turn player-id]
  {:event/type :phase-entered
   :event/phase phase
   :event/turn turn
   :event/player player-id})

(defn permanent-tapped-event [object-id controller-id]
  {:event/type :permanent-tapped
   :event/object-id object-id
   :event/controller controller-id})

(defn zone-change-event [object-id from-zone to-zone]
  {:event/type :zone-change
   :event/object-id object-id
   :event/from-zone from-zone
   :event/to-zone to-zone})
```

### 3. Create src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger-registry.cljs
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.trigger-registry
  "Dynamic trigger registration for event-driven trigger system.
   Triggers register when cards enter active zones, unregister when leaving.")

;; Registry: {trigger-id -> trigger-def}
(defonce registry (atom {}))

(defn register-trigger!
  "Register a trigger. Idempotent - registering same ID overwrites.
   Returns the trigger-id."
  [trigger]
  (let [id (:trigger/id trigger)]
    (swap! registry assoc id trigger)
    id))

(defn unregister-trigger!
  "Remove a trigger by ID. No-op if ID doesn't exist."
  [trigger-id]
  (swap! registry dissoc trigger-id)
  nil)

(defn unregister-by-source!
  "Remove all triggers from a source (when card leaves zone).
   No-op if source has no triggers."
  [source-id]
  (swap! registry (fn [r] (->> r (remove (fn [[_ t]] (= source-id (:trigger/source t)))) (into {}))))
  nil)

(defn get-triggers-for-event
  "Find all registered triggers that match this event type.
   Returns empty seq if no matches."
  [event]
  (->> @registry vals (filter #(= (:event/type event) (:trigger/event-type %)))))

(defn get-all-triggers
  "Return all registered triggers. For debugging/testing."
  []
  (vals @registry))

(defn clear-registry!
  "Remove all triggers. Use in tests and game reset."
  []
  (reset! registry {})
  nil)
```

### 4. Create src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger-dispatch.cljs
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.trigger-dispatch
  "Event dispatch and trigger matching.
   Handles both immediate (turn-based) and stacked (card) triggers."
  (:require [fizzle.engine.trigger-registry :as registry]
            [fizzle.engine.triggers :as triggers]))

(defn matches-filter?
  "Check if event matches trigger's filter criteria.
   :self in filter value matches trigger's source object.
   Returns true if no filter (matches all events of type)."
  [trigger event]
  (let [f (:trigger/filter trigger)]
    (if (and f (seq f))
      (every? (fn [[k v]]
                (cond
                  (= v :self) (= (get event k) (:trigger/source trigger))
                  :else (= (get event k) v)))
              f)
      true)))

(defn dispatch-event
  "Dispatch event to all matching triggers. Returns new db.
   Turn-based actions (uses-stack? false) execute immediately.
   Card triggers (uses-stack? true) go on stack.
   Order: immediate triggers first, then stacked triggers."
  [db event]
  (let [all-triggers (registry/get-triggers-for-event event)
        matching (filter #(matches-filter? % event) all-triggers)
        ;; Group by uses-stack? - note: false = immediate, true = stacked
        {stacked true, immediate false} (group-by #(get % :trigger/uses-stack? true) matching)]
    (as-> db db'
      ;; Execute immediate triggers first (turn-based actions)
      (reduce (fn [d t] (triggers/resolve-trigger d t)) db' (or immediate []))
      ;; Add stacked triggers to stack (card triggers)
      (reduce (fn [d t] (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack d t)) db' (or stacked [])))))
```

### 5. Write tests in src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_registry_test.cljs

### 6. Write tests in src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_dispatch_test.cljs

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events.cljs created with 3 event constructor functions (phase-entered, permanent-tapped, zone-change)
- [ ] trigger-registry.cljs created with register/unregister/query functions
- [ ] trigger-dispatch.cljs created with matches-filter? and dispatch-event
- [ ] 8+ registry tests pass covering:
  - test_register_trigger_adds_to_registry
  - test_register_trigger_idempotent_same_id_overwrites
  - test_unregister_trigger_removes_from_registry
  - test_unregister_nonexistent_trigger_no_op
  - test_unregister_by_source_removes_all_from_source
  - test_unregister_by_source_nonexistent_no_op
  - test_get_triggers_for_event_filters_by_type
  - test_get_triggers_for_event_empty_registry_returns_empty
- [ ] 8+ dispatch tests pass covering:
  - test_matches_filter_no_filter_matches_all
  - test_matches_filter_exact_match
  - test_matches_filter_self_matches_source
  - test_matches_filter_mismatch_returns_false
  - test_dispatch_event_immediate_executes_first
  - test_dispatch_event_stacked_goes_to_stack
  - test_dispatch_event_no_matching_triggers_returns_db_unchanged
  - test_dispatch_event_order_immediate_before_stacked
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Idempotent Registration:**
- Same trigger ID registered twice should overwrite, not duplicate
- This is intentional - allows updating trigger definition

**Graceful No-Ops:**
- Unregistering non-existent trigger: return nil, no error
- Unregistering by non-existent source: return nil, no error
- Get triggers for event with empty registry: return empty seq

**Default uses-stack? Value:**
- If trigger doesn't specify :trigger/uses-stack?, default to true (card triggers use stack)
- Turn-based actions must explicitly set :trigger/uses-stack? false

**Dispatch Order:**
- MUST execute immediate triggers before adding stacked triggers
- This matches MTG rules: turn-based actions happen, THEN triggered abilities go on stack

**Empty/Nil Handling:**
- Empty filter map should match all events of that type
- Nil filter should match all events of that type
- Empty matching list should return db unchanged

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO throwing on missing trigger (graceful no-op)
- ❌ NO mutating event maps (events are immutable)
- ❌ NO direct atom access outside registry module (use functions)
- ❌ NO assuming triggers exist (always handle empty case)
- ❌ NO changing dispatch order (immediate MUST come before stacked)

title: Feature: Add trigger descriptions to stack display
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Triggers MUST have a :trigger/description field in card definitions
- Stack display MUST show format: 'Trigger: [Card Name] [description]'
- Description field is optional - triggers without description fall back to current behavior
- All existing triggers in iggy_pop.cljs MUST have descriptions added

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] City of Brass trigger displays as 'Trigger: City of Brass deals 1 damage to you'
- [ ] Schema includes :trigger/description attribute
- [ ] create-trigger passes description from card trigger definition
- [ ] stack-item-view uses card name + description when available
- [ ] All triggers in iggy_pop.cljs have :trigger/description
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-generating descriptions from effects (explicit descriptions only per user decision)
- ❌ NO changing the 'Trigger:' prefix (user wants to keep it)
- ❌ NO breaking existing triggers without descriptions (must be backwards compatible)

## Approach
Add :trigger/description as an optional field to trigger definitions in card EDN data. Pass this through the trigger creation pipeline. Update stack-item-view to display card name + description when available, falling back to current behavior for triggers without descriptions.

## Architecture
- db/schema.cljs - Add :trigger/description attribute
- engine/triggers.cljs - Copy description in create-trigger
- core.cljs - Update stack-item-view display logic
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Add descriptions to all triggers

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Triggers on the stack show unhelpful text like 'Trigger: becomes-tapped' instead of describing what they actually do (e.g., 'City of Brass deals 1 damage to you').

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Triggers defined with :trigger/type and :trigger/effects, no description field
- db/schema.cljs:64-72 - Trigger schema has :trigger/id, :trigger/type, :trigger/source, :trigger/controller, :trigger/data
- engine/triggers.cljs - create-trigger builds trigger from card definition
- core.cljs:172-186 - stack-item-view generates 'Trigger: ' + type name

**Pattern:**
- Triggers are pure EDN data on cards
- Engine interprets data, doesn't encode card-specific logic
- Adding a field follows existing data-driven pattern

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Explicit description field on card triggers ✓
**What it is:** Add :trigger/description to trigger EDN data, display in stack-item-view

**Pros:**
- Explicit control over wording
- Follows data-over-code principle
- Simple implementation

**Cons:**
- Requires manually writing each description

**Chosen because:** User explicitly chose this over auto-generation

#### 2. Auto-generate from effects ❌
**What it is:** Parse :trigger/effects and generate human-readable text

**Why we looked at this:** Could reduce manual work

**Cons:**
- Complex parsing logic
- Effect combinations hard to describe naturally
- Less control over wording

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User explicitly chose explicit descriptions

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** User changes their mind about wanting auto-generation

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Adding description field to schema
- Passing description through trigger creation
- Updating stack display
- Adding descriptions to existing triggers

**Out of scope:**
- Auto-generating descriptions
- Changing trigger resolution logic
- Adding descriptions to spells on stack

### Open Questions
- None - requirements are clear

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Where should description come from? | Card definition (explicit) | Add :trigger/description to card trigger EDN |
| What display format? | Keep 'Trigger:' prefix | Format: 'Trigger: Card Name description' |

title: Phase 1: Minimal UI for Goldfishing
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- User can see cards in hand with card names displayed
- User can click a card to select it
- User can see current mana pool by color
- User can see current storm count
- User can click Cast button to cast selected spell (pays mana, puts on stack, creates storm trigger)
- User can click Resolve button to resolve top of stack
- User can complete Phase 1 milestone: cast Dark Ritual, Cabal Ritual, Brain Freeze with storm copies
- Uses re-frame event sourcing pattern (events wrap engine functions)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Browser loads without errors (make dev works)
- [ ] Can cast Dark Ritual and see mana pool update to BBB
- [ ] Can cast Cabal Ritual and see mana update
- [ ] Can cast Brain Freeze and see storm copies created on stack
- [ ] Can resolve all stack items
- [ ] Storm count displays correctly after each cast
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO game logic in events (events delegate to engine functions - keeps engine testable)
- ❌ NO game logic in components (components only subscribe and dispatch)
- ❌ NO direct atom manipulation (use re-frame events for all state changes - enables fork/replay)
- ❌ NO CSS frameworks or external styling (inline styles only - add Tailwind later)
- ❌ NO new game engine code (UI consumes existing engine, doesn't extend it)

## Approach
Full re-frame architecture with datascript db nested inside app-db. Events wrap existing engine functions (rules/cast-spell, rules/resolve-spell). Subscriptions use existing db/queries functions. Components are pure Reagent functions that subscribe to state and dispatch events.

This architecture enables Phase 3 fork/replay because every game action is an event in the action log. State is a pure reduction over events.

## Architecture
```
User Click -> re-frame Event -> Engine Function -> New Datascript DB
                                                         |
                                                         v
Component <- Subscription <- db/queries <- app-db[:game/db]
```

Key files:
- core.cljs: Entry point, mounts root component, dispatches ::init-game
- events/game.cljs: ::init-game, ::select-card, ::cast-spell, ::resolve-spell
- subs/game.cljs: ::game-db, ::hand, ::mana-pool, ::storm-count, ::stack, ::can-cast?
- views/app.cljs: Root component with layout
- views/hand.cljs: Card display with selection
- views/mana.cljs: Mana pool display
- views/controls.cljs: Cast/Resolve buttons

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Engine is complete and tested but there's no way to actually play the game. Phase 1 milestone requires demonstrating the storm combo works.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- shadow-cljs.edn configured for browser target, expects fizzle.core/init
- Reagent 1.2.0 already in deps, re-frame missing
- db/queries.cljs has all needed queries (get-hand, get-mana-pool, get-storm-count, get-stack-items)
- engine/rules.cljs has cast-spell and resolve-spell ready to wrap

**External:**
- re-frame standard pattern: events produce new db, subscriptions derive views
- Datascript inside app-db is common pattern for complex state

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Full re-frame setup ✓
**What it is:** Add re-frame, create events/subs/views directories, events wrap engine functions.

**Chosen because:** Matches design doc architecture, enables Phase 3 fork/replay, engine functions already pure and tested.

#### 2. Pure Reagent with atoms ❌
**What it is:** Skip re-frame, use Reagent atoms directly for simpler setup.

**Why abandoned:** Loses event history needed for fork/replay. Would require rework in Phase 3.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Fork/replay feature is cut from roadmap.

#### 3. Lightweight re-frame ❌
**What it is:** Add re-frame but use single ::update-game event with new db directly.

**Why abandoned:** Loses granular event history. Same problem as pure atoms for fork/replay.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Hand display, mana pool, storm count, cast/resolve buttons
- Stack view (to verify storm copies)
- Inline styles only

**Out of scope:**
- Battlefield view (no permanents in Phase 1 cards)
- Graveyard view (Phase 2)
- Action log (Phase 2)
- Styling/polish (future)
- Opponent interaction (goldfish only)

## Design Discovery

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | Answer | Implication |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| UI scope? | Bare minimum | Hand, mana, storm, buttons only |
| Styling? | Inline only | No Tailwind setup, faster to ship |
| Architecture? | Full re-frame | Events wrap engine, enables fork/replay |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### Build Configuration
- shadow-cljs.edn expects fizzle.core/init entry point
- resources/public/index.html missing, needs creation
- re-frame not in deps, needs adding

#### Existing Queries
- queries/get-hand, get-mana-pool, get-storm-count all exist and tested
- queries/get-stack-items exists for stack display
- rules/can-cast? exists for button enable/disable logic
close_reason: Phase 1 UI complete. Goldfishing works with storm combo. All 92 tests pass, lint/fmt clean.

title: Add draw effect (library → hand)
design:
## Goal

Implement the :draw effect type that moves cards from the top of a player's library to their hand. This is the foundational effect needed for opening hands and draw steps.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (follows existing mill effect pattern closely)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code:
- engine/effects.cljs:38-59 - Mill effect pattern (library → graveyard)
- db/queries.cljs:144-159 - get-top-n-library query (already exists)
- engine/zones.cljs:1-25 - move-to-zone function

### 2. Write tests first (TDD):

**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs

| Test Name | What It Catches |
|-----------|-----------------|
| test-draw-single-card | Basic draw broken |
| test-draw-multiple-cards | Loop/iteration bugs |
| test-draw-from-empty-library-sets-loss | Missing loss condition |
| test-draw-partial-when-library-small | Off-by-one, partial handling |
| test-draw-zero-is-noop | Edge case mishandling |
| test-draw-negative-is-noop | Invalid input handling |
| test-draw-nonexistent-player-is-noop | Missing player guard |

**Assertions for each test:**
- Card zone changes from :library to :hand
- Library size decreases by cards drawn
- Hand size increases by cards drawn
- Card order: first drawn = lowest position in library (top of deck)
- Loss condition set when forced to draw from empty library

### 3. Implementation checklist:
- [ ] effects.cljs:60+ - Add (defmethod execute-effect :draw ...) multimethod
- [ ] Effect reads :effect/amount (default 1) for number of cards
- [ ] Effect reads :effect/target (default: player-id) for who draws
- [ ] Check if library has enough cards BEFORE drawing
- [ ] If library empty and amount > 0: set :game/loss-condition on db, return db
- [ ] If library has some cards but < amount: draw all available, then set loss condition
- [ ] Use q/get-top-n-library to get card IDs (returns [] if none)
- [ ] Use zones/move-to-zone for each card (library → hand)
- [ ] Return db (not a map wrapper - keep effect contract consistent)

### 4. Schema addition (if needed):
- Check if :game/loss-condition exists in schema
- If not, add to db/schema.cljs: :game/loss-condition {} ; keyword like :empty-library, :life-zero

## Success Criteria

- [ ] test-draw-single-card passes: draw 1 from 10-card library → hand has +1, library has -1
- [ ] test-draw-multiple-cards passes: draw 3 → hand has +3, library has -3
- [ ] test-draw-from-empty-library-sets-loss passes: draw 1 from 0-card library → :game/loss-condition is :empty-library
- [ ] test-draw-partial-when-library-small passes: draw 5 from 2-card library → draws 2, sets loss condition
- [ ] test-draw-zero-is-noop passes: draw 0 → no state change
- [ ] test-draw-negative-is-noop passes: draw -1 → no state change
- [ ] test-draw-nonexistent-player-is-noop passes: invalid player-id → no state change, no crash
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (make test)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO changing effect return type (effects return db, not {:db ... :failed ...})
- ❌ NO throwing exceptions on empty library (use :game/loss-condition attribute)
- ❌ NO crashing on invalid inputs (guard clauses, return db unchanged)
- ❌ NO modifying db then failing (check preconditions first, atomic operation)
- ❌ NO hardcoding player IDs (use :effect/target or player-id parameter)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Loss Condition Architecture:**
The original task proposed returning {:db ... :draw-failed true}. This would break the effect contract (all effects return db). Instead:
- Add :game/loss-condition attribute to game entity
- Draw effect sets this when library empty
- Separate subscription/event checks for loss condition after each action
- Keeps effect system simple and consistent

**Edge Case: Partial Draw**
When library has 2 cards but asked to draw 5:
- Draw all 2 available cards (player gets them)
- THEN set loss condition (tried to draw from empty)
- Order matters: player sees what they drew before losing

**Edge Case: Draw 0 or Negative**
- Draw 0: no-op, no loss condition (didn't try to draw)
- Draw negative: treat as no-op (defensive)

**Edge Case: Invalid Player**
- If player-id doesn't exist, return db unchanged
- Follow mill effect pattern: (or (q/get-top-n-library ...) [])

**Card Ordering:**
- q/get-top-n-library returns cards sorted by :object/position (lowest = top)
- Cards should enter hand maintaining this order (first drawn = top of deck)
- Hand doesn't have explicit ordering in current schema - cards just exist in :hand zone

**Reference Implementation:**
- Mill effect at effects.cljs:38-59 is the pattern to follow
- Uses reduce over card IDs, calling zones/move-to-zone for each
- Handles empty/partial library gracefully

title: Test Quality Improvement Epic
description:
Improve test effectiveness by removing tautological tests, strengthening weak tests, and adding missing corner case coverage based on test effectiveness analysis.

## Goal
- Remove 4 RED tautological tests that provide false confidence
- Strengthen YELLOW tests with better assertions
- Add missing corner case coverage for P0-P2 modules

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All RED tests removed or replaced with meaningful tests
- [ ] All YELLOW tests strengthened with proper assertions
- [ ] All P0-P1 missing corner cases covered
- [ ] Test suite still passes after changes

## Scope
- cards/careful_study_test.cljs (4 RED tests)
- cards/lotus_petal_test.cljs, led_test.cljs (weak assertions)
- engine/effects, engine/triggers, events modules (missing corner cases)

## Anti-patterns
- Adding tests that only check != nil
- Adding tests that verify mock behavior
- Adding happy-path-only tests
- Leaving tautological tests "for coverage"

## Out of Scope
- Hybrid mana tests (not in Premodern format)
- Phyrexian mana tests (not in Premodern format)

title: Add turn structure with phase advancement
design:
## Goal

Implement turn structure and phase advancement so players can take multiple turns. This is foundational for the 'Player can take multiple turns' success criterion.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-c69 (draw effect) and fizzle-yxj (lose/gain-life effects). The engine can now draw cards and track life. Next we need turn progression so players can:
- Advance through phases (untap, upkeep, draw, main1, combat, main2, end)
- Start new turns (which resets storm count, untaps permanents)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code:
- src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs:54-57 - :game/turn, :game/phase, :game/step already exist
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs - existing re-frame event patterns (see ::init-game, ::cast-spell)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs - has empty-pool function to clear mana

### 2. Write tests first (TDD):

**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/events/turn_test.cljs (new file)

| Test Name | Bug It Catches |
|-----------|----------------|
| test-advance-phase-from-main1-to-combat | Basic phase advancement wrong |
| test-advance-phase-from-combat-to-main2 | Phase sequence ordering wrong |
| test-advance-phase-from-cleanup-stays-cleanup | Auto-advancing to next turn (should require explicit start-turn) |
| test-advance-phase-clears-mana-pool | Mana persisting across phases |
| test-start-turn-increments-turn-number | Turn counter not incrementing |
| test-start-turn-resets-storm-count | Storm count carrying over between turns |
| test-start-turn-resets-land-plays-to-one | Land plays not resetting to 1 |
| test-start-turn-sets-phase-to-untap | Turn not starting at untap phase |
| test-start-turn-clears-mana-pool | Mana persisting from previous turn |

### 3. Implementation checklist:
- [ ] Define phase sequence constant in events/game.cljs:
  ```clojure
  (def phases [:untap :upkeep :draw :main1 :combat :main2 :end :cleanup])
  ```
- [ ] Add helper function to get next phase:
  ```clojure
  (defn next-phase [current-phase]
    (let [idx (.indexOf phases current-phase)]
      (if (= idx (dec (count phases)))
        current-phase  ;; Stay at cleanup, don't auto-advance
        (nth phases (inc idx)))))
  ```
- [ ] Add ::advance-phase event:
  - Get current phase from :game/phase
  - Calculate next phase using next-phase helper
  - Clear mana pool using (mana/empty-pool db player-id)
  - Update :game/phase in datascript
- [ ] Add ::start-turn event:
  - Increment :game/turn
  - Set :game/phase to :untap
  - Reset :player/storm-count to 0 for active player
  - Reset :player/land-plays-left to 1 for active player
  - Clear mana pool using (mana/empty-pool db player-id)
- [ ] Add subscriptions in subs/game.cljs:
  ```clojure
  (rf/reg-sub ::current-phase
    (fn [db] (some-> (:game/db db) queries/get-game-state :game/phase)))
  
  (rf/reg-sub ::current-turn
    (fn [db] (some-> (:game/db db) queries/get-game-state :game/turn)))
  ```

### 4. Reference patterns:
- Follow ::cast-spell pattern for event structure (events/game.cljs:86-95)
- Use d/db-with for datascript updates (see effects.cljs:68-69)
- Get game entity ID pattern (events/game.cljs:70-74)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] test-advance-phase-from-main1-to-combat passes: phase main1 → combat
- [ ] test-advance-phase-from-combat-to-main2 passes: phase combat → main2
- [ ] test-advance-phase-from-cleanup-stays-cleanup passes: cleanup → cleanup (NOT untap)
- [ ] test-advance-phase-clears-mana-pool passes: mana pool all zeros after advance
- [ ] test-start-turn-increments-turn-number passes: turn 1 → turn 2
- [ ] test-start-turn-resets-storm-count passes: storm count → 0
- [ ] test-start-turn-resets-land-plays-to-one passes: land-plays-left → 1
- [ ] test-start-turn-sets-phase-to-untap passes: phase → :untap
- [ ] test-start-turn-clears-mana-pool passes: mana pool all zeros
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (make test)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Cleanup Phase Boundary**
- Advancing from :cleanup MUST NOT auto-start next turn
- User must explicitly call ::start-turn to begin new turn
- Why: Allows UI to prompt "End turn?" or handle end-of-turn effects
- Test: test-advance-phase-from-cleanup-stays-cleanup verifies this

**Edge Case: Mana Pool Clearing**
- MUST clear mana pool at EVERY phase transition (not just end of turn)
- Per modern MTG rules, mana empties at each phase/step transition
- Use existing mana/empty-pool function (check it exists, may need to add)

**Edge Case: Land Plays Reset**
- Default land plays per turn is 1 (not 0, not unlimited)
- MUST reset to 1 at turn start, not at end of previous turn
- Why: Effects like "play an additional land" need to know base value

**Edge Case: Storm Count Scope**
- Storm count ONLY resets at turn start
- Does NOT reset at phase transitions
- Why: Storm triggers care about spells THIS TURN across all phases

**Reference Implementation**
- Study mana/empty-pool or mana/add-mana for pool manipulation pattern
- Study ::cast-spell event for re-frame event structure

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO infinite mana pools (mana empties at phase transitions)
- ❌ NO skipping untap step (turns MUST start at untap)
- ❌ NO auto-advancing from cleanup to next turn (must be explicit)
- ❌ NO hardcoded player-id :player-1 in events (use db to get active player)
- ❌ NO resetting storm count at phase transitions (only at turn start)

title: Data-Driven Ability System
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Card abilities defined as EDN data in :card/abilities field
- Card triggers defined as EDN data in :card/triggers field
- Card ETB effects defined as EDN data in :card/etb-effects field
- Cost payment uses multimethod dispatch on cost type (extensible)
- Ability activation uses generic engine, no card-specific checks in events
- State-based actions use registry pattern (extensible)
- City of Brass uses :card/triggers with :becomes-tapped trigger type
- Gemstone Mine uses :card/abilities with :remove-counter cost and :card/etb-effects
- :deal-damage effect type distinct from :lose-life (damage vs life loss)
- :add-counters effect type for ETB counter initialization
- No card IDs checked in engine code (data over code principle)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] City of Brass defined with :card/triggers and :card/abilities (no hardcoded logic)
- [ ] Gemstone Mine defined with :card/abilities and :card/etb-effects (no hardcoded logic)
- [ ] engine/costs.cljs exists with multimethod for :tap and :remove-counter
- [ ] engine/abilities.cljs exists with generic mana ability activation
- [ ] engine/state_based.cljs exists with registry pattern
- [ ] :becomes-tapped trigger type implemented in engine/triggers.cljs
- [ ] :deal-damage and :add-counters effect types in engine/effects.cljs
- [ ] events/game.cljs activate-mana-ability has NO card ID checks
- [ ] Gemstone Mine sacrifices when counters reach 0 (via state-based action)
- [ ] City of Brass deals damage when tapped (via trigger, not ability effect)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] New tests cover ability/trigger/cost/SBA systems
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO card ID checks in engine code (violates data-over-code: engine interprets data generically)
- ❌ NO hardcoded cost handling in cond-> blocks (use multimethod dispatch for extensibility)
- ❌ NO hardcoded SBA checks in single function (use registry for extensibility)
- ❌ NO conflating damage with life loss (MTG rules: damage can be prevented, life loss cannot)
- ❌ NO ability effects for tap triggers (City of Brass damage is trigger, not mana ability effect)
- ❌ NO modifying existing methods to add new cost types (add new multimethod instead)

## Approach

Extend the existing multimethod pattern (used for effects and triggers) to abilities and costs. Create three new engine modules: costs.cljs for cost payment dispatch, abilities.cljs for generic ability activation, state_based.cljs for SBA registry. Update card definitions to use pure data, refactor events/game.cljs to read card data and delegate to engine.

## Architecture

**New Modules:**
- engine/costs.cljs - Multimethod dispatch on cost type (:tap, :remove-counter)
- engine/abilities.cljs - Generic ability activation, can-activate?, pay-all-costs
- engine/state_based.cljs - Registry of SBA check functions

**Updated Modules:**
- engine/effects.cljs - Add :deal-damage, :add-counters effect types
- engine/triggers.cljs - Add :becomes-tapped trigger type
- db/schema.cljs - Add :card/etb-effects, :card/triggers fields
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Update City of Brass, Gemstone Mine definitions
- events/game.cljs - Refactor activate-mana-ability to use generic engine

**Data Flow:**
1. Player activates mana ability on land
2. events/game.cljs reads :card/abilities from card data
3. engine/abilities.cljs checks can-activate? using engine/costs.cljs
4. engine/costs.cljs pays each cost via multimethod dispatch
5. Mana added to pool
6. engine/triggers.cljs fires :becomes-tapped triggers
7. engine/state_based.cljs checks all registered SBAs

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Mana abilities currently hardcode card-specific logic in events/game.cljs (line 382: checking card-id for City of Brass). This violates the 'data over code' principle. Every new land requires code changes instead of just data.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- engine/effects.cljs:13-154 - Multimethod pattern for effects, works well
- engine/triggers.cljs:53-126 - Same pattern for triggers
- db/schema.cljs:25 - :card/abilities field exists but unused
- events/game.cljs:382 - Hardcoded City of Brass check (architecture violation)

**MTG Rules:**
- Damage vs life loss are distinct (damage preventable, life loss not)
- City of Brass trigger fires on ANY tap, not just mana ability
- State-based actions checked after any game action

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Multimethod Pattern for Costs and Abilities ✓

**What it is:** Apply same multimethod dispatch pattern used for effects/triggers to costs and abilities. Registry pattern for state-based actions.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed engine/effects.cljs - multimethod pattern extensible, tested
- Pattern allows adding new types without modifying existing code

**Pros:**
- Consistent with existing architecture
- Extensible without code modification
- Each cost/SBA type independently testable

**Cons:**
- More files/indirection than hardcoded approach

**Chosen because:** Matches existing patterns, truly extensible, testable in isolation.

#### 2. Conditional Cost Handling ❌

**What it is:** Use cond-> in pay-costs function to handle each cost type.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler initial implementation.

**Investigation:**
- Would require modifying pay-costs for every new cost type
- Same problem as current hardcoded approach

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Not extensible - adding :pay-life, :sacrifice, :discard would require modifying existing code.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We decide extensibility doesn't matter (it does for MTG).

#### 3. Keep Mana Abilities Special ❌

**What it is:** Only data-drive non-mana abilities, keep mana abilities as event handlers.

**Why we looked at this:** Less refactoring effort.

**Investigation:**
- Every new land still requires code changes
- Violates stated architecture principle

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Violates 'data over code' principle explicitly stated in CLAUDE.md.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We abandon the data-over-code architecture entirely.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Mana abilities with :tap and :remove-counter costs
- :becomes-tapped trigger type
- ETB effects for counter initialization
- State-based action for 0-counter sacrifice
- City of Brass and Gemstone Mine refactored

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Non-mana activated abilities (no cards need them yet)
- Other trigger types (no cards need them yet)
- Other cost types beyond :tap/:remove-counter (no cards need them yet)
- Damage prevention mechanics (Phase 2+)

### Open Questions
- Should triggers use the stack? (default: no for mana ability triggers, matches MTG)
- SBA check frequency? (default: after every game action that modifies state)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Tap cost explicit or implicit? | Explicit in ability data | {:ability/cost {:tap true}} |
| Mana color choice | Player chooses at activation | {:ability/mana {:any 1}}, color passed as param |
| ETB effects location | Separate :card/etb-effects field | Not part of :card/abilities |
| Counter costs | Generic cost system | {:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}} |
| Sacrifice timing | State-based action | Engine checks after resolution, not ability-specific |
| City of Brass damage | Triggered ability, not mana ability effect | Separate :card/triggers field |
| Damage vs life loss | Distinct effect types | :deal-damage preventable, :lose-life not |
| Extensibility pattern | Multimethods for costs, registry for SBAs | No cond-> blocks that need modification |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Effect System Pattern
**Question explored:** How does current effect system work?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/effects.cljs:13-154 - multimethod implementation
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - effect data structures

**Findings:**
- Multimethod dispatches on :effect/type
- Pure functions: (db, player-id, effect) -> db
- Unknown types return db unchanged (graceful degradation)

**Conclusion:** Apply same pattern to costs and abilities

### Dead-End Paths

#### Ability Effects for Tap Triggers
**Why explored:** Initially modeled City of Brass damage as part of mana ability
**Investigation:** User pointed out card text says 'whenever becomes tapped' - separate trigger

**Why abandoned:** Incorrect MTG rules modeling - damage should fire on ANY tap, not just mana ability

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Feels like a smell to keep adding to methods' → Multimethod pattern solves this
- 'Damage vs life loss distinction' → Added :deal-damage as separate effect type
close_reason: All success criteria met. 207 tests pass. Implementation verified via code review and automated tests. Manual UI validation deferred (UI doesn't support phases/turns yet).

title: Add land cards and play-land mechanics
design:
## Goal
Implement land card definitions (City of Brass, Gemstone Mine) and the play-land mechanics so players can play lands from hand to battlefield, tracked by :player/land-plays-left.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed:
- fizzle-c69: Draw effect (library → hand) works
- fizzle-dbg: Turn structure with phase advancement works (including land-plays reset in start-turn)
- fizzle-yxj: Lose-life and gain-life effects work

Schema already has:
- :player/land-plays-left (reset to 1 each turn in start-turn)
- :game/phase and :game/step fields
- :object/zone for :battlefield

Existing patterns to follow:
- zones.cljs:move-to-zone for zone transitions
- events/game.cljs:start-turn pattern for pure functions wrapped by re-frame events
- turn_test.cljs pattern for helper functions and test structure
- iggy_pop.cljs card definition format

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Write tests for play-land pure function
File: src/test/fizzle/events/play_land_test.cljs

Create test namespace with helpers similar to turn_test.cljs:
- add-card-to-hand helper (creates object in :hand zone)
- get-object-zone helper (query object's current zone)
- get-land-plays helper (reuse from turn_test.cljs)

Tests to write (follow existing test naming conventions):

```clojure
(deftest test-play-land-moves-to-battlefield
  ;; Bug caught: Zone transition doesn't happen
  ;; Setup: Land in hand, land-plays-left = 1
  ;; Assert: After play-land, object zone is :battlefield)

(deftest test-play-land-decrements-land-plays
  ;; Bug caught: Land plays not tracked
  ;; Setup: land-plays-left = 1
  ;; Assert: After play-land, land-plays-left = 0)

(deftest test-play-land-fails-when-no-plays-left
  ;; Bug caught: Can play unlimited lands
  ;; Setup: land-plays-left = 0
  ;; Assert: Returns unchanged db, card still in hand)

(deftest test-play-land-fails-for-non-land
  ;; Bug caught: Can play any card as land
  ;; Setup: Dark Ritual in hand (type :instant)
  ;; Assert: Returns unchanged db, card still in hand)

(deftest test-play-land-fails-for-card-not-in-hand
  ;; Bug caught: Can play cards from graveyard/library
  ;; Setup: Land in graveyard, not hand
  ;; Assert: Returns unchanged db, card still in graveyard)

(deftest test-play-land-fails-outside-main-phase
  ;; Bug caught: Can play lands during combat
  ;; Setup: Phase is :combat
  ;; Assert: Returns unchanged db, card still in hand)
```

### Step 2: Run tests RED
Command: make test
Expected: All 6 tests fail (play-land function doesn't exist)

### Step 3: Add land card definitions
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

Add cards following existing pattern (no mana abilities yet - just card data):

```clojure
(def city-of-brass
  {:card/id :city-of-brass
   :card/name "City of Brass"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Whenever City of Brass becomes tapped, it deals 1 damage to you. {T}: Add one mana of any color."})

(def gemstone-mine
  {:card/id :gemstone-mine
   :card/name "Gemstone Mine"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Gemstone Mine enters the battlefield with 3 mining counters. {T}, Remove a mining counter: Add one mana of any color. If there are no mining counters on Gemstone Mine, sacrifice it."})

;; Update all-cards vector
(def all-cards
  [dark-ritual brain-freeze cabal-ritual city-of-brass gemstone-mine])
```

### Step 4: Implement play-land pure function
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

Add helper function to check if card is a land:
```clojure
(defn land-card?
  "Check if a card entity has :land in its types."
  [db object-id]
  (let [obj (queries/get-object db object-id)
        card-eid (:object/card obj)
        card (d/pull db '[*] card-eid)]
    (contains? (:card/types card) :land)))
```

Add play-land pure function (following advance-phase pattern):
```clojure
(defn play-land
  "Play a land from hand to battlefield.
   Pure function: (db, player-id, object-id) -> db

   Validation:
   - Player must have land-plays-left > 0
   - Object must be in player's hand
   - Card must be a land type
   - Phase must be :main1 or :main2

   Returns unchanged db if any validation fails."
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [game-state (queries/get-game-state db)
        phase (:game/phase game-state)
        player-eid (queries/get-player-eid db player-id)
        land-plays (d/q '[:find ?plays .
                          :in $ ?pid
                          :where [?e :player/id ?pid]
                          [?e :player/land-plays-left ?plays]]
                        db player-id)
        obj (queries/get-object db object-id)]
    (if (and (pos? land-plays)
             (= (:object/zone obj) :hand)
             (= (:object/owner obj) player-eid)
             (land-card? db object-id)
             (#{:main1 :main2} phase))
      (-> db
          (zones/move-to-zone object-id :battlefield)
          (d/db-with [[:db/add player-eid :player/land-plays-left (dec land-plays)]]))
      db)))
```

Add re-frame event wrapper:
```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::play-land
  (fn [db [_ object-id]]
    (let [game-db (:game/db db)]
      (assoc db :game/db (play-land game-db :player-1 object-id)))))
```

### Step 5: Run tests GREEN
Command: make test
Expected: All 6 play-land tests pass

### Step 6: Run full validation
Command: make validate
Expected: lint + fmt-check + all tests pass

### Step 7: Commit changes
Command: git add -A && git commit -m "Add land cards and play-land mechanics"

## Success Criteria
- [ ] city-of-brass and gemstone-mine defined in cards/iggy_pop.cljs with :types #{:land}
- [ ] play-land pure function in events/game.cljs moves land from :hand to :battlefield
- [ ] play-land decrements :player/land-plays-left by 1
- [ ] play-land returns unchanged db when land-plays-left = 0 (no land left to play)
- [ ] play-land returns unchanged db for non-land cards (prevents exploit)
- [ ] play-land returns unchanged db for cards not in hand (prevents cheating)
- [ ] play-land returns unchanged db outside :main1/:main2 phases (MTG rule)
- [ ] 6+ unit tests pass with meaningful assertions
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO playing lands from zones other than hand
- ❌ NO allowing land plays when land-plays-left = 0
- ❌ NO silently ignoring errors - return unchanged db for invalid plays
- ❌ NO mocking in tests - use real game state
- ❌ NO implementing mana abilities in this task (separate task)
- ❌ NO hardcoding player-id (:player-1) in pure function - accept as parameter
- ❌ NO tautological tests - each test must catch a specific bug

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Object ID doesn't exist**
- queries/get-object returns nil for non-existent object
- play-land should handle gracefully (check obj is not nil)
- Add test: test-play-land-handles-invalid-object-id

**Edge Case: Player doesn't exist**
- queries/get-player-eid returns nil for invalid player
- play-land should check player-eid is not nil
- Test: test-play-land-handles-invalid-player-id

**Phase Restriction (MTG Rules)**
- Lands can only be played during main phases (not untap, draw, combat, etc.)
- MUST check phase before allowing play-land
- Test: test-play-land-fails-outside-main-phase

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow zones.cljs:move-to-zone for zone transition
- Follow events/game.cljs:start-turn for pure function + re-frame event pattern
- Follow turn_test.cljs for test helper patterns

## Notes
- This task does NOT implement mana abilities (tap to produce mana) - next task
- This task does NOT implement land counters (Gemstone Mine) - part of mana abilities task
- This task does NOT implement untap logic - separate concern
- Lands bypass the stack per MTG rules (hand → battlefield directly)

title: Task 1: Create comprehensive card DSL documentation
description: Create docs/card-dsl.md with comprehensive reference for all card definition patterns
design:
## Goal
Create docs/card-dsl.md documenting all effect types, ability types, cost types, trigger types, and conditions with examples from existing cards.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### Step 1: Extract complete type lists from source
Run grep/read to extract ALL dispatched types:
```bash
# Effect types (dispatch on :effect/type)
grep -E ":effect/type" src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs

# Ability types
grep -E ":ability/type" src/main/fizzle/engine/abilities.cljs

# Cost types
grep -E "defmethod pay-cost" src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

# Trigger types
grep -E ":trigger/type" src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs
```
Create master list of ALL types before documenting.

### Step 2: Study source files for each type
For each type found:
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Find usage examples
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Find required/optional fields
- src/main/fizzle/engine/abilities.cljs - Mana vs activated distinction
- src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs - Cost map structure
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs - Trigger fields

### Step 3: Create document structure

```markdown
# Fizzle Card DSL Reference

## Overview
- What this document covers
- How cards are defined (pure EDN data)
- Link to comprehensive MTG rules for mechanics reference

## Base Card Attributes
| Field | Type | Required | Example |
| :card/id | keyword | yes | :dark-ritual |
| :card/name | string | yes | "Dark Ritual" |
[... all fields ...]

## Effect Types
### :add-mana
**Required fields:** :effect/mana
**Optional fields:** :effect/condition
**Example:**
\`\`\`clojure
{:effect/type :add-mana
 :effect/mana {:black 3}}
\`\`\`
**Used by:** Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal

[... repeat for each effect type ...]

## Ability Types
### Mana Abilities (don't use stack)
[...]
### Activated Abilities (use stack)
[...]

## Cost Types
[table format with examples]

## Trigger Types
[table format with examples]

## Conditions
### :threshold
[...]

## Mana Cost Conversion
| Scryfall | Fizzle |
| {B} | {:black 1} |
| {U} | {:blue 1} |
[...]

## Common Gotchas
1. Mana cost is map, not string
2. Colors/types are sets, not keywords
3. CMC is integer
[...]
```

### Step 4: Verify against existing cards
For EACH example in the doc:
- Verify it matches actual card definition in iggy_pop.cljs
- Cross-reference with Scryfall for accuracy
- DO NOT invent examples - only use real cards

### Step 5: Verify completeness
- [ ] Count effect types in doc vs grep output (must match)
- [ ] Count ability types in doc vs source (must match)
- [ ] Count cost types in doc vs source (must match)
- [ ] Count trigger types in doc vs source (must match)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] docs/card-dsl.md exists
- [ ] All effect types documented (verified: count matches grep of effects.cljs)
- [ ] All ability types documented (verified: mana + activated both covered)
- [ ] All cost types documented (verified: count matches pay-cost defmethods)
- [ ] All trigger types documented (verified: count matches triggers.cljs)
- [ ] Common gotchas section with 5+ items
- [ ] Mana cost conversion table complete (all 6 colors + colorless + generic)
- [ ] Every code example verified against actual card in iggy_pop.cljs
- [ ] No example contains incorrect MTG rules (cross-check with Scryfall)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Completeness Verification:**
Before marking complete, run these verification commands:
- Count effect types: grep for defmethod in effects.cljs
- Count documented types in card-dsl.md
- Counts must match - no undocumented types allowed

**Example Accuracy Risk:**
The Polluted Delta incident showed we can hallucinate MTG rules.
For EVERY example:
- Copy exact code from iggy_pop.cljs (don't retype)
- Cross-check card behavior with Scryfall oracle text
- If unsure about a ruling, note it as open question

**Undocumented/Experimental Types:**
If grep finds a type not used by any card:
- Document it anyway (it exists in engine)
- Mark as "No current usage" in notes
- Consult user if unsure whether to include

**Future Maintenance:**
- Add note at top: "Last updated: [date]"
- Add note: "Update this doc when adding new effect/ability/cost/trigger types"

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO invented examples (use only actual cards from iggy_pop.cljs)
- ❌ NO assumed MTG rules (verify with Scryfall oracle text)
- ❌ NO undocumented types (every type in engine must be in docs)
- ❌ NO retyped code examples (copy-paste from source to prevent typos)
- ❌ NO missing data types (every field must specify ClojureScript type: keyword, string, integer, set, map)

title: Task 2: Add Linting and Formatting Commands
design:
## Goal

Add clj-kondo linting and cljstyle formatting to the Makefile. This completes the validation toolchain.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1: Makefile exists with repl, dev, test, clean, help targets. Java detection working.

## Implementation

### 1. Install clj-kondo via npm

```bash
npm install --save-dev clj-kondo
```

Verify: `npx clj-kondo --version` shows version number.

### 2. Install cljstyle via Homebrew

cljstyle has NO npm package. Install via Homebrew cask:

```bash
brew install --cask cljstyle
```

**Post-install (macOS Gatekeeper):** Binary is unsigned, may need:
```bash
xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /usr/local/bin/cljstyle
```

Verify: `cljstyle version` shows version number.

### 3. Add Makefile targets

```makefile
.PHONY: lint fmt-check fmt validate

lint:
	npx clj-kondo --lint src/

fmt-check:
	cljstyle check src/

fmt:
	cljstyle fix src/

validate: lint fmt-check test
```

### 4. Run lint on existing code and fix issues

Run `make lint` on existing code. If errors found:
- Fix legitimate issues in source files
- Add exceptions to .clj-kondo/config.edn ONLY for false positives
- Do NOT suppress real errors

### 5. Run fmt-check on existing code and fix formatting

Run `make fmt-check` on existing code. If formatting issues found:
- Run `make fmt` to auto-fix
- Review changes before committing
- Commit formatting fixes separately from Makefile changes

### 6. Create .clj-kondo/config.edn (if needed for exceptions)

Only create if false positives need suppression:

```clojure
{:linters {:unused-namespace {:level :warning}
           :unused-binding {:level :warning}}}
```

### 7. Update help target

Add new commands to help output:

```makefile
help:
	@echo "Fizzle Development Commands"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make repl      - Start ClojureScript REPL (node)"
	@echo "  make dev       - Start browser dev server"
	@echo "  make test      - Run all tests"
	@echo "  make lint      - Run clj-kondo linter"
	@echo "  make fmt-check - Check code formatting"
	@echo "  make fmt       - Auto-fix code formatting"
	@echo "  make validate  - Run lint + fmt-check + test"
	@echo "  make clean     - Remove build artifacts"
```

### 8. Verify all commands work

- [ ] `make lint` runs clj-kondo, exits 0 (no errors) or non-zero (errors found)
- [ ] `make fmt-check` exits 0 (formatted) or non-zero (needs formatting)
- [ ] `make fmt` modifies files and exits 0
- [ ] `make validate` runs all three in sequence, fails fast on first error
- [ ] `make help` shows all new commands

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `npm ls clj-kondo` shows clj-kondo in devDependencies
- [ ] `npx clj-kondo --version` outputs version
- [ ] `cljstyle version` outputs version (installed via Homebrew)
- [ ] `make lint` exits 0 on clean code (all lint issues fixed)
- [ ] `make fmt-check` exits 0 on formatted code (all formatting fixed)
- [ ] `make fmt` auto-fixes and exits 0
- [ ] `make validate` runs lint, fmt-check, test in sequence
- [ ] `make validate` fails immediately if lint fails (doesn't continue to fmt-check)
- [ ] `make help` lists all commands including lint, fmt-check, fmt, validate
- [ ] All 40 existing tests still pass
- [ ] Changes committed (Makefile changes + any code fixes in separate commits)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Existing Code Has Lint Errors**
- EXPECT this to happen - new linter on existing code
- Fix errors in code, don't suppress legitimate warnings
- May need to fix unused requires, shadowed vars, etc.
- Commit lint fixes separately from Makefile changes

**Edge Case: Existing Code Has Formatting Issues**
- EXPECT this to happen - new formatter on existing code
- Run `make fmt` to auto-fix all formatting
- Review diff before committing
- Commit formatting fixes separately from Makefile changes

**Edge Case: cljstyle Not Installed**
- cljstyle is NOT available via npm
- Makefile will fail with clear error if cljstyle not found
- Document Homebrew installation in error message or README
- Fallback: Could check for cljstyle and skip if not found, but this defeats purpose

**Edge Case: macOS Gatekeeper Blocks cljstyle**
- Binary is unsigned, macOS may block execution
- Solution: `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /usr/local/bin/cljstyle`
- Document in README or show in error message

**Portability**
- clj-kondo: npx works cross-platform (npm is prerequisite anyway)
- cljstyle: Homebrew is macOS-only; Linux users download binary manually
- Windows: Out of scope (WSL recommended)

**validate Target Behavior**
- MUST fail fast: if lint fails, don't run fmt-check or test
- Use `&&` chaining in Makefile: `validate: ; make lint && make fmt-check && make test`
- Alternative: Keep as dependencies but this runs all even on failure

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO suppressing real lint errors (fix the code, not the linter)
- ❌ NO auto-formatting on validate (fmt-check only; fmt is explicit action)
- ❌ NO hard-coded paths for tools (npx for clj-kondo, PATH for cljstyle)
- ❌ NO skipping lint/format steps even if tools missing (fail clearly)
- ❌ NO mixing Makefile changes with code fixes in same commit

title: Task 2: Update stack-item-view to display card name + description
design:
## Goal
Update the stack-item-view component to display triggers with format: 'Trigger: [Card Name] [description]'

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-032: Added :trigger/description to schema and create-trigger. Description now flows from card definition to trigger on stack.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- core.cljs:172-186 - stack-item-view currently shows 'Trigger: ' + type name
- subs/game.cljs:36-49 - ::stack subscription combines triggers and spells
- db/queries.cljs:130-139 - get-stack-items returns triggers with all attributes

### 2. Data Flow Understanding
Current: Trigger has :trigger/source (object ID) and :trigger/description (string)
Need: Card name from the source object's card

Path: :trigger/source → look up object → :object/card → :card/name

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] db/queries.cljs - Update get-stack-items to join source object and get card name:
      ```clojure
      ;; Add helper function
      (defn get-trigger-source-card-name [db trigger]
        (when-let [source-id (:trigger/source trigger)]
          (when-let [obj (get-object db source-id)]
            (get-in obj [:object/card :card/name]))))
      ```
- [ ] subs/game.cljs - Update ::stack subscription to enrich triggers with :trigger/card-name:
      ```clojure
      (let [triggers (queries/get-stack-items game-db)
            enriched-triggers (mapv (fn [t]
                                      (assoc t :trigger/card-name
                                             (queries/get-trigger-source-card-name game-db t)))
                                    triggers)
            ...]
      ```
- [ ] core.cljs:172-186 - Update stack-item-view to display new format:
      ```clojure
      (if (:trigger/type item)
        (if-let [desc (:trigger/description item)]
          (str "Trigger: " (:trigger/card-name item) " " desc)
          (str "Trigger: " (name (:trigger/type item))))
        label)
      ```
- [ ] Manual test: Run dev server, tap City of Brass, verify stack display
- [ ] Run `make test` - all tests passing
- [ ] Run `make validate` - lint/format pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Triggers with description show 'Trigger: City of Brass deals 1 damage to you'
- [ ] Triggers without description show 'Trigger: becomes-tapped' (backwards compatible)
- [ ] Source object in graveyard still shows card name (handles moved objects)
- [ ] All tests passing (`make test`)
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Data Access Pattern:**
- Subscription enriches triggers with card name (view stays pure)
- get-object handles objects that moved zones (graveyard) - still returns object
- Card name lookup happens once per render, not per item

**Edge Case: Source Object Moved/Destroyed:**
- :trigger/source is an object ID, not a ref
- Object may have moved to graveyard after trigger created
- get-object should still find it (objects persist across zones)
- If object somehow doesn't exist, :trigger/card-name will be nil
- View should handle nil card-name gracefully (fall back to type)

**Edge Case: No Description:**
- Must check for :trigger/description before accessing
- Fall back to current 'Trigger: type' format when nil
- Backwards compatible with existing triggers

**Performance:**
- Enrichment happens in subscription (cached by re-frame)
- Only re-computes when game-db changes
- N lookups for N triggers (acceptable for small stacks)

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO auto-generating descriptions (explicit only)
- ❌ NO changing the 'Trigger:' prefix
- ❌ NO breaking triggers without descriptions
- ❌ NO doing db lookups in view component (keep views pure)
