title: Migrate City of Brass trigger to event system and remove dead code
design:
## Goal

Migrate City of Brass :becomes-tapped trigger from scanning-based system to event-based system, then delete the now-dead scanning code.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study Existing Patterns

**Event system (use this):**
- trigger_registry.cljs:12-23 - register-trigger! pattern
- trigger_registry.cljs:39-54 - unregister-by-source! on zone leave
- trigger_dispatch.cljs:34-57 - dispatch-event flow
- events.cljs:25-37 - permanent-tapped-event exists

**Old system (delete this):**
- triggers.cljs:220-253 - fire-matching-triggers
- triggers.cljs:274-310 - fire-and-resolve-triggers
- triggers.cljs:74-83 - check-trigger :becomes-tapped
- triggers.cljs:106-117 - resolve-trigger :becomes-tapped
- game.cljs:529 - call to fire-matching-triggers

### 2. Register City of Brass Trigger on ETB

In game.cljs play-land or a new registration helper:

\`\`\`clojure
;; When City of Brass enters battlefield, register its trigger
(defn register-card-triggers!
  [object-id controller-id card]
  (doseq [trigger (:card/triggers card)]
    (registry/register-trigger!
      {:trigger/id (random-uuid)
       :trigger/event-type :permanent-tapped
       :trigger/source object-id
       :trigger/controller controller-id
       :trigger/filter {:event/object-id :self}  ; only when THIS permanent taps
       :trigger/uses-stack? true
       :trigger/effects (:trigger/effects trigger)
       :trigger/description (:trigger/description trigger)})))
\`\`\`

### 3. Dispatch Event on Tap

In activate-mana-ability (game.cljs), after tapping:

\`\`\`clojure
;; Replace fire-matching-triggers with:
(dispatch/dispatch-event db (game-events/permanent-tapped-event object-id player-id))
\`\`\`

### 4. Add resolve-trigger :permanent-tapped

In triggers.cljs:

\`\`\`clojure
(defmethod resolve-trigger :permanent-tapped
  [db trigger]
  ;; Same logic as old :becomes-tapped resolver
  (let [controller (:trigger/controller trigger)
        effects-list (:trigger/effects trigger)]
    (reduce (fn [db' effect]
              (let [resolved-effect (if (= :controller (:effect/target effect))
                                      (assoc effect :effect/target controller)
                                      effect)]
                (effects/execute-effect db' controller resolved-effect)))
            db
            effects-list)))
\`\`\`

### 5. Unregister Triggers on Zone Leave

Ensure triggers are unregistered when permanent leaves battlefield:

\`\`\`clojure
;; In zone change handling (or sacrifice/destroy):
(registry/unregister-by-source! object-id)
\`\`\`

### 6. Delete Dead Code

**triggers.cljs - Remove:**
- Lines 58-71: check-trigger multimethod and :default
- Lines 74-83: check-trigger :becomes-tapped
- Lines 106-117: resolve-trigger :becomes-tapped
- Lines 212-253: fire-matching-triggers and get-battlefield-objects helper
- Lines 274-310: fire-and-resolve-triggers

**game.cljs - Remove:**
- Line 529: call to triggers/fire-matching-triggers
- Remove fire-matching-triggers from require if no other usages

### 7. Update Tests

**Delete tests for removed functions:**
- triggers_test.cljs: test-fire-matching-triggers-* (lines 238-340)

**Add tests for new event-based flow (in triggers_test.cljs or new file):**
- test-cob-trigger-registered-on-etb: Verify registry/get-all-triggers includes CoB trigger after ETB
- test-cob-trigger-unregistered-on-leave: Verify registry empty after sacrifice/destroy
- test-cob-tap-dispatches-event: Verify dispatch-event called with :permanent-tapped event type
- test-cob-trigger-deals-damage: Tap CoB, resolve trigger, assert player life reduced by 1
- test-cob-multiple-taps: Tap CoB twice, verify two triggers fire, life reduced by 2

## Success Criteria

- [ ] City of Brass trigger works via dispatch-event (verify: tap CoB, life decreases by 1)
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers function deleted: \`rg "fire-matching-triggers" src/\` returns 0 results
- [ ] fire-and-resolve-triggers function deleted: \`rg "fire-and-resolve-triggers" src/\` returns 0 results
- [ ] check-trigger multimethod deleted: \`rg "defmulti check-trigger" src/\` returns 0 results
- [ ] No references to deleted functions: \`rg "(fire-matching-triggers|fire-and-resolve-triggers|check-trigger)" src/\` returns 0 results
- [ ] 5+ new tests for event-based trigger flow pass
- [ ] Existing City of Brass tests still pass (if any)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Trigger Source Leaves Battlefield**
- If City of Brass is sacrificed/destroyed before trigger resolves, trigger should still resolve
- Trigger has controller-id, can still deal damage even if source gone
- Verify: sacrifice CoB with trigger on stack, damage still dealt

**Edge Case: Multiple Permanents with Same Trigger**
- Two City of Brass on battlefield, tap one
- Only the tapped one's trigger should fire (filter by :event/object-id :self)
- Verify: tap one CoB, only 1 damage dealt

**Edge Case: Rapid Multiple Taps**
- Tap same CoB multiple times (if untapped between)
- Each tap should dispatch event and add trigger
- Verify: untap, tap, untap, tap = 2 triggers = 2 damage

**Unregistration on Zone Leave**
- CRITICAL: Must unregister triggers when permanent leaves battlefield
- If not unregistered, ghost triggers remain in registry
- Verify: sacrifice CoB, registry/get-all-triggers no longer contains its trigger

**Reference Implementation**
- Study how turn-based triggers use dispatch-event in turn_based.cljs
- Follow same pattern for card triggers

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO leaving check-trigger or fire-matching-triggers code (even commented out)
- ❌ NO registering triggers without corresponding unregister on zone leave
- ❌ NO hardcoded object-ids in tests (use dynamic test fixture IDs)
- ❌ NO skipping unregister verification test (ghost triggers cause bugs)
- ❌ NO modifying City of Brass card definition (card data stays same, only engine changes)

title: Manual mana allocation for generic costs
description: Implement MTGO-style mana payment: auto-deduct colored requirements, then let the player click mana in their pool to allocate generic costs. Currently auto-pays generic from largest pool which removes player agency on mana decisions (e.g., saving blue for Brain Freeze while spending black on generic). Phase 1 auto-pay is acceptable stopgap.

title: Add schema fields and new effect types
design:
## Goal
Add foundational schema fields and effect types needed for data-driven ability system.

## Context
First task of epic fizzle-dg4. Establishes data structures before implementing ability/cost/trigger systems.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation Steps

### 1. RED: Write tests for :add-counters effect type
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs (add to existing)

```clojure
(deftest test-add-counters-to-permanent
  (testing "add-counters sets counters on permanent with no existing counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent on battlefield with nil :object/counters
    ;; Action: execute {:effect/type :add-counters :effect/counters {:mining 3}}
    ;; Assert: permanent has {:mining 3} in :object/counters
    ))

(deftest test-add-counters-merges-existing
  (testing "add-counters merges with existing counters (does not overwrite)"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:charge 2} counters
    ;; Action: execute {:effect/type :add-counters :effect/counters {:mining 3}}
    ;; Assert: permanent has {:charge 2 :mining 3}
    ))

(deftest test-add-counters-increments-same-type
  (testing "add-counters increments existing counter of same type"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 1} counters
    ;; Action: execute {:effect/type :add-counters :effect/counters {:mining 2}}
    ;; Assert: permanent has {:mining 3}
    ))
```

### 2. GREEN: Implement :add-counters in engine/effects.cljs

```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect :add-counters [db player-id effect]
  ;; :effect/target - object-id to add counters to (required)
  ;; :effect/counters - map of counter-type to amount {:mining 3}
  ;; Merges counters, incrementing existing types
  )
```

Reference: Follow pattern at engine/effects.cljs:38-59 (:mill effect)

### 3. RED: Write tests for :deal-damage effect type
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs (add to existing)

```clojure
(deftest test-deal-damage-reduces-life
  (testing "deal-damage reduces target player life total"
    ;; Setup: player at 20 life
    ;; Action: execute {:effect/type :deal-damage :effect/amount 3 :effect/target :player-1}
    ;; Assert: player at 17 life
    ))

(deftest test-deal-damage-zero-amount
  (testing "deal-damage with 0 amount does nothing"
    ;; Setup: player at 20 life
    ;; Action: execute {:effect/type :deal-damage :effect/amount 0 :effect/target :player-1}
    ;; Assert: player still at 20 life
    ))

(deftest test-deal-damage-preserves-source
  (testing "deal-damage effect includes source for future damage prevention"
    ;; NOTE: For Phase 1.5, damage behaves like life loss
    ;; Source field exists for future damage prevention implementation
    ;; This test documents the interface, actual prevention is future work
    ))
```

### 4. GREEN: Implement :deal-damage in engine/effects.cljs

```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect :deal-damage [db player-id effect]
  ;; :effect/amount - damage amount (integer)
  ;; :effect/target - player-id receiving damage
  ;; :effect/source - object-id dealing damage (optional, for future prevention)
  ;; For now: identical to :lose-life, but separate for future damage prevention
  )
```

### 5. Update db/schema.cljs
Add two new fields after :card/abilities (line 25):

```clojure
:card/etb-effects  {}  ; EDN vector of effects to execute on entering battlefield
:card/triggers     {}  ; EDN vector of triggered abilities {:trigger/type :becomes-tapped ...}
```

### 6. VERIFY
- Run: make validate (lint + format + tests)
- Confirm: All 146+ existing tests still pass
- Confirm: New tests pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] test-add-counters-to-permanent passes (catches: counters not set)
- [ ] test-add-counters-merges-existing passes (catches: overwrite bug)
- [ ] test-add-counters-increments-same-type passes (catches: replacement vs increment)
- [ ] test-deal-damage-reduces-life passes (catches: effect not implemented)
- [ ] test-deal-damage-zero-amount passes (catches: edge case handling)
- [ ] :card/etb-effects field in schema (verified: field exists in schema map)
- [ ] :card/triggers field in schema (verified: field exists in schema map)
- [ ] make validate passes (all tests, lint, format)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO mutating counters in place (use Datascript transactions)
- ❌ NO skipping nil/empty counter handling (must handle {:object/counters nil})
- ❌ NO conflating :deal-damage with :lose-life implementation (keep separate methods)
- ❌ NO tests that only verify syntax/existence (tests must catch specific bugs)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Nil Counters Map**:
- Permanent may have nil :object/counters (never had counters)
- :add-counters must handle nil → {:mining 3}, not throw
- Test: test-add-counters-to-permanent covers this

**Counter Merge Semantics**:
- Adding {:mining 3} to {:mining 1} should yield {:mining 4}, not {:mining 3}
- Use merge-with + for counter maps
- Test: test-add-counters-increments-same-type covers this

**Damage vs Life Loss Distinction**:
- For Phase 1.5: behave identically (no damage prevention exists)
- Keep as separate effect types for future extensibility
- Document :effect/source field even though not used yet

**Object Target for Counters**:
- :add-counters needs :effect/target to identify which permanent
- For ETB effects, target will be the entering permanent itself
- Validate target exists before adding counters

## Reference Implementation
- Study engine/effects.cljs:38-59 (:mill effect) for multimethod pattern
- Study engine/effects.cljs:113-133 (:lose-life effect) for life modification pattern

title: Task 3: Zone Transitions (move-to-zone)
design:
## Goal

Implement zone transitions: moving game objects between zones (hand, stack, graveyard, battlefield, library, exile). This is foundational for cast-spell and resolve-spell.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed Task 2: Mana system (add, can-pay?, pay-mana).
Discovery: Datascript d/db-with returns new immutable db. Queries use pull for entity data with references.

## Implementation

### 1. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/engine/zones_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.engine.zones-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]))

;; === move-to-zone tests ===

(deftest move-to-zone-hand-to-stack-test
  (testing "move-to-zone moves object from hand to stack"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :stack)]
      (is (= :stack (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (empty? (q/get-hand db' :player-1)))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :stack)))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-stack-to-graveyard-test
  (testing "move-to-zone moves object from stack to graveyard"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (-> db
                  (zones/move-to-zone object-id :stack)
                  (zones/move-to-zone object-id :graveyard))]
      (is (= :graveyard (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (empty? (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :stack)))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :graveyard)))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-preserves-other-attributes-test
  (testing "move-to-zone only changes zone, not owner/controller/card"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          original-obj (q/get-object db object-id)
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :stack)
          moved-obj (q/get-object db' object-id)]
      (is (= (:object/owner original-obj) (:object/owner moved-obj)))
      (is (= (:object/controller original-obj) (:object/controller moved-obj)))
      (is (= (:object/card original-obj) (:object/card moved-obj))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-same-zone-no-op-test
  (testing "move-to-zone to same zone is a no-op"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :hand)]
      (is (= :hand (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-hand db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-battlefield-test
  (testing "move-to-zone can move to battlefield"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :battlefield)]
      (is (= :battlefield (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :battlefield)))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-exile-test
  (testing "move-to-zone can move to exile"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :exile)]
      (is (= :exile (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :exile)))))))
```

### 2. Implementation checklist

- [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs - Zone transition operations
  - (move-to-zone db object-id new-zone) -> db with object in new zone
- [ ] Create src/test/fizzle/engine/zones_test.cljs - Unit tests (6 tests)
- [ ] Run tests RED (namespace not found)
- [ ] Implement move-to-zone function
- [ ] Run tests GREEN (all 6 pass)
- [ ] Commit changes

### 3. Key implementation details

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.zones
  "Zone transition operations for Fizzle.

   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db"
  (:require [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]))

(defn move-to-zone
  "Move a game object to a new zone. Pure function: (db, args) -> db

   Valid zones: :hand, :library, :graveyard, :stack, :battlefield, :exile
   Moving to the same zone is a no-op.
   
   IMPORTANT: Caller must ensure object-id exists. If object doesn't exist,
   query returns nil and d/db-with will fail."
  [db object-id new-zone]
  (let [current-zone (:object/zone (q/get-object db object-id))]
    (if (= current-zone new-zone)
      db
      (let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e .
                           :in $ ?oid
                           :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
                         db object-id)]
        (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/zone new-zone]])))))
```

### 4. Verification

```bash
export PATH="/usr/local/opt/openjdk/bin:$PATH"
npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] move-to-zone moves object from hand to stack
- [ ] move-to-zone moves object from stack to graveyard
- [ ] move-to-zone preserves owner/controller/card references
- [ ] move-to-zone to same zone is no-op
- [ ] move-to-zone works for all zones (battlefield, exile)
- [ ] All 6 zone transition tests pass
- [ ] Verification command passes: `npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js`

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Nonexistent Object**
- If object-id doesn't exist, q/get-object returns nil
- Accessing `:object/zone` on nil returns nil
- Query for entity ID returns nil, d/db-with fails
- This is acceptable: invalid object-id is a programming error
- Caller (rules.cljs) must ensure object exists before calling

**Edge Case: Invalid Zone Keyword**
- No validation of zone keyword (could pass :invalid-zone)
- Datascript will accept any keyword value
- Queries for that zone will return empty results
- Rules layer should only pass valid zones; engine trusts caller

**Performance Note**
- q/get-object does a full pull with card reference
- For zone change, we only need current zone
- Could optimize with direct query if needed later
- Not a concern for MVP (single-card tests)

**Reference: Zone List**
Valid zones per design doc: :hand, :library, :graveyard, :stack, :battlefield, :exile

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO zone-specific side effects (purity: zone change is just a db update)
- ❌ NO validation of legal zone transitions (simplicity: engine trusts caller; rules layer validates)
- ❌ NO automatic triggers (scope: triggers are later; zone change is mechanical)
- ❌ NO defensive nil checks (trust caller: invalid object-id is programming error)

title: Add :becomes-tapped trigger type to engine/triggers.cljs
design:
## Goal
Implement the :becomes-tapped trigger type so that cards like City of Brass can trigger abilities when they become tapped.

## Context
Completed fizzle-31d (costs.cljs) and fizzle-4zd (abilities.cljs). Now need trigger infrastructure so City of Brass can deal damage whenever it becomes tapped.

**Key Insight:** The existing trigger system has resolve-trigger multimethod but NO mechanism to detect trigger conditions. We need:
1. A way to check if a trigger condition is met (check-trigger multimethod)
2. A way to fire triggers based on game state changes

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Implementation Steps

### 1. RED: Write tests for check-trigger and :becomes-tapped
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/triggers_test.cljs

Add 8 tests:

```clojure
;; === check-trigger tests ===

(deftest test-check-trigger-becomes-tapped-matches
  (testing "check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns true when object transitions untapped->tapped"
    ;; Setup: old-db with untapped permanent, new-db with same permanent tapped
    ;; Trigger: {:trigger/type :becomes-tapped :trigger/source object-id}
    ;; Assert: (triggers/check-trigger old-db new-db trigger) => true
    ;; Catches: Not detecting state transition
    ))

(deftest test-check-trigger-becomes-tapped-no-change
  (testing "check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns false when object was already tapped"
    ;; Setup: old-db with tapped permanent, new-db with same permanent still tapped
    ;; Assert: (triggers/check-trigger old-db new-db trigger) => false
    ;; Catches: Firing on every tapped state, not just transition
    ))

(deftest test-check-trigger-becomes-tapped-untapped-still
  (testing "check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns false when object remains untapped"
    ;; Setup: old-db with untapped permanent, new-db with same permanent still untapped
    ;; Assert: (triggers/check-trigger old-db new-db trigger) => false
    ;; Catches: Firing when no tap occurred
    ))

(deftest test-check-trigger-becomes-tapped-missing-object
  (testing "check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns false when object doesn't exist"
    ;; Setup: old-db with no object, new-db with no object
    ;; Assert: (triggers/check-trigger old-db new-db trigger) => false (not crash)
    ;; Catches: NPE on missing object
    ))

;; === fire-matching-triggers tests ===

(deftest test-fire-matching-triggers-creates-trigger-on-stack
  (testing "fire-matching-triggers adds trigger to stack when condition matches"
    ;; Setup: permanent with :card/triggers [{:trigger/type :becomes-tapped ...}]
    ;; old-db: untapped, new-db: tapped
    ;; Action: (triggers/fire-matching-triggers old-db new-db)
    ;; Assert: trigger entity exists on stack in returned db
    ;; Catches: Not creating trigger objects
    ))

(deftest test-fire-matching-triggers-no-match-unchanged
  (testing "fire-matching-triggers returns db unchanged when no triggers match"
    ;; Setup: permanent with :card/triggers, but condition not met
    ;; Assert: db unchanged, no new triggers on stack
    ;; Catches: Creating spurious triggers
    ))

(deftest test-fire-matching-triggers-multiple-triggers
  (testing "fire-matching-triggers handles multiple triggers on same permanent"
    ;; Setup: permanent with two :becomes-tapped triggers
    ;; old-db: untapped, new-db: tapped
    ;; Assert: both triggers added to stack
    ;; Catches: Only firing first trigger
    ))

(deftest test-fire-matching-triggers-multiple-permanents
  (testing "fire-matching-triggers checks all permanents with triggers"
    ;; Setup: two permanents each with :becomes-tapped trigger
    ;; old-db: both untapped, new-db: first tapped, second still untapped
    ;; Assert: only first permanent's trigger fires
    ;; Catches: Checking all triggers regardless of condition
    ))
```

### 2. GREEN: Implement check-trigger multimethod

File: src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs

```clojure
(defmulti check-trigger
  "Check if a trigger condition is met.
   
   Arguments:
     old-db - Database state before action
     new-db - Database state after action  
     trigger - Trigger definition from card data
   
   Returns:
     Boolean - true if trigger should fire."
  (fn [_old-db _new-db trigger] (:trigger/type trigger)))

(defmethod check-trigger :default [_old-db _new-db _trigger] false)

(defmethod check-trigger :becomes-tapped
  [old-db new-db trigger]
  (let [source-id (:trigger/source trigger)]
    (if-let [new-obj (q/get-object new-db source-id)]
      (let [old-obj (q/get-object old-db source-id)
            was-tapped (get old-obj :object/tapped false)
            now-tapped (get new-obj :object/tapped false)]
        ;; Trigger fires only on transition: untapped -> tapped
        (and (not was-tapped) now-tapped))
      false)))
```

### 3. GREEN: Implement fire-matching-triggers

```clojure
(defn fire-matching-triggers
  "Check all triggers on battlefield and fire those whose conditions are met.
   
   Arguments:
     old-db - Database state before action
     new-db - Database state after action
   
   Returns:
     New db with matching triggers added to stack."
  [old-db new-db]
  (let [;; Get all permanents on battlefield
        battlefield (q/get-battlefield new-db)
        ;; Collect matching triggers
        matching-triggers
        (for [obj battlefield
              :let [card (q/get-card-data new-db (:object/card obj))
                    triggers (:card/triggers card)]
              trigger triggers
              :let [trigger-with-source (assoc trigger :trigger/source (:object/id obj))]
              :when (check-trigger old-db new-db trigger-with-source)]
          {:trigger trigger-with-source
           :controller (:object/controller obj)})]
    ;; Add all matching triggers to stack
    (reduce (fn [db {:keys [trigger controller]}]
              (let [full-trigger (create-trigger 
                                   (:trigger/type trigger)
                                   (:trigger/source trigger)
                                   controller
                                   {:effects (:trigger/effects trigger)})]
                (add-trigger-to-stack db full-trigger)))
            new-db
            matching-triggers)))
```

### 4. GREEN: Add resolve-trigger for :becomes-tapped

```clojure
(defmethod resolve-trigger :becomes-tapped
  [db trigger]
  (let [controller (:trigger/controller trigger)
        effects (get-in trigger [:trigger/data :effects] [])]
    (reduce (fn [db' effect]
              (effects/execute-effect db' controller effect))
            db
            effects)))
```

### 5. VERIFY
- Run: make validate
- All 8 new tests pass
- All existing 189 tests still pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] check-trigger multimethod exists with :becomes-tapped implementation
- [ ] check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns true ONLY on untapped->tapped transition
- [ ] check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns false for already-tapped or still-untapped
- [ ] check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns false (not crash) for missing object
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers scans battlefield and fires matching triggers
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers handles multiple triggers per permanent
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers handles multiple permanents
- [ ] resolve-trigger :becomes-tapped executes trigger effects
- [ ] 8 new tests pass in triggers_test.cljs
- [ ] All 189+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**State Transition Detection:**
- Must compare old-db to new-db, not just check current state
- :becomes-tapped fires on transition (untapped->tapped), not on being tapped
- Permanents entering battlefield tapped do NOT trigger :becomes-tapped

**Missing Object Handling:**
- If trigger source no longer exists, check-trigger returns false (not crash)
- Defensive coding: use get with default for :object/tapped

**Controller vs Owner:**
- Triggered abilities are controlled by permanent's controller (may differ from owner)
- Use :object/controller when creating trigger on stack

**Effect Execution:**
- Trigger effects use existing effects/execute-effect multimethod
- Unknown effect types are no-op (graceful degradation)
- Empty effects list is valid (trigger fires but does nothing)

**Integration Point:**
- fire-matching-triggers must be called AFTER any game action that might tap permanents
- Caller (events/game.cljs) responsible for calling this at appropriate times
- This task does NOT wire up the caller - that's a separate integration task

**Reference Implementation:**
- Study resolve-trigger :storm in triggers.cljs for effect execution pattern
- Study costs_test.cljs for add-permanent test helper

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO card ID checks in trigger code (data over code)
- ❌ NO hardcoded trigger logic (use multimethod dispatch)
- ❌ NO triggering on every tap check (only state transitions)
- ❌ NO crashing on missing objects (return false gracefully)
- ❌ NO ignoring multiple triggers on same permanent

title: Add effect condition system and sacrifice effect type
design:
## Goal
Add infrastructure for conditional effects and the sacrifice effect type. This enables effects to have an optional :effect/condition that gates execution.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- engine/effects.cljs:13-31 - Current execute-effect multimethod pattern, dispatches on :effect/type
- engine/effects.cljs:162-188 - Example effect implementation (:add-counters) showing target resolution pattern
- events/game.cljs:385-388 - How :self is resolved to object-id before calling execute-effect
- db/queries.cljs - Query patterns for object data

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**File: src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs**

Add new test namespace section for conditional effects:

- test-effect-without-condition-executes-normally
  - Setup: Object with counters, effect with no :effect/condition
  - Execute: Run effect (e.g., :add-mana)
  - Assert: Effect executed (mana added)
  - Bug caught: Regression if condition check breaks normal effects

- test-no-counters-condition-skips-when-counters-positive
  - Setup: Object with {:mining 2} counters
  - Execute: {:effect/type :sacrifice :effect/target obj-id :effect/condition {:condition/type :no-counters :condition/counter-type :mining}}
  - Assert: Object still on battlefield
  - Bug caught: Premature sacrifice when counters remain

- test-no-counters-condition-executes-when-counters-zero
  - Setup: Object with {:mining 0} counters
  - Execute: Same effect as above
  - Assert: Object moved to graveyard
  - Bug caught: Failure to sacrifice when counters depleted

- test-no-counters-condition-executes-when-counter-type-missing
  - Setup: Object with {} (no counters map) or {:charge 1} (different type)
  - Execute: Same effect checking :mining
  - Assert: Object moved to graveyard (nil/missing counts as zero)
  - Bug caught: NPE or incorrect handling of missing counter type

- test-sacrifice-effect-moves-to-graveyard
  - Setup: Object on battlefield
  - Execute: {:effect/type :sacrifice :effect/target obj-id}
  - Assert: Object zone is :graveyard
  - Bug caught: Wrong zone transition

- test-sacrifice-effect-invalid-target-is-noop
  - Setup: No object with given ID
  - Execute: {:effect/type :sacrifice :effect/target (random-uuid)}
  - Assert: db unchanged, no exception
  - Bug caught: NPE on invalid target

- test-unknown-condition-type-skips-effect
  - Setup: Object on battlefield
  - Execute: {:effect/type :sacrifice :effect/target obj-id :effect/condition {:condition/type :unknown-type}}
  - Assert: Object still on battlefield (fail-safe: unknown condition = don't execute)
  - Bug caught: Executing effects with unhandled conditions

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] effects.cljs - Add check-condition multimethod
  ```clojure
  (defmulti check-condition
    "Check if an effect's condition is met. Dispatches on :condition/type.
     Returns true if condition met, false otherwise.
     Unknown condition types return false (fail-safe: don't execute)."
    (fn [_db _player-id _effect condition] (:condition/type condition)))
  
  (defmethod check-condition :default [_db _player-id _effect _condition] false)
  ```

- [ ] effects.cljs - Add :no-counters condition method
  ```clojure
  (defmethod check-condition :no-counters
    [db _player-id effect condition]
    (let [target-id (:effect/target effect)
          counter-type (:condition/counter-type condition)]
      (if-let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]] db target-id)]
        (let [counters (or (d/q '[:find ?c . :in $ ?e :where [?e :object/counters ?c]] db obj-eid) {})
              count-value (get counters counter-type 0)]
          (<= count-value 0))
        ;; Object doesn't exist - condition met (vacuously true, effect will be no-op anyway)
        true)))
  ```

- [ ] effects.cljs - Create execute-effect-impl as renamed current multimethod
  - Rename current execute-effect multimethod to execute-effect-impl
  - Keep all existing defmethods unchanged

- [ ] effects.cljs - Create wrapper execute-effect function
  ```clojure
  (defn execute-effect
    "Execute an effect, checking condition first if present.
     If :effect/condition exists and is not met, returns db unchanged.
     If no condition or condition met, dispatches to execute-effect-impl."
    [db player-id effect]
    (if-let [condition (:effect/condition effect)]
      (if (check-condition db player-id effect condition)
        (execute-effect-impl db player-id effect)
        db)
      (execute-effect-impl db player-id effect)))
  ```

- [ ] effects.cljs - Add :sacrifice effect to execute-effect-impl
  ```clojure
  (defmethod execute-effect-impl :sacrifice
    "Sacrifice a permanent - move it to the graveyard.
     Effect keys:
       :effect/target - Object ID (caller must resolve :self before calling)
     Handles edge cases:
       - Invalid target: no-op (returns db unchanged)"
    [db _player-id effect]
    (let [target-id (:effect/target effect)]
      (if (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]] db target-id)
        (zones/move-to-zone db target-id :graveyard)
        db)))
  ```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] check-condition multimethod exists and dispatches on :condition/type
- [ ] :no-counters condition returns true when counter type is 0, nil, or missing
- [ ] :no-counters condition returns false when counter type > 0
- [ ] Unknown condition types return false (fail-safe)
- [ ] Effects without conditions execute normally (no regression)
- [ ] Effects with conditions only execute when condition met
- [ ] :sacrifice effect moves target to graveyard
- [ ] :sacrifice effect is no-op for invalid target
- [ ] 7+ unit tests pass covering all scenarios above
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (lint, format, test)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: :self Resolution**
- :self MUST be resolved to object-id by caller before execute-effect is called
- Pattern already exists in events/game.cljs:385-388 (play-land ETB effects)
- Condition uses :effect/target, so resolving target also resolves for condition
- Document this requirement in execute-effect docstring

**Edge Case: Missing Counter Type**
- Object may have no :object/counters (nil)
- Object may have counters but not the requested type
- Both cases should be treated as "count is zero" (condition met)
- Use (get counters counter-type 0) with fallback

**Edge Case: Invalid Target**
- Object may not exist (moved to graveyard between cost payment and effect)
- :sacrifice should be no-op for invalid target (matches existing patterns)
- :no-counters condition on invalid target: return true (vacuously true, effect will no-op anyway)

**Edge Case: Unknown Condition Type**
- Multimethod :default should return false (fail-safe)
- If we can't evaluate condition, don't execute effect
- Log warning in future (not blocking for this task)

**Reference Implementation**
- Study engine/effects.cljs:162-188 (:add-counters) for target lookup pattern
- Study events/game.cljs:383-391 for :self resolution pattern

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO checking condition AFTER effect execution (must be before)
- ❌ NO panic/throw on unknown condition type (return false, fail-safe)
- ❌ NO assuming :effect/target is always valid UUID (may be invalid)
- ❌ NO modifying existing execute-effect behavior for effects without conditions
close_reason: Implemented effect condition system with check-condition multimethod, :no-counters condition type, and :sacrifice effect. All tests passing.

title: Feature: Turn Structure UI
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Turn counter displays current turn number
- Phase bar shows all 8 phases (untap, upkeep, draw, main1, combat, main2, end, cleanup)
- Current phase is visually highlighted
- 'Next Phase' button advances to next phase
- Button changes to 'New Turn' when at cleanup phase
- Clicking 'New Turn' starts the next turn (calls start-turn event)
- UI positioned at top of screen, above battlefield

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Turn number displays and updates when new turn starts
- [ ] All 8 phases visible in horizontal strip
- [ ] Current phase visually distinct from others
- [ ] Next Phase button dispatches ::advance-phase event
- [ ] Button text changes to 'New Turn' at cleanup phase
- [ ] New Turn button dispatches ::start-turn event
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-advance of phases (user wants manual control for practice)
- ❌ NO complex animations/transitions (POC - keep simple)
- ❌ NO new subscriptions when existing ones work (use ::current-phase, ::current-turn)
- ❌ NO separate component files (POC - add to core.cljs like other components)

## Approach
Add a turn-bar component to core.cljs following existing patterns (mana-pool-view, life-view). Uses existing subscriptions and events - no engine changes needed.

## Architecture
- src/fizzle/core.cljs - Add turn-bar component, insert at top of app
- Uses: subs/game.cljs ::current-phase, ::current-turn (existing)
- Dispatches: events/game.cljs ::advance-phase, ::start-turn (existing)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Turn/phase structure exists in engine but is invisible to user. Cannot practice turn sequencing or test phase-dependent behavior without UI.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- src/fizzle/subs/game.cljs:73-83 - ::current-phase and ::current-turn subscriptions exist
- src/fizzle/events/game.cljs:173-175 - phases vector defines order
- src/fizzle/events/game.cljs:240-250 - ::advance-phase and ::start-turn events exist
- src/fizzle/core.cljs - UI components follow Reagent + Tailwind pattern

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Display turn counter
- Display phase bar with current phase highlighted
- Next Phase / New Turn button

**Out of scope:**
- Opponent turn support (single player for now)
- Phase-specific styling/theming
- Keyboard shortcuts
close_reason: All success criteria met. Turn-bar component implemented with turn counter, 8-phase strip, current phase highlighting, and Next Phase/New Turn button. Commit a861b40.

title: Task 3: Implement Lion's Eye Diamond (LED)
design:
## Goal
Add Lion's Eye Diamond card that sacrifices itself and discards hand to produce 3 mana of any color.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
Completed Task 2 (Lotus Petal with :sacrifice-self cost). LED requires :discard-hand cost type in addition to :sacrifice-self.
LED timing restriction (activate only as instant) is deferred per epic design.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing cost patterns
- src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs - Already has :sacrifice-self, need to add :discard-hand
- Pattern: multimethod dispatch on cost type key
- :discard-hand cost should move all cards from player's hand to graveyard

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Add tests to: src/test/fizzle/cards/led_test.cljs (new file)

Test cases (7 tests):
```clojure
;; test_led_sacrifice_and_discard_for_black_mana
;; Setup: LED on battlefield, 3 cards in hand
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :black
;; Verify: mana pool has {:black 3}, LED zone is :graveyard, hand is empty, graveyard has 4 objects (3 hand + LED)
;; BUG CAUGHT: Verifies discard moves cards to graveyard AND sacrifice moves LED

;; test_led_sacrifice_and_discard_for_blue_mana
;; Same pattern but :blue - verifies any-color produces chosen color

;; test_led_sacrifice_and_discard_for_white_mana
;; Same pattern but :white

;; test_led_with_empty_hand
;; Setup: LED on battlefield, 0 cards in hand
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :red
;; Verify: still works - mana pool has {:red 3}, LED zone is :graveyard
;; BUG CAUGHT: Empty hand edge case - should not block activation

;; test_led_cannot_activate_from_graveyard
;; Setup: LED in graveyard (simulate post-sacrifice)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability
;; Verify: returns unchanged db, mana pool unchanged
;; BUG CAUGHT: Zone check prevents graveyard activation

;; test_led_cannot_activate_from_hand
;; Setup: LED in hand (not battlefield)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability
;; Verify: returns unchanged db, mana pool unchanged
;; BUG CAUGHT: Must be on battlefield to activate

;; test_led_is_artifact_type
;; Verify card definition has :card/types #{:artifact}
```

### 3. Add :discard-hand cost type
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

Add can-pay? multimethod for :discard-hand:
```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :discard-hand [db object-id _cost]
  ;; Can always discard hand (even if empty)
  ;; Object must exist on battlefield for this to be valid cost
  (if-let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (= :battlefield (d/q '[:find ?z .
                           :in $ ?e
                           :where [?e :object/zone ?z]]
                         db obj-eid))
    false))
```

Add pay-cost multimethod for :discard-hand:
```clojure
(defmethod pay-cost :discard-hand [db object-id cost]
  ;; Move all cards in controller's hand to graveyard
  ;; NOTE: Use controller, not owner (in case of stolen permanents)
  (when (can-pay? db object-id cost)
    (let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)
          controller-eid (d/q '[:find ?c .
                                :in $ ?e
                                :where [?e :object/controller ?c]]
                              db obj-eid)
          ;; Query objects where controller owns AND in hand zone
          hand-objects (d/q '[:find [?e ...]
                              :in $ ?controller
                              :where [?e :object/owner ?controller]
                                     [?e :object/zone :hand]]
                            db controller-eid)]
      (if (seq hand-objects)
        (d/db-with db (mapv (fn [eid] [:db/add eid :object/zone :graveyard])
                            hand-objects))
        db))))  ; Empty hand is valid, just return unchanged
```

### 4. Add LED card definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
;; Lion's Eye Diamond - Explosive mana acceleration
;; {T}, Sacrifice Lion's Eye Diamond, Discard your hand: Add three mana of any one color.
;; Note: Timing restriction "Activate only as an instant" deferred to future task
(def lions-eye-diamond
  {:card/id :lions-eye-diamond
   :card/name "Lion's Eye Diamond"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:artifact}
   :card/text "{T}, Sacrifice Lion's Eye Diamond, Discard your hand: Add three mana of any one color. Activate only as an instant."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true
                                    :sacrifice-self true
                                    :discard-hand true}
                     :ability/produces {:any 3}}]})
```

### 5. Update all-cards vector
Add lions-eye-diamond to all-cards vector.

### 6. Run validation
- make test (expect 7 new tests pass)
- make validate (lint + fmt + tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] can-pay? :discard-hand returns true for battlefield objects (even with empty hand)
- [ ] pay-cost :discard-hand moves all controller's hand cards to graveyard
- [ ] LED definition exists with :card/types #{:artifact}
- [ ] LED mana ability has :tap, :sacrifice-self, and :discard-hand costs
- [ ] LED produces 3 mana of chosen color (any of 5 colors)
- [ ] Works with empty hand (edge case)
- [ ] Cannot activate from graveyard
- [ ] Cannot activate from hand
- [ ] 7 tests in led_test.cljs all pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO implementing LED timing restriction yet (deferred per epic)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in costs.cljs (discard-hand is generic for any card)
- ❌ NO skipping TDD (write failing tests first)
- ❌ NO using :object/owner instead of :object/controller for hand query (control can differ from ownership)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Cost Payment Order**
- abilities/pay-all-costs iterates through cost map entries
- ClojureScript map iteration order is not guaranteed for small maps
- HOWEVER: All three costs (:tap, :sacrifice-self, :discard-hand) are independent
- :tap modifies tapped state, :sacrifice-self changes zone, :discard-hand affects hand
- Order doesn't matter because:
  - :tap only checks object exists + not tapped (zone irrelevant)
  - :sacrifice-self checks battlefield zone
  - :discard-hand checks battlefield zone via can-pay? guard
- If sacrifice happens first, subsequent can-pay? checks will fail BUT pay-all-costs short-circuits on failure
- VERIFIED: pay-all-costs uses reduce with (reduced nil) on failure, safe

**Zone Check After Sacrifice**
- :sacrifice-self moves object to graveyard
- If :sacrifice-self pays first, object no longer on battlefield
- :discard-hand can-pay? checks battlefield zone
- BUT: can-pay? for :discard-hand is called BEFORE pay-cost iteration starts
- abilities/can-activate? checks ALL costs can be paid upfront
- So by the time pay-cost runs, we know all costs were payable initially
- Edge case is handled correctly by existing pattern

**Controller vs Owner for Hand Query**
- LED discards "your hand" = controller's hand
- In single-player practice tool, controller == owner always
- Query uses owner for hand lookup (owner's objects in hand zone)
- This is actually correct for this context
- Note: In multiplayer with control-changing effects, would need adjustment

**Empty Hand Edge Case**
- MTG rules: Discarding 0 cards is legal
- can-pay? returns true even with empty hand
- pay-cost returns db unchanged if no hand objects (correct behavior)

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow same test structure as lotus_petal_test.cljs
- Same helper functions: create-test-db, add-artifact-to-battlefield, add-card-to-zone
