title: Implement tutor effect type and Merchant Scroll
design:
## Goal

Implement the :tutor effect type with library search, criteria filtering, selection modal, and fail-to-find option. Validate by implementing Merchant Scroll card.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Phase 1 complete: City of Brass trigger migrated to event system, dead code removed.

Phase 2 of epic fizzle-wjl: Tutor Infrastructure.

## Implementation

### 1. Study Existing Patterns

**Selection modal (Careful Study pattern):**
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:588-717 - pending-selection system
- src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs - selection state tests
- Key: resolve-spell-with-selection handles selection-requiring effects

**Library access:**
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:142-161 - get-top-n-library pattern
- Library uses :object/position for ordering (0 = top)

**Shuffle implementation needed:**
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:28-30 - uses cljs `shuffle` at init
- Need new `shuffle-library` function in zones.cljs or queries.cljs

### 2. Add shuffle-library Function (NEW)

In src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:

```clojure
(defn shuffle-library
  "Randomize the order of all cards in a player's library.
   Updates :object/position for all library cards with new random positions.
   Returns updated db."
  [db player-id]
  (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)
        library-cards (d/q '[:find ?e
                              :in $ ?owner
                              :where [?e :object/owner ?owner]
                                     [?e :object/zone :library]]
                           db player-eid)
        shuffled-eids (vec (shuffle library-cards))
        position-txs (map-indexed (fn [pos [eid]]
                                    [:db/add eid :object/position pos])
                                  shuffled-eids)]
    (d/db-with db position-txs)))
```

### 3. Add query-library-by-criteria Function

In src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:

```clojure
(defn query-library-by-criteria
  "Return objects in library matching criteria.
   Criteria map supports:
     :card/types    - Set of types, object must have ALL (AND)
     :card/colors   - Set of colors, object must have ANY (OR - blue OR white)
     :card/subtypes - Set of subtypes, object must have ANY (OR)
   Returns vector of objects with card data, or [] if no matches."
  [db player-id criteria]
  (let [player-eid (get-player-eid db player-id)
        library-objs (get-objects-in-zone db player-id :library)]
    (when player-eid
      (filterv
        (fn [obj]
          (let [card (:object/card obj)
                card-types (:card/types card #{})
                card-colors (:card/colors card #{})
                card-subtypes (:card/subtypes card #{})]
            (and
              ;; All required types must be present (AND logic)
              (every? card-types (get criteria :card/types #{}))
              ;; At least one required color (OR logic) - empty criteria = matches all
              (or (empty? (get criteria :card/colors #{}))
                  (some card-colors (get criteria :card/colors #{})))
              ;; At least one required subtype (OR logic)
              (or (empty? (get criteria :card/subtypes #{}))
                  (some card-subtypes (get criteria :card/subtypes #{}))))))
        library-objs))))
```

### 4. Add :tutor Effect Type to effects.cljs

In src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:

```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :tutor
  ;; Search library for a card matching criteria.
  ;; 
  ;; Effect keys:
  ;;   :effect/criteria - Map of card attributes to match (types, colors, subtypes)
  ;;   :effect/target-zone - Zone to move found card to (:hand for Merchant Scroll)
  ;;   :effect/shuffle? - Whether to shuffle after (default true)
  ;;
  ;; This effect returns db UNCHANGED. The actual tutor happens at the
  ;; app-db level via re-frame events when the player confirms their selection.
  ;; The calling code checks for :selection :tutor and sets up pending selection.
  [db _player-id _effect]
  ;; Return unchanged - selection handled at app-db level
  db)
```

### 5. Extend Selection System for Tutor

In src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs, extend resolve-spell-with-selection:

```clojure
;; In resolve-spell-with-selection, detect :tutor effect similar to :discard
;; Key differences from discard selection:
;;   :selection/zone :library (not :hand)
;;   :selection/count 1 (tutors find exactly 1)
;;   :selection/allow-fail-to-find true (always!)
;;   :selection/candidates <matching-object-ids>  ; Precomputed matches

;; Selection state for tutor:
{:selection/zone :library
 :selection/count 1
 :selection/player-id player-id
 :selection/selected #{}
 :selection/spell-id object-id
 :selection/remaining-effects effects-after
 :selection/effect-type :tutor
 :selection/target-zone :hand  ; Where card goes if found
 :selection/allow-fail-to-find true
 :selection/candidates matching-ids}  ; Pre-filtered valid choices
```

### 6. Add confirm-tutor-selection Event

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
 ::confirm-tutor-selection
 (fn [db _]
   (let [selection (:game/pending-selection db)
         selected (:selection/selected selection)  ; #{} or #{object-id}
         spell-id (:selection/spell-id selection)
         target-zone (:selection/target-zone selection)
         player-id (:selection/player-id selection)
         game-db (:game/db db)]
     (if (empty? selected)
       ;; Fail-to-find: just shuffle
       (-> db
           (assoc :game/db (zones/shuffle-library game-db player-id))
           (dissoc :game/pending-selection))
       ;; Found card: move to target zone, then shuffle
       (let [card-id (first selected)
             db-after-move (zones/move-to-zone game-db card-id target-zone)
             db-after-shuffle (zones/shuffle-library db-after-move player-id)]
         (-> db
             (assoc :game/db db-after-shuffle)
             (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))))))
```

### 7. Implement Merchant Scroll Card

In src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:

```clojure
(def merchant-scroll
  {:card/id :merchant-scroll
   :card/name "Merchant Scroll"
   :card/types #{:sorcery}
   :card/mana-cost {:blue 1 :generic 1}
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :tutor
                   :effect/criteria {:card/types #{:instant}
                                     :card/colors #{:blue}}
                   :effect/target-zone :hand
                   :effect/shuffle? true}]
   :card/text "Search your library for a blue instant card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle."})
```

### 8. Add Tests

In src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs:

**Query function tests (what bugs they catch):**
- test-query-library-single-match: Catches regression if criteria matching broken
- test-query-library-multiple-matches: Catches bug where only first match returned
- test-query-library-no-matches: Catches crash on empty result set
- test-query-library-type-and-color: Catches AND/OR logic bugs

**Tutor effect tests:**
- test-tutor-shows-selection-modal: Catches missing UI interaction
- test-tutor-fail-to-find-shuffles: Catches shuffle skip on empty selection
- test-tutor-card-to-hand: Catches zone movement bugs
- test-tutor-shuffles-after-find: Catches shuffle order bugs (must shuffle after move)

**Merchant Scroll integration tests:**
- test-merchant-scroll-finds-blue-instant: Happy path, catches basic integration
- test-merchant-scroll-ignores-non-blue: Catches color filter bug
- test-merchant-scroll-ignores-non-instant: Catches type filter bug
- test-merchant-scroll-fail-to-find-works: Catches fail-to-find path regression
- test-merchant-scroll-with-empty-library: Catches crash on edge case

## Success Criteria

- [ ] query-library-by-criteria function exists in queries.cljs
- [ ] shuffle-library function exists in zones.cljs
- [ ] :tutor effect type defined in effects.cljs
- [ ] Selection modal appears for tutor with matching cards + "Find Nothing" option
- [ ] Selecting card moves it to target zone (hand for Merchant Scroll)
- [ ] Selecting "Find Nothing" shuffles library without moving card
- [ ] Library shuffled after BOTH find and fail-to-find
- [ ] Merchant Scroll card definition in iggy_pop.cljs
- [ ] 12+ tests passing: `make test` shows tutor tests green
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty Library**
- If library is empty when tutor resolves, modal shows only "Find Nothing" option
- Must not crash, must still shuffle (shuffle of empty is no-op)
- Test: test-merchant-scroll-with-empty-library

**Edge Case: No Matching Cards**
- If library has cards but none match criteria, modal shows only "Find Nothing"
- Player must be able to legally fail-to-find (MTG rules)
- Test: test-merchant-scroll-no-blue-instants-in-library

**Edge Case: Exactly One Match**
- If exactly one card matches, still show modal with that card + "Find Nothing"
- NEVER auto-select (player may want to fail-to-find for bluffing)
- Anti-pattern enforces this

**Shuffle Timing**
- CRITICAL: Move card BEFORE shuffle
- If shuffle first: card ends up at random position then moved (wastes entropy)
- Correct order: move to hand → shuffle remaining library
- Test: test-tutor-shuffles-after-find verifies shuffle happens after move

**Selection Count**
- Tutor selects exactly 0 or 1 card (not 2 like Careful Study)
- :selection/count should be 1 but allow 0 for fail-to-find
- Different from discard which requires exact count

**Reference Implementation**
- Study careful_study_test.cljs:275-305 for resolve-spell-with-selection pattern
- Tutor is similar but: zone=library, count=0-1, has candidates filter

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO auto-selecting when only one match (always show modal with fail-to-find)
- ❌ NO omitting fail-to-find option (even when matches exist)
- ❌ NO shuffling before moving card (shuffle AFTER move)
- ❌ NO revealing non-revealed tutors (Merchant Scroll reveals - text says "reveal it")
- ❌ NO skipping shuffle on fail-to-find (always shuffle, even if nothing found)
- ❌ NO crashing on empty library (handle gracefully)
- ❌ NO leaking card order pre-shuffle (don't iterate library in visible order then shuffle)
close_reason: Tutor infrastructure implemented with Merchant Scroll card. Tests passing, make validate passed.

title: Add pay-life cost to Polluted Delta
design:
## Goal
Add the 1 life payment cost to Polluted Delta's activated ability.

## Context
Polluted Delta's Oracle text: "{T}, Pay 1 life, Sacrifice Polluted Delta: Search your library..."
Current implementation is missing the life payment.

## Current State
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

Existing cost types (multimethod dispatch):
- `:tap` - tap the permanent
- `:remove-counter` - remove counters from permanent
- `:sacrifice-self` - sacrifice the permanent  
- `:discard-hand` - discard controller's hand (LED)

`:pay-life` is NOT implemented.

## Implementation

### Step 1: Add can-pay? :pay-life method
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :pay-life [db object-id cost]
  ;; Can pay life if:
  ;; 1. Object exists
  ;; 2. Controller has life >= required amount
  ;; Note: Can pay life even if it would reduce to 0 (MTG rules)
  (if-let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (let [controller-eid (d/q '[:find ?c .
                                :in $ ?e
                                :where [?e :object/controller ?c]]
                              db obj-eid)
          current-life (d/q '[:find ?life .
                              :in $ ?p
                              :where [?p :player/life ?life]]
                            db controller-eid)
          required (:pay-life cost)]
      (>= (or current-life 0) required))
    false))
```

### Step 2: Add pay-cost :pay-life method
```clojure
(defmethod pay-cost :pay-life [db object-id cost]
  ;; Deduct life from controller
  ;; Note: No can-pay? guard - by time pay-cost called, already validated
  (if-let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (let [controller-eid (d/q '[:find ?c .
                                :in $ ?e
                                :where [?e :object/controller ?c]]
                              db obj-eid)
          current-life (d/q '[:find ?life .
                              :in $ ?p
                              :where [?p :player/life ?life]]
                            db controller-eid)
          required (:pay-life cost)
          new-life (- current-life required)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add controller-eid :player/life new-life]]))
    db))
```

### Step 3: Update Polluted Delta card definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
:ability/cost {:tap true
               :sacrifice-self true
               :pay-life 1}  ;; ADD THIS
```

### Step 4: Add tests
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/costs_test.cljs (or new file)

Tests:
1. test-pay-life-can-pay-with-sufficient-life
   - Player has 10 life, cost is 1
   - can-pay? returns true

2. test-pay-life-cannot-pay-with-insufficient-life  
   - Player has 0 life, cost is 1
   - can-pay? returns false

3. test-pay-life-can-pay-exact-life
   - Player has 1 life, cost is 1
   - can-pay? returns true (going to 0 is allowed)

4. test-pay-life-deducts-correctly
   - Player starts at 20 life, pays 1
   - Player ends at 19 life

## Success Criteria
- [ ] can-pay? :pay-life returns true when controller has >= required life
- [ ] can-pay? :pay-life returns false when controller has < required life
- [ ] pay-cost :pay-life deducts life from controller
- [ ] Polluted Delta ability cost includes :pay-life 1
- [ ] Activating Polluted Delta deducts 1 life from player
- [ ] Cannot activate Polluted Delta if player has 0 life
- [ ] Existing tests still pass

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO special-casing Polluted Delta - use generic :pay-life cost type
- ❌ NO checking life > 0 after payment (going to 0 is legal, loss check is separate)

title: Task 2: Implement turn-based actions using event system
design:
## Goal
Wire up the event system to game phase transitions. Implement draw step and untap step as turn-based actions (game-rule triggers) that fire on phase entry events.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1: Event bus and trigger registry infrastructure now exists.
- events.cljs has phase-entered-event helper
- trigger-registry.cljs has registration functions
- trigger-dispatch.cljs handles immediate vs stacked triggers

Key codebase findings:
- advance-phase is at events/game.cljs:188-200 - updates phase and clears mana pool
- start-turn is at events/game.cljs:221-236 - has inline untap via untap-all-permanents
- untap-all-permanents is at events/game.cljs:203-218 - untaps all tapped permanents
- effects.cljs:191-222 has :draw effect that handles empty library (sets :game/loss-condition)
- Turn 1 is NOT stored in DB at init - current turn is in game-state

## Implementation

### 1. Study current phase handling (15 min)
- Read events/game.cljs:188-236 (advance-phase, start-turn, untap-all-permanents)
- Note: start-turn calls untap-all-permanents inline - we will move this to trigger
- Note: advance-phase just changes phase, doesn't fire events yet

### 2. Create turn-based trigger registration function
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/turn_based.cljs (new file)

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.turn-based
  "Turn-based game actions implemented as game-rule triggers."
  (:require
    [fizzle.engine.trigger-registry :as registry]))

(defn register-turn-based-actions!
  "Register all turn-based action triggers.
   Call once at game initialization."
  []
  ;; Draw step - draw a card (skip turn 1)
  (registry/register-trigger!
    {:trigger/id :game-rule-draw
     :trigger/source :game-rule
     :trigger/type :draw-step
     :trigger/event-type :phase-entered
     :trigger/filter {:event/phase :draw}
     :trigger/uses-stack? false
     :trigger/controller :player-1})  ; Active player
  
  ;; Untap step - untap all permanents
  (registry/register-trigger!
    {:trigger/id :game-rule-untap
     :trigger/source :game-rule
     :trigger/type :untap-step
     :trigger/event-type :phase-entered
     :trigger/filter {:event/phase :untap}
     :trigger/uses-stack? false
     :trigger/controller :player-1}))
```

### 3. Implement resolve-trigger methods for turn-based actions
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs (add methods)

```clojure
;; Add at top: require effects, queries
;; (:require [fizzle.engine.effects :as effects]
;;           [fizzle.db.queries :as queries])

(defmethod resolve-trigger :draw-step
  [db trigger]
  (let [game-state (queries/get-game-state db)
        turn (:game/turn game-state)
        active-player (:trigger/controller trigger)]
    ;; Skip draw on turn 1 (MTG rules: play/draw rule)
    (if (and turn (> turn 1))
      (effects/execute-effect db active-player {:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 1})
      db)))

(defmethod resolve-trigger :untap-step
  [db trigger]
  (let [active-player (:trigger/controller trigger)]
    ;; untap-all-permanents already handles empty battlefield gracefully
    (game/untap-all-permanents db active-player)))
```

### 4. Modify advance-phase to produce and dispatch events
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs (modify advance-phase)

```clojure
;; Add requires at top:
;; (:require [fizzle.engine.events :as game-events]
;;           [fizzle.engine.trigger-dispatch :as dispatch])

(defn advance-phase
  "Advance to the next phase, clear mana pool, and dispatch phase-entered event."
  [db player-id]
  (let [game-state (queries/get-game-state db)
        game-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :game/id _]] db)
        current-phase (:game/phase game-state)
        new-phase (next-phase current-phase)
        turn (:game/turn game-state)]
    (-> db
        (mana/empty-pool player-id)
        (d/db-with [[:db/add game-eid :game/phase new-phase]])
        ;; NEW: Dispatch phase-entered event to fire turn-based actions
        (dispatch/dispatch-event (game-events/phase-entered-event new-phase turn player-id)))))
```

### 5. Remove inline untap from start-turn
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs (modify start-turn)

Keep start-turn but remove the untap-all-permanents call since untap now happens via trigger when advancing to :untap phase.

```clojure
(defn start-turn
  "Start a new turn: increment turn counter, set phase to untap,
   reset storm count and land plays to 1, clear mana pool.
   Untap happens via :untap-step trigger when phase changes."
  [db player-id]
  (let [game-state (queries/get-game-state db)
        game-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :game/id _]] db)
        player-eid (queries/get-player-eid db player-id)
        current-turn (or (:game/turn game-state) 0)]
    (-> db
        ;; REMOVED: (untap-all-permanents player-id) - now via trigger
        (mana/empty-pool player-id)
        (d/db-with [[:db/add game-eid :game/turn (inc current-turn)]
                    [:db/add game-eid :game/phase :untap]
                    [:db/add player-eid :player/storm-count 0]
                    [:db/add player-eid :player/land-plays-left 1]])
        ;; Dispatch untap phase event to fire turn-based actions
        (dispatch/dispatch-event (game-events/phase-entered-event :untap (inc current-turn) player-id)))))
```

### 6. Call register-turn-based-actions! at game init
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs (modify init-game-state)

Add call to register turn-based triggers before returning game state.

### 7. Write tests
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/turn_based_test.cljs (new file)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] advance-phase calls dispatch-event with phase-entered event (verify via test)
- [ ] :draw-step trigger fires on draw phase: test_draw_step_fires_on_draw_phase
- [ ] :draw-step skips turn 1: test_draw_step_skips_turn_1
- [ ] :draw-step draws one card turn 2+: test_draw_step_draws_card_turn_2
- [ ] :untap-step trigger fires on untap phase: test_untap_step_fires_on_untap_phase
- [ ] :untap-step untaps all permanents: test_untap_step_untaps_all_permanents
- [ ] :untap-step handles no permanents gracefully: test_untap_step_empty_battlefield
- [ ] Existing tests still pass: make test
- [ ] 8+ new tests for turn-based actions
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty Library Draw**
- effects.cljs:191-222 already handles this
- Sets :game/loss-condition to :empty-library
- :draw-step trigger relies on this - no additional handling needed
- Test: test_draw_step_empty_library_sets_loss_condition

**Edge Case: No Permanents to Untap**
- untap-all-permanents already handles empty result gracefully
- Returns unchanged db if no tapped permanents
- Test: test_untap_step_empty_battlefield

**Edge Case: Turn Counter**
- Turn 1 check: `(> turn 1)` handles both turn 1 and nil turn
- If turn is nil (shouldn't happen), draws nothing (safe)
- Test with explicit turn 1 and turn 2 scenarios

**Edge Case: Multiple Phase Advances**
- Each advance-phase call produces one event
- No risk of double-firing since dispatch is per event
- Test: test_advance_multiple_phases_fires_each_event

**Trigger Registration Timing**
- register-turn-based-actions! called at game init
- Must happen BEFORE first advance-phase call
- Verify: test_triggers_registered_at_init

**Reference: untap-all-permanents**
- Location: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:203-218
- Already handles edge cases (no permanents, nil player)
- Reuse directly in :untap-step trigger

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded phase logic in advance-phase (use registered triggers)
- ❌ NO breaking existing untap behavior (tests must still pass)
- ❌ NO duplicate untap logic (reuse untap-all-permanents)
- ❌ NO calling register-turn-based-actions! multiple times (call once at init)
- ❌ NO skipping turn 1 check for draw (MTG rules requirement)

title: Implement threshold condition and Cabal Ritual
design:
## Goal

Implement the threshold condition system and Cabal Ritual card with conditional effects. Threshold is a common Premodern mechanic that checks if you have 7+ cards in graveyard.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context

Completed:
- Mill effect (fizzle-wkb)
- Storm trigger infrastructure
- Brain Freeze with storm

Epic requires threshold for Cabal Ritual: BBB normally, BBBBB with threshold.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

Reference implementations:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - effect multimethod pattern
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:79-90 - get-objects-in-zone for graveyard counting
- Epic's architecture section describes conditional effects design

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/conditions_test.cljs (new)

Test cases for threshold:
- [ ] test-threshold-false-with-zero-cards: Empty graveyard returns false (catches: incorrect boundary)
- [ ] test-threshold-false-with-six-cards: 6 cards returns false (catches: off-by-one)
- [ ] test-threshold-true-with-seven-cards: 7 cards returns true (catches: boundary handling)
- [ ] test-threshold-true-with-many-cards: 10+ cards returns true (catches: only-exact-7 bug)
- [ ] test-threshold-only-counts-owner-graveyard: Player 1's graveyard doesn't count opponent's cards (catches: wrong player)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs (add to existing)

Test cases for conditional effects:
- [ ] test-cabal-ritual-without-threshold: Resolving Cabal Ritual with <7 graveyard adds BBB
- [ ] test-cabal-ritual-with-threshold: Resolving Cabal Ritual with 7+ graveyard adds BBBBB
- [ ] test-threshold-checked-at-resolution: Milling to 7+ during resolution enables threshold for that effect (catches: check at wrong time)

### 3. Implementation checklist

Create new file src/main/fizzle/engine/conditions.cljs:
- [ ] (defn threshold? [db player-id])
  - Count cards in player's graveyard via get-objects-in-zone
  - Return true if count >= 7, false otherwise

Add to src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs (if not already present):
- [ ] (defn count-graveyard [db player-id])
  - Simple: (count (get-objects-in-zone db player-id :graveyard))
  - Or direct query for efficiency

Add conditional effect handling to src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:
- [ ] Modify resolve-spell to check :card/conditional-effects
- [ ] If card has :card/conditional-effects map, select effects based on conditions
- [ ] Pattern: {:threshold effects-with-threshold, :default effects-without}
- [ ] Call conditions/threshold? to determine which effect set to use

Add to src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:
- [ ] def cabal-ritual
```clojure
(def cabal-ritual
  {:card/id :cabal-ritual
   :card/name "Cabal Ritual"
   :card/cmc 2
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :black 1}
   :card/colors #{:black}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Add {B}{B}{B}. Threshold - Add {B}{B}{B}{B}{B} instead."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 3}}]
   :card/conditional-effects {:threshold [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 5}}]}})
```

### 4. Key design decisions

**Effect selection pattern:**
- Card has :card/effects (default) and :card/conditional-effects (map of condition -> effects)
- At resolution, check conditions and select appropriate effect set
- If threshold? true and :card/conditional-effects has :threshold, use those
- Otherwise use :card/effects

**Threshold check timing:**
- Check at resolution time, not cast time
- Graveyard count can change between cast and resolve (e.g., from other effects)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] threshold? function returns correct boolean for boundary cases
- [ ] Cabal Ritual adds BBB without threshold
- [ ] Cabal Ritual adds BBBBB with threshold (7+ graveyard)
- [ ] Threshold checked at resolution time, not cast time
- [ ] 5 threshold condition tests pass
- [ ] 3 Cabal Ritual tests pass
- [ ] All existing tests still pass: make test
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: make validate

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO condition checks inside effect methods (epic requirement: card-level modes)
- ❌ NO hardcoded Cabal Ritual logic in engine (card is data)
- ❌ NO threshold check at cast time (must be resolution time)
- ❌ NO off-by-one errors (threshold is >= 7, not > 7)
- ❌ NO counting opponent's graveyard for player's threshold

title: Fix Gemstone Mine sacrifice to be part of mana ability
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Gemstone Mine sacrifice must happen as part of the mana ability resolution, not as a state-based effect
- Effect system must support conditional effects via :effect/condition field
- :no-counters condition type must check if specified counter type is zero on target
- Zero-counters SBA must be removed entirely
- Sacrifice effect type must exist to move permanents to graveyard

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Gemstone Mine sacrifices itself when mana ability resolves AND no mining counters remain
- [ ] Gemstone Mine does NOT sacrifice if counters removed by other means (no SBA)
- [ ] Effect conditions are checked before effect execution
- [ ] :sacrifice effect moves target to graveyard
- [ ] All existing tests pass (updated as needed)
- [ ] New tests verify conditional effect behavior
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO state-based effect for counter depletion (rules error: sacrifice is part of ability, not SBA)
- ❌ NO custom effect types per card (maintainability: use generic conditional system)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Gemstone Mine logic in effect executor (extensibility: condition system must be generic)

## Approach
Add effect-level condition support to the effect executor. Effects with :effect/condition are only executed if the condition evaluates to true. Implement :no-counters condition type that checks if a counter type is zero. Update Gemstone Mine card definition to include conditional sacrifice as part of mana ability effects. Remove the zero-counters SBA entirely.

## Architecture
- engine/effects.cljs - Add check-condition multimethod, update execute-effect to check conditions
- engine/conditions.cljs (new) - Condition evaluation logic, or inline in effects.cljs
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Update Gemstone Mine with conditional sacrifice effect
- engine/state_based.cljs - Remove zero-counters SBA code and registration

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Gemstone Mine's sacrifice is implemented as a state-based effect, but the actual card text says 'If there are no mining counters on Gemstone Mine, sacrifice it' as part of the mana ability. This means the sacrifice should only happen when activating the mana ability, not when counters are removed by other means.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:79-100 - Current Gemstone Mine definition uses ETB for counters, mana ability for tap+remove
- engine/state_based.cljs:60-94 - Zero-counters SBA incorrectly sacrifices when counters hit 0
- engine/effects.cljs - Effect executor has no condition checking currently
- engine/costs.cljs:73-103 - Counter removal happens during cost payment

**External:**
- Scryfall rules note: 'the land won't be sacrificed if the last counter is removed through means other than activating the mana ability'

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Effect-level conditions ✓
**What it is:** Add :effect/condition field to any effect. Effect executor checks condition before running effect. Condition system is a multimethod dispatching on :condition/type.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed effects.cljs - clean place to add condition check
- Existing condition system for threshold is card-level, not effect-level
- Multimethod pattern matches existing code style

**Pros:**
- Generic, reusable for future cards
- Data-driven, keeps card definitions declarative
- Clean separation of concerns

**Cons:**
- Slightly more complex than custom effect type

**Chosen because:** Extensibility - avoids proliferation of custom effect types for each conditional card

#### 2. Custom :sacrifice-if-no-counters effect ❌
**What it is:** Create a specific effect type that combines sacrifice with counter check.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler immediate implementation

**Pros:**
- Quick to implement
- No new systems needed

**Cons:**
- Would need new effect type for each conditional pattern
- Not reusable

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User explicitly said we need general conditional support to avoid many custom effects

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We decide effect conditions add too much complexity (unlikely)

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Effect-level condition system
- :no-counters condition type
- Gemstone Mine fix
- Remove zero-counters SBA

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Other condition types (:has-counters, :counter-equals, etc.) - add when needed
- Evaluating broader SBA system - separate future effort
- Other counter-based cards - handle if they come up

### Open Questions
- Should conditions.cljs be separate file or inline in effects.cljs? (decide during implementation, lean toward inline for now)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Fix scope? | Fix Gemstone Mine + remove zero-counters SBA | SBA system stays but zero-counters removed |
| Other cards to audit? | Only Gemstone Mine for now | No broader card audit needed |
| Condition system design? | Effect-level :effect/condition | Each effect can have optional condition |
| Condition types to support? | :no-counters only | Minimal implementation, extend later |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Gemstone Mine Rules
**Question explored:** How does the sacrifice actually work per MTG rules?
**Sources consulted:**
- Scryfall card page and rules notes
- Current implementation in state_based.cljs

**Findings:**
- Sacrifice is part of mana ability text, not a separate triggered ability
- Rules note explicitly states: won't sacrifice if counters removed by other means
- Current SBA implementation is a rules error

**Conclusion:** Must move sacrifice into ability effects with condition check

### Dead-End Paths

#### State-Based Effect Approach
**Why explored:** Was the original implementation approach
**Investigation:**
- Implemented as check-zero-counters SBA
- Fires after any action when counters <= 0

**Why abandoned:** Rules error - sacrifice should only happen as part of mana ability activation

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Is the SBA system correct in light of this?' → Deferred to future evaluation, not blocking this fix

title: Task 3: Create interactive UI components for goldfishing
design:
## Goal
Create Reagent view components that enable interactive goldfishing: clickable hand cards, Cast/Resolve buttons, mana pool display, storm count, and stack view. This completes the Phase 1 milestone.

## Context
Completed Task 1 (re-frame infrastructure) and Task 2 (events/subscriptions wrapping engine). Events and subscriptions are fully wired. Now need components that subscribe to state and dispatch events.

## Implementation

### 1. Update core.cljs with interactive app component

Replace the text-only display with a full interactive layout. All views can live in core.cljs for now (3 cards, simple UI) - extract to separate files only if it becomes unwieldy.

Component structure:
- Hand: shows each card as a clickable div with card name, highlights selected
- Mana pool: shows each color with non-zero amount
- Storm count: shows current storm count
- Stack: shows items on stack (triggers and spells) with names
- Controls: Cast button (enabled when card selected and castable), Resolve button (enabled when stack non-empty)

Key events to dispatch:
- `::events/select-card` on card click (pass object-id)
- `::events/cast-spell` on Cast button click
- `::events/resolve-top` on Resolve button click

Subscriptions to consume:
- `::subs/hand` - cards in hand
- `::subs/mana-pool` - mana by color
- `::subs/storm-count` - storm count
- `::subs/stack` - stack items
- `::subs/selected-card` - currently selected card
- `::subs/can-cast?` - whether selected card is castable

### 2. Styling
Inline styles only (anti-pattern forbids CSS frameworks).
- Cards: bordered divs, highlighted border when selected
- Mana: colored text or symbols for each mana color
- Buttons: basic button styling, disabled state visible

### 3. Verify in browser
- Run `make dev` and open localhost:8080
- Verify: 3 cards in hand, 2 black mana, 0 storm
- Click Dark Ritual -> Cast -> mana becomes BBB+1=4B (started with 2, -1 cost, +3 from effect... wait: start 2B, cast DR costs 1B -> 1B remaining, resolve DR adds 3B -> 4B)
- Actually: casting puts on stack (pays mana), resolving executes effects
- Flow: Select DR -> Cast (pays 1B, moves to stack, storm=1) -> Resolve (adds 3B, moves to graveyard) -> mana pool should show 4B

### 4. Run make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Browser loads at localhost:8080 without console errors
- [ ] Hand shows 3 clickable cards with names (Dark Ritual, Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual)
- [ ] Clicking a card highlights it and shows it as selected
- [ ] Cast button works when card selected and castable
- [ ] Resolve button works when stack has items
- [ ] Mana pool updates correctly after casting and resolving
- [ ] Storm count increments on each cast
- [ ] Stack shows triggers and spells
- [ ] Can complete full combo: DR -> CR -> BF with storm copies
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO game logic in components (subscribe and dispatch only)
- NO direct atom manipulation
- NO CSS frameworks (inline styles only)
- NO new engine code
close_reason: Interactive UI components implemented in core.cljs. Hand with clickable cards, mana pool display, storm count, stack view, and Cast/Resolve buttons. All lint/format/tests pass.

title: Epic: Best-in-Class Developer Experience
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Single command REPL: `make repl` starts node REPL ready to eval ClojureScript
- Single command validation: `make validate` runs lint + format-check + tests, fails fast
- All dev commands documented in CLAUDE.md for agent use
- Makefile is primary interface (familiar to Clojure devs)
- clj-kondo for static analysis/linting
- cljstyle for code formatting (check and fix modes)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] `make repl` starts REPL in one command, ready to eval immediately
- [ ] `make test` runs all tests and reports pass/fail
- [ ] `make lint` runs clj-kondo on src/, reports issues
- [ ] `make fmt-check` verifies formatting without changes
- [ ] `make fmt` auto-fixes formatting
- [ ] `make validate` runs lint + fmt-check + test sequentially, fails on first error
- [ ] `make dev` starts browser watch mode
- [ ] `make clean` removes build artifacts
- [ ] CLAUDE.md updated with Development Commands section
- [ ] All existing tests still pass

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO multi-step REPL setup (simplicity: one command must work)
- ❌ NO npm scripts as primary interface (consistency: Makefile is standard for Clojure projects)
- ❌ NO manual PATH exports in commands (usability: Makefile handles Java path internally)
- ❌ NO hard-coded absolute paths (portability: must work on any machine)

## Approach

Create a Makefile that wraps shadow-cljs commands with proper Java PATH handling. Add clj-kondo via npm for linting and cljstyle as a standalone tool for formatting. Update CLAUDE.md with all commands so the agent uses them consistently.

## Architecture

```
Makefile              # Primary interface
├── repl              # npx shadow-cljs node-repl
├── dev               # npx shadow-cljs watch app  
├── test              # npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
├── lint              # clj-kondo --lint src/
├── fmt-check         # cljstyle check src/
├── fmt               # cljstyle fix src/
├── validate          # lint && fmt-check && test
└── clean             # rm -rf out/ .shadow-cljs/

CLAUDE.md             # Agent instructions
└── Development Commands section
```

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Current DX requires multiple steps for REPL (watch + connect), complex test commands with PATH exports, and no linting/formatting. This slows development and causes friction.

### Research Findings

**External:**
- shadow-cljs node-repl provides one-command REPL for non-browser dev
- clj-kondo is the standard ClojureScript linter (2M+ weekly downloads)
- cljstyle is the recommended formatter (successor to cljfmt)
- Makefile is idiomatic for Clojure projects

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Makefile with node-repl ✓

**What it is:** Use Makefile as primary interface, shadow-cljs node-repl for instant REPL.

**Chosen because:** Familiar to Clojure devs, one-command REPL, handles PATH internally.

#### 2. npm scripts ❌

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Not familiar to Clojure devs, user explicitly requested Makefile.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** User changes preference.

#### 3. deps.edn aliases ❌

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** shadow-cljs standalone is recommended over JVM invocation per official docs.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Need tight integration with other Clojure tooling.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Makefile with all dev commands
- clj-kondo linting
- cljstyle formatting
- CLAUDE.md updates

**Out of scope:**
- Editor integration (Calva/CIDER) - user can configure separately
- CI/CD setup - future epic
- Pre-commit hooks - future epic

## Design Discovery

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| CLI interface? | Makefile (familiar to Clojure devs) | Primary interface is make commands |
| REPL experience? | One command, auto-connects | Use node-repl, no browser needed |
| Validation scope? | All: format + lint + test | make validate runs everything |

title: Strengthen weak card type assertions

title: Task 4: Implement Careful Study with selection modal
design:
## Goal
Add Careful Study spell that draws 2 cards, then requires player to select and discard 2 cards via a modal UI.

## Effort Estimate
3-5 hours (includes selection modal system)

## Context
Completed Task 3 (LED with discard-hand cost). Careful Study requires:
1. A :discard effect with :selection :player that pauses for player input
2. A modal UI component for selecting cards from hand
3. State management for pending selections

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing effect patterns
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs

Look for:
- How execute-effect multimethod dispatches on :effect/type
- How :draw effect adds cards to hand
- How effects return modified db
- Pattern for effects that need player input (may not exist yet)

### 2. Add pending-selection to app-db
Note: This is app-level state (UI), NOT Datascript game state.
Selection state lives in re-frame app-db, not game/db.

File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs (or new selection.cljs)

Selection state structure in app-db:
```clojure
:game/pending-selection
{:selection/zone :hand           ;; Zone to select from
 :selection/count 2              ;; Exact number to select
 :selection/player-id :player-1  ;; Who is selecting
 :selection/selected #{}         ;; Set of object-ids currently selected
 :selection/effect {:effect/type :discard ...}} ;; Effect to resume after selection
```

### 3. Write tests first (TDD)
Add tests to: src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs (new file)

Test cases with bug-catching rationale:

```clojure
;; test_careful_study_is_sorcery_type
;; Verify card definition has correct type
;; Bug caught: Wrong card type would affect timing rules

;; test_careful_study_draws_two_cards
;; Setup: Player has 3 cards in hand, cast Careful Study
;; Verify: Hand size increased by 2 (minus the spell = net +1)
;; Bug caught: Missing or broken :draw effect

;; test_careful_study_creates_pending_selection
;; Setup: Player casts Careful Study from hand
;; Action: Resolve spell
;; Verify: :game/pending-selection exists with :count 2, :zone :hand
;; Bug caught: Missing selection state creation

;; test_toggle_selection_adds_and_removes_card
;; Setup: Pending selection exists
;; Action: Toggle card A (add), toggle card A again (remove)
;; Verify: Selected set correctly reflects toggles
;; Bug caught: Broken toggle logic

;; test_confirm_selection_requires_exact_count
;; Setup: Pending selection with count=2, only 1 selected
;; Action: Attempt confirm
;; Verify: Confirm fails or is disabled, selection persists
;; Bug caught: Allowing wrong count to be confirmed

;; test_confirm_selection_discards_selected_cards
;; Setup: Pending selection with 2 cards selected
;; Action: Confirm selection
;; Verify: Those 2 cards move to graveyard, selection cleared
;; Bug caught: Cards not actually discarded

;; test_careful_study_with_exactly_two_cards_after_draw
;; Setup: Player has 0 cards, draws 2 from Careful Study
;; Verify: Must discard both cards (selection required even when obvious)
;; Bug caught: Auto-selecting when hand equals count

;; test_careful_study_with_more_than_two_cards_after_draw
;; Setup: Player has 3 cards, draws 2 (now 5 in hand)
;; Verify: Must select exactly 2 of the 5
;; Bug caught: Not offering full hand for selection
```

### 4. Add :discard effect type with selection support
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs

```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect :discard [db player-id effect]
  (let [selection (:effect/selection effect)
        count-to-discard (:effect/count effect)]
    (if (= :player selection)
      ;; Player selection required - return db unchanged
      ;; The selection state is handled at app-db level, not game-db
      ;; Spell resolution pauses until selection confirmed
      db
      ;; Programmatic discard (random or specified) - not needed for Careful Study
      db)))
```

Note: The actual selection handling happens in re-frame events, not in execute-effect.
The effect returns db unchanged; the calling code checks for :selection :player
and sets up the pending selection.

### 5. Modify spell resolution to handle selection effects
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

Modify resolve-spell or add wrapper:
```clojure
(defn resolve-spell-with-selection [db player-id object-id]
  (let [spell-obj (queries/get-object db object-id)
        card (:object/card spell-obj)
        effects (:card/effects card)]
    ;; Execute effects until we hit a selection effect
    (loop [db' db
           remaining-effects effects]
      (if (empty? remaining-effects)
        ;; All effects done, move spell to graveyard
        (zones/move-to-zone db' object-id :graveyard)
        (let [effect (first remaining-effects)]
          (if (= :player (:effect/selection effect))
            ;; Pause for selection - return db with spell still on stack
            ;; Pending selection will be set up at app-db level
            {:db db'
             :pending {:selection/zone :hand
                       :selection/count (:effect/count effect)
                       :selection/player-id player-id
                       :selection/remaining-effects (rest remaining-effects)
                       :selection/spell-id object-id}}
            ;; Execute effect and continue
            (recur (effects/execute-effect db' player-id effect)
                   (rest remaining-effects))))))))
```

### 6. Add selection state management events
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::toggle-selection
  (fn [db [_ object-id]]
    (let [selected (get-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected] #{})
          new-selected (if (contains? selected object-id)
                         (disj selected object-id)
                         (conj selected object-id))]
      (assoc-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected] new-selected))))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::confirm-selection
  (fn [db _]
    (let [selection (:game/pending-selection db)
          selected (:selection/selected selection)
          count-required (:selection/count selection)]
      (if (= (count selected) count-required)
        ;; Valid selection - discard selected cards and clear selection
        (let [game-db (:game/db db)
              db-after-discard (reduce (fn [gdb obj-id]
                                         (zones/move-to-zone gdb obj-id :graveyard))
                                       game-db
                                       selected)]
          (-> db
              (assoc :game/db db-after-discard)
              (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))
        ;; Invalid count - do nothing
        db))))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::cancel-selection
  (fn [db _]
    ;; Note: Cancel behavior for Careful Study is complex
    ;; Cards already drawn can't be "undrawn"
    ;; For MVP: Cancel clears selection but keeps drawn cards
    ;; Player must still discard 2 (selection re-opens)
    (assoc-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected] #{})))
```

### 7. Add selection modal component
File: src/main/fizzle/core.cljs

```clojure
(defn selection-modal []
  (let [selection @(rf/subscribe [::subs/pending-selection])
        hand @(rf/subscribe [::subs/hand :player-1])]
    (when selection
      [:div.selection-modal
       {:class "fixed inset-0 bg-black bg-opacity-50 flex items-center justify-center"}
       [:div.modal-content
        {:class "bg-gray-800 p-4 rounded-lg max-w-md"}
        [:h2.text-white.text-lg.mb-2
         (str "Select " (:selection/count selection) " cards to discard")]
        [:p.text-gray-400.mb-4
         (str (count (:selection/selected selection)) " selected")]
        
        ;; Card grid
        [:div.grid.grid-cols-3.gap-2.mb-4
         (for [card hand]
           (let [obj-id (:object/id card)
                 selected? (contains? (:selection/selected selection) obj-id)]
             ^{:key obj-id}
             [:div.card
              {:class (if selected? "ring-2 ring-blue-500" "")
               :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/toggle-selection obj-id])}
              (:card/name (:object/card card))]))]
        
        ;; Buttons
        [:div.flex.justify-end.gap-2
         [:button.px-4.py-2.bg-gray-600.rounded
          {:on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/cancel-selection])}
          "Cancel"]
         [:button.px-4.py-2.bg-blue-600.rounded
          {:disabled (not= (count (:selection/selected selection))
                           (:selection/count selection))
           :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/confirm-selection])}
          "Confirm"]]]])))
```

### 8. Add Careful Study card definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
;; Careful Study - Card filtering cantrip
;; Draw two cards, then discard two cards.
(def careful-study
  {:card/id :careful-study
   :card/name "Careful Study"
   :card/cmc 1
   :card/mana-cost {:blue 1}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:sorcery}
   :card/text "Draw two cards, then discard two cards."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 2}
                  {:effect/type :discard
                   :effect/count 2
                   :effect/selection :player}]})
```

### 9. Add subscription for pending selection
File: src/main/fizzle/subs.cljs (or core.cljs if subs are there)

```clojure
(rf/reg-sub
  ::pending-selection
  (fn [db _]
    (:game/pending-selection db)))
```

### 10. Update all-cards vector and run validation

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Careful Study definition exists with :card/mana-cost {:blue 1}
- [ ] Careful Study has :card/types #{:sorcery}
- [ ] Casting Careful Study draws 2 cards to hand
- [ ] After draw, pending selection created with :count 2, :zone :hand
- [ ] Selection modal appears when pending-selection exists
- [ ] Clicking card toggles it in/out of :selection/selected set
- [ ] Confirm button disabled when count != required count
- [ ] Confirming valid selection moves selected cards to graveyard
- [ ] Cancel button clears selection (re-prompts)
- [ ] 8+ tests pass in careful_study_test.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-selecting discard targets (user agency: player must choose)
- ❌ NO blocking UI during selection (must be dismissable)
- ❌ NO skipping selection even if hand has exactly 2 cards (consistency)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card IDs in selection logic (generic system)
- ❌ NO storing selection state in Datascript game-db (use app-db)
- ❌ NO modifying spell resolution to auto-discard on timeout

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Selection State Location**
- Selection state is UI state, NOT game state
- Lives in re-frame app-db at :game/pending-selection
- Do NOT add to Datascript schema - that's for game state persistence
- This allows selection to be cleared on UI reset without affecting game

**Cancel Behavior Complexity**
- Careful Study's cancel is tricky: cards already drawn can't be "undrawn"
- For MVP: Cancel clears selection but player must still discard 2
- Alternative: Cancel reverts entire spell (complex - need state snapshot)
- Decision: MVP approach - cancel just re-prompts selection

**Effect Execution Pause Pattern**
- Effects execute synchronously in engine
- Selection breaks this - need async pattern
- Solution: resolve-spell returns partial state when hitting selection effect
- Remaining effects stored in pending-selection
- On confirm, remaining effects continue

**Hand Size Edge Cases**
- After drawing 2, player might have exactly 2 cards → must still select
- After drawing 2, player might have only 1 card → ERROR CASE
  - This shouldn't happen (Careful Study draws 2, discard 2)
  - But if library was depleted mid-draw, could have <2 cards
  - Decision: If hand < count required, force discard all available
  - Add test for this edge case

**Reference Implementation**
- Study src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs for re-frame event patterns
- Study src/main/fizzle/core.cljs for Reagent component patterns
- Study how :game/selected-card already works for card selection

title: Phase 1: Storm Keyword + Card Definitions
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Storm triggers on CAST, not resolution (rules-accurate timing)
- Storm creates a separate trigger object on the stack
- Storm trigger resolves BEFORE original spell (trigger goes on top)
- Storm copies are NOT cast (do not increment storm count)
- Storm copies do not have active storm keyword (no cascade)
- Storm count = spells cast BEFORE the storm spell this turn
- Threshold activates at exactly 7+ cards in graveyard
- Conditional effects use card-level modes (not per-effect condition checks)
- Mill handles partial library gracefully (mill N or remaining, whichever is less)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual, LED, Dark Ritual, basic lands implemented
- [ ] Storm trigger creates correct number of copies
- [ ] Copies resolve before original spell
- [ ] Threshold condition affects Cabal Ritual output
- [ ] Mill effect moves cards from library to graveyard
- [ ] Integration test: Cast 2 rituals + Brain Freeze = 3 mill resolutions (9 cards)
- [ ] All edge case tests pass (zero storm, empty library, threshold boundary)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass (lint, format)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO storm copies incrementing storm count (breaks rules: copies are not cast)
- ❌ NO condition checks inside individual effect methods (duplication: use card-level modes)
- ❌ NO storm triggering on resolution (timing: MTG rules say cast-time trigger)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card logic in engine (architecture: cards are data, engine interprets)
- ❌ NO skipping edge case tests (quality: edge cases reveal design flaws)

## Approach

Implement storm as a rules-accurate triggered ability system:

1. Extend stack to hold trigger objects alongside spells
2. When casting a spell with :storm keyword, create trigger on top of spell
3. Storm trigger resolution creates N copies (N = spells cast before this turn)
4. Copies are new objects with :object/is-copy true
5. Conditional effects use card-level :card/conditional-effects for threshold

This approach matches MTG rules precisely while building infrastructure for future triggered abilities (Prowess, ETB effects, etc).

## Architecture

### New Schema
- :trigger/id, :trigger/type, :trigger/source, :trigger/controller, :trigger/data
- :object/is-copy (boolean, default false)

### Modified Functions
- rules/cast-spell: Check for :storm keyword, create trigger if present
- rules/resolve-spell: Handle trigger resolution separately from spell resolution

### New Functions
- rules/create-storm-trigger: Creates trigger object
- rules/resolve-trigger: Multimethod dispatching on :trigger/type
- rules/create-spell-copy: Creates copy of spell on stack
- effects/execute-effect :mill: Move cards from library to graveyard
- conditions/threshold?: Check graveyard count >= 7
- rules/get-active-effects: Select effects based on conditions

### Card Definitions
- brain-freeze: {instant, :storm, mill 3}
- cabal-ritual: {instant, :threshold, add-mana BBB/BBBBB}
- lions-eye-diamond: {artifact, activated ability}
- Basic lands (swamp, island, etc)

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Phase 1 requires storm combo to work. Current engine has no triggered ability system and only one card (Dark Ritual). Cannot test storm combos.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- engine/effects.cljs:1-20 - Multimethod dispatch on :effect/type, easy to extend
- engine/rules.cljs:30-35 - Storm count tracked, incremented on cast-spell
- db/queries.cljs - get-storm-count, get-objects-in-zone available
- No infrastructure for triggers or copies

**External:**
- MTG rules: Storm triggers on cast, creates copies equal to prior spell count
- Copies are NOT cast (critical for storm count accuracy)
- Trigger resolves before spell (copies first, then original)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Strict Rules-Accurate (Full Trigger Model) ✓

**What it is:** Model storm as a triggered ability with explicit trigger objects on stack. Trigger resolves first, creates copies, then original spell resolves.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed MTG comprehensive rules for storm timing
- Examined codebase for extension points (multimethod pattern)
- Confirmed stack can hold different object types

**Pros:**
- Accurate MTG timing
- Builds trigger infrastructure for future abilities (ETB, Prowess)
- Supports future counter-spell interactions

**Cons:**
- More complex than simplified approaches

**Chosen because:** User explicitly chose rules-accurate approach; infrastructure investment pays off for future phases

#### 2. Simplified Cast-Time Copies ❌

**What it is:** Create copies during cast-spell without explicit trigger objects. Correct resolution order but no separate trigger.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler implementation for goldfish practice

**Investigation:**
- Would work for Phase 1 but limits future interactions
- Cannot model 'counter the trigger' scenarios

**Pros:**
- Simpler implementation
- Correct resolution order

**Cons:**
- No trigger interaction support
- Technical debt for Phase 3+ (fork/replay of trigger decisions)

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User chose rules-accurate; future phases need trigger model

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Requirements change to goldfish-only permanently

#### 3. Ultra-Simplified Resolution Multiplier ❌

**What it is:** Execute effects N+1 times during resolve-spell. No copies on stack.

**Why we looked at this:** Minimal code change

**Investigation:**
- Breaks MTG timing model entirely
- Cannot visualize copies on stack for practice

**Pros:**
- Simplest implementation

**Cons:**
- Rules-inaccurate timing
- No stack visualization for learning
- Breaks mental model for combo practice

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Defeats purpose of practice tool (learning real timing)

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Requirements pivot to non-educational tool

### Conditional Effects Design Decision

**Decision:** Card-level effect modes (Option B) over per-effect condition checks

**Rationale:**
- Avoids duplicating condition checks in every effect method
- Effects stay pure and simple
- Rules layer handles condition selection once
- Matches MTG card text structure ('Add BBB. Threshold - Add BBBBB instead.')

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Storm keyword with full trigger model
- Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual, LED, basic lands
- Mill effect, threshold condition
- Edge case tests

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- UI (Phase 1 completion, separate task)
- Other keywords (flashback, madness)
- Sideboard cards
- Deck import/export

### Open Questions
- LED mana color choice: UI required, may stub initially
- Threshold during resolution: Should graveyard count update mid-stack? (Default: yes, recalculate per effect)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Which Phase 1 area to tackle? | Cards + Storm together | Implement both in single epic |
| Storm implementation approach? | Strict Rules-Accurate | Full trigger model, not simplified |
| Triggered ability architecture? | Yes, proceed | Explicit trigger objects on stack |
| Card definitions correct? | Yes for Phase 1 | Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual, LED, lands |
| Conditional effects approach? | Option B: Card-level modes | No condition checks inside effects |
| Testing strategy? | Add edge cases | Comprehensive tests including boundaries |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Codebase Architecture
**Question explored:** How do effects and rules work?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/effects.cljs - multimethod dispatch pattern
- engine/rules.cljs - cast/resolve flow, storm count tracking
- db/queries.cljs - available queries for graveyard, storm count

**Findings:**
- Clean extension points via multimethod
- Storm count already tracked but copies not implemented
- No trigger infrastructure exists

**Conclusion:** Extend existing multimethod pattern, add trigger resolution

#### MTG Storm Rules
**Question explored:** Exact timing and mechanics of storm
**Sources consulted:**
- MTG Comprehensive Rules
- Judge blogs on storm mechanics
- Gatherer rulings for Brain Freeze

**Findings:**
- Storm triggers on cast, not resolution
- Copies NOT cast (critical)
- Trigger resolves first, then spell

**Conclusion:** Need explicit trigger model for accuracy

### Dead-End Paths

#### Per-Effect Condition Checks
**Why explored:** Initial design had conditions inside each effect method
**Investigation:** User pointed out duplication concern
**Why abandoned:** Would duplicate condition logic in every effect type

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Condition checks duplicated?' → Moved to card-level effect modes (Option B)
- 'LED needs color choice UI' → May stub initially, full UI in later task
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: Storm keyword with full trigger model, Brain Freeze/Cabal Ritual/Dark Ritual cards, mill effect, threshold condition. All 75 tests pass. Integration test verifies 2 rituals + Brain Freeze = 9 cards milled.

title: Card Batch 2: Tutor, Triggers, Flashback Infrastructure
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

1. **Tutor System**: Implement :tutor effect type with library search, criteria filtering, selection modal, and fail-to-find option
2. **Event System Migration**: Migrate City of Brass :becomes-tapped trigger to event system, remove old scanning code (fire-matching-triggers, fire-and-resolve-triggers)
3. **Land-Entered Trigger**: Add :land-entered event type for City of Traitors sacrifice trigger
4. **Flashback System**: Implement graveyard casting with alternate cost and exile-on-resolution
5. **Cards**: Merchant Scroll, Polluted Delta, City of Traitors, Deep Analysis

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Merchant Scroll tutors for blue instant with selection modal and fail-to-find
- [ ] Polluted Delta fetches Island/Swamp subtype to battlefield, sacrifices self
- [ ] City of Traitors produces {C}{C}, sacrifices when another land enters
- [ ] Deep Analysis castable from graveyard with flashback cost, exiles on resolution
- [ ] City of Brass trigger uses event system (no scanning)
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers and fire-and-resolve-triggers removed from codebase
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO scanning-based triggers (tech debt: event system is the pattern, scanning doesn't work for Gaea's Blessing-style cards)
- ❌ NO auto-select for tutors (rules: fail-to-find must always be available, hidden information matters)
- ❌ NO new trigger code using check-trigger multimethod (consistency: use trigger-registry + dispatch-event)
- ❌ NO flashback cards going to graveyard on resolution (rules: flashback exiles)
- ❌ NO leaving dead code (cleanup: if it's not used, delete it)

## Approach

**Phase 1: Event System Cleanup**
- Migrate City of Brass :becomes-tapped to event system
- Add :permanent-tapped event dispatch to activate-mana-ability
- Register CoB trigger on ETB, unregister on leave
- Delete fire-matching-triggers, fire-and-resolve-triggers, check-trigger :becomes-tapped
- Update/delete affected tests

**Phase 2: Tutor Infrastructure**
- Add :tutor effect type to effects.cljs
- Add library query function with criteria matching (types, colors, subtypes)
- Add tutor selection modal (reuse Careful Study pattern)
- Include fail-to-find option in all tutor selections
- Implement Merchant Scroll as validation

**Phase 3: Land-Entered Trigger**
- Add land-entered-event to events.cljs
- Dispatch event in play-land
- Add :exclude-self filter support to matches-filter?
- Implement City of Traitors

**Phase 4: Fetchland**
- Implement Polluted Delta using tutor + sacrifice ability
- Add :pay-life cost type if not present
- Tutor to battlefield (not hand)

**Phase 5: Flashback**
- Add :card/flashback-cost property
- Extend can-cast? to check graveyard zone
- Track cast-via-flashback for exile-on-resolution
- Implement Deep Analysis

## Architecture

**Event System (existing, to be used consistently):**
- events.cljs: Event creation helpers
- trigger_registry.cljs: Register/unregister triggers
- trigger_dispatch.cljs: dispatch-event matching and execution

**New Components:**
- effects.cljs: Add :tutor effect type
- queries.cljs: Add query-library-by-criteria
- rules.cljs: Extend can-cast? for flashback
- views/selection.cljs: Extend modal for tutor selection

**Card Definitions (iggy_pop.cljs):**
- merchant-scroll, polluted-delta, city-of-traitors, deep-analysis

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Iggy Pop deck needs tutor effects (Merchant Scroll, fetchlands), land sacrifice triggers (City of Traitors), and flashback (Deep Analysis). Current codebase has partial trigger system migration leaving dead code and inconsistent patterns.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- trigger_registry.cljs - Event-based trigger registration exists
- trigger_dispatch.cljs - dispatch-event with filter matching exists
- triggers.cljs:220-310 - Old fire-matching-triggers still used by City of Brass
- game.cljs:529 - activate-mana-ability calls old scanning system
- events.cljs - Has permanent-tapped-event, zone-change-event helpers

**Patterns:**
- Selection modal exists (Careful Study discard)
- Sacrifice-self cost exists (Lotus Petal, LED)
- Pay-life may need implementation for fetchlands

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Event-Based Triggers for All Cards ✓

**What it is:** Use trigger_registry + dispatch-event for all card triggers. Migrate City of Brass, implement new cards with same pattern.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed trigger_dispatch.cljs - clean dispatch with filter matching
- Reviewed trigger_registry.cljs - register/unregister pattern
- Confirmed turn-based actions already use this system

**Pros:**
- Consistent architecture
- Works for zone-change triggers (Gaea's Blessing)
- Clean event-driven model

**Cons:**
- Requires migrating City of Brass
- Must delete old code

**Chosen because:** Scanning won't work for cards that trigger on zone changes (mill triggers). Event system is the correct long-term architecture.

#### 2. Keep Dual Systems ❌

**What it is:** Leave City of Brass on old system, use new system for new cards.

**Why we looked at this:** Less scope, faster delivery.

**Investigation:**
- Would leave dead code
- Inconsistent patterns for similar triggers
- Tech debt compounds

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Tech debt, inconsistency, user explicitly wants cleanup.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Never - this is a cleanup requirement.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- 4 cards: Merchant Scroll, Polluted Delta, City of Traitors, Deep Analysis
- Tutor effect with selection modal
- Land-entered trigger type
- Flashback mechanic
- City of Brass migration
- Dead code removal

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Intuition (pile selection UI - more complex)
- Ill-Gotten Gains (multi-player selection)
- Cunning Wish (sideboard zone)
- Other fetchlands (same pattern as Polluted Delta)

### Open Questions
- Should tutor shuffle happen before or after selection confirmed? (After - selection might fail)
- Should flashback cost show in graveyard view? (Yes - display alternate cost)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Card priority | All 4 cards | Full batch scope |
| Tutor UI | Modal with filtered list | Reuse Careful Study pattern |
| City of Traitors trigger | When you play another land | :land-entered event + exclude-self filter |
| Flashback UI | Full implementation | Graveyard casting, exile on resolution |
| Fetch targets | Match subtypes | Query by :card/subtypes |
| Auto-select tutors | No - fail to find required | Always show modal with Find Nothing option |
| Migrate City of Brass | Yes | Clean up tech debt, remove old code |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Trigger System Architecture
**Question explored:** Which trigger system to use?
**Sources consulted:**
- trigger_registry.cljs - event registration pattern
- trigger_dispatch.cljs - event dispatch with filtering
- triggers.cljs - old scanning approach
- game.cljs:529 - where old system is called

**Findings:**
- Old system scans battlefield after state change
- New system dispatches events, registered triggers match
- New system required for zone-change triggers (Gaea's Blessing)
- City of Brass is only card using old system

**Conclusion:** Migrate to event system, delete old code

### Dead-End Paths

#### Scanning-Based Triggers
**Why explored:** Already implemented, seemed to work
**Investigation:**
- User pointed out Gaea's Blessing won't work with scanning
- Scanning checks state diff, doesn't know what caused change
- Zone-change triggers need event context

**Why abandoned:** Doesn't support zone-change triggers, event system already exists

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Code for scan based system still exists' → Full cleanup, remove dead code
- 'Gaea's Blessing compatibility' → Event system handles zone-change triggers correctly
notes:
## Epic Closure Summary

### Delivered (6/7 tasks)
- ✅ **Event System Migration** (fizzle-h4l): City of Brass trigger migrated to event system, old scanning code removed
- ✅ **Tutor System** (fizzle-pk2): Implemented :tutor effect type with selection modal, fail-to-find, shuffle - Merchant Scroll working
- ✅ **Land-Entered Trigger** (fizzle-3al): Added :land-entered event type with exclude-self filter - City of Traitors working  
- ✅ **Fetchland** (fizzle-7ef): Implemented Polluted Delta with fetch-to-battlefield
- ✅ **Pay-Life Cost** (fizzle-pxz): Added :pay-life cost type for Polluted Delta
- ✅ **Activated Abilities on Stack** (fizzle-6gi): Polluted Delta (and all activated abilities) now use the stack

### Deferred to Future Epic
- ⏸️ **Flashback System** (fizzle-2q8): Well-documented implementation plan for Deep Analysis and generic flashback mechanic

### Why Scope Reduced
Original epic scope was ambitious. The activated abilities stack work (fizzle-6gi) was emergent complexity discovered during fetchland implementation. Flashback requires significant rules engine changes (graveyard casting, life costs, exile-on-resolution) that deserve their own focused epic.

### Success Criteria Status
- ✅ Merchant Scroll tutors for blue instant with selection modal and fail-to-find
- ✅ Polluted Delta fetches Island/Swamp subtype to battlefield, sacrifices self  
- ✅ City of Traitors produces {C}{C}, sacrifices when another land enters
- ⏸️ Deep Analysis - deferred (flashback system not implemented)
- ✅ City of Brass trigger uses event system (no scanning)
- ✅ fire-matching-triggers and fire-and-resolve-triggers removed from codebase
- ✅ All tests passing
- ✅ make validate passes
close_reason: Epic delivered 6/7 planned features: tutor system, land-entered triggers, fetchland, pay-life costs, activated abilities stack integration, and event system migration. Flashback system (fizzle-2q8) deferred to future epic - well-documented for pickup.

title: Implement mill effect and Brain Freeze card
design:
## Goal

Implement the mill effect type and Brain Freeze card definition to enable storm-mill combos.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context

Completed tasks:
- fizzle-21w: Trigger infrastructure (create-trigger, resolve-trigger, stack integration)
- fizzle-1fm: Storm keyword (copies created on cast, resolve before original)

Now need: Mill effect + Brain Freeze card to test storm-mill combo.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

Reference implementations:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:31-33 - execute-effect :add-mana pattern (follow this)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:12-33 - move-to-zone function (use for moving cards)
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Dark Ritual card definition pattern
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:79-90 - get-objects-in-zone pattern
- src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs:38 - :object/position for library ordering

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs (add to existing file)

Test cases with bug-catching rationale:
- [ ] test-mill-effect-moves-cards: Mill 3 moves top 3 from library to graveyard (catches: effect not working at all)
- [ ] test-mill-effect-respects-position: Mill takes cards with LOWEST :object/position (catches: wrong order - bottom instead of top)
- [ ] test-mill-effect-partial-library: Mill 5 with 2 cards in library moves 2 (catches: crash on partial library)
- [ ] test-mill-effect-empty-library: Mill on empty library is no-op, returns unchanged db (catches: crash on empty library)
- [ ] test-mill-effect-preserves-order: First card milled (lowest position) goes to graveyard first (catches: flashback order issues later)
- [ ] test-mill-effect-targets-opponent: Mill with :effect/target :opponent mills opponent, not caster (catches: targeting wrong player)

Note: Brain Freeze card definition tests are deferred to integration testing - card definitions are pure data and testing their structure is tautological. Instead, we test the behavior when used.

### 3. Implementation checklist

Add query to src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:
- [ ] get-top-n-library [db player-id n]
  - Query objects where :object/owner = player and :object/zone = :library
  - Sort by :object/position ascending (lowest = top of library)
  - Take first n (or all if fewer than n)
  - Return vector of object-ids (just the :object/id values, not full objects)
  - Handle empty library: return []
  - Handle nil player: return nil

Example implementation:
```clojure
(defn get-top-n-library
  "Get the top N cards from a player's library by position.
   Returns vector of object-ids (lowest position = top).
   Returns [] if library has fewer than N cards.
   Returns nil if player doesn't exist."
  [db player-id n]
  (let [player-eid (get-player-eid db player-id)]
    (when player-eid
      (->> (d/q '[:find ?oid ?pos
                  :in $ ?owner
                  :where [?obj :object/owner ?owner]
                         [?obj :object/zone :library]
                         [?obj :object/id ?oid]
                         [?obj :object/position ?pos]]
                db player-eid)
           (sort-by second)  ; sort by position ascending
           (take n)
           (mapv first)))))  ; extract just object-ids
```

Add to src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:
- [ ] Add require for zones: [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]
- [ ] Add require for queries: [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
- [ ] defmethod execute-effect :mill [db player-id effect]
  - Get target-player: (get effect :effect/target player-id) - defaults to caster if not specified
  - If target is :opponent, resolve to actual opponent player-id (query game state)
  - Get count from (:effect/amount effect)
  - Get top N object-ids via (q/get-top-n-library db target-player count)
  - Reduce over object-ids, calling (zones/move-to-zone db oid :graveyard) for each
  - Return final db

Example implementation:
```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect :mill
  [db player-id effect]
  (let [target (get effect :effect/target player-id)
        target-player (if (= target :opponent)
                        (q/get-opponent-id db player-id)
                        target)
        amount (:effect/amount effect)
        cards-to-mill (q/get-top-n-library db target-player amount)]
    (reduce (fn [db' oid]
              (zones/move-to-zone db' oid :graveyard))
            db
            cards-to-mill)))
```

- [ ] Add helper query if needed: get-opponent-id [db player-id]
  - Find player where :player/is-opponent is opposite of given player
  - Or simpler: find player where :player/id != player-id

Add to src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:
- [ ] def brain-freeze
```clojure
(def brain-freeze
  {:card/id :brain-freeze
   :card/name "Brain Freeze"
   :card/cmc 2
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :blue 1}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/keywords #{:storm}
   :card/text "Target player mills 3. Storm."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :mill
                   :effect/amount 3
                   :effect/target :opponent}]})
```
- [ ] Update all-cards to include brain-freeze: (def all-cards [dark-ritual brain-freeze])

## Success Criteria

- [ ] get-top-n-library query returns cards sorted by :object/position (lowest first)
- [ ] get-top-n-library handles empty library (returns [])
- [ ] get-top-n-library handles partial library (returns all available)
- [ ] Mill effect moves cards from library to graveyard zone
- [ ] Mill effect targets correct player (:opponent resolves to opponent)
- [ ] Brain Freeze card defined with :storm keyword and :mill effect
- [ ] 6 mill effect tests pass (see test list above)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass: make test (60 tests)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: make validate

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Empty Library**
- get-top-n-library returns [] for empty library
- Mill effect reduce over empty list is no-op (returns db unchanged)
- Test: test-mill-effect-empty-library verifies this

**Edge Case: Partial Library**
- get-top-n-library uses (take n) which handles n > available
- Mill 5 with 2 cards in library moves 2, not crash
- Test: test-mill-effect-partial-library verifies this

**Edge Case: Library Ordering**
- :object/position determines top of library (lowest = top)
- MUST sort ascending, not descending
- Test: test-mill-effect-respects-position verifies correct order

**Target Resolution**
- :effect/target :opponent is a symbolic reference
- Must resolve to actual player-id at execution time
- If target doesn't exist, reduce over [] is no-op (safe)

**Graveyard Ordering**
- Cards milled go to graveyard in order (first milled = "bottom" of graveyard)
- Important for flashback, dredge, etc. later
- Test: test-mill-effect-preserves-order verifies this

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow execute-effect :add-mana pattern (effects.cljs:31-33)
- Follow move-to-zone pattern (zones.cljs:12-33)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO crashing on empty/partial library (handle gracefully with take/reduce)
- ❌ NO hardcoded Brain Freeze logic in engine (card is data, engine interprets)
- ❌ NO milling more cards than exist in library (use take n, not nth)
- ❌ NO assuming library order without :object/position (always sort by position)
- ❌ NO milling wrong player (must resolve :opponent to actual player-id)
- ❌ NO ignoring graveyard order (matters for flashback/dredge later)
close_reason: Implemented mill effect and Brain Freeze card with tests
