title: Task 2: Create re-frame events and subscriptions wrapping engine
design:
## Goal
Create the re-frame event and subscription layer that wraps existing engine functions. Events delegate to engine pure functions, subscriptions use existing query functions. This is the data bridge between UI components and the game engine.

## Context
Completed fizzle-1rd: Re-frame infrastructure is set up with core.cljs entry point and placeholder app-db. Now need the actual game events and subscriptions.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

Events wrap engine functions. No game logic in events (anti-pattern).

```clojure
(ns fizzle.events.game
  (:require
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as queries]
    [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [fizzle.engine.triggers :as triggers]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]))
```

**Event 1: ::init-game**

Creates datascript db with all 3 Phase 1 cards in hand. Uses d/create-conn + d/transact! pattern (same as db/init.cljs) because ref attributes need entity IDs from prior transactions.

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::init-game
  (fn [_ _]
    (let [conn (d/create-conn schema)]
      ;; Transact card definitions
      (d/transact! conn cards/all-cards)
      ;; Transact player with 2 black starting mana
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :player-1
                          :player/name "Player"
                          :player/life 20
                          :player/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 2
                                             :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
                          :player/storm-count 0
                          :player/land-plays-left 1}])
      ;; Transact opponent (needed for Brain Freeze mill effect)
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :opponent
                          :player/name "Opponent"
                          :player/life 20
                          :player/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0
                                             :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
                          :player/storm-count 0
                          :player/land-plays-left 0
                          :player/is-opponent true}])
      ;; Get entity IDs for references
      (let [db @conn
            player-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?pid :where [?e :player/id ?pid]] db :player-1)
            dr-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :dark-ritual)
            cr-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :cabal-ritual)
            bf-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :brain-freeze)
            opp-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?pid :where [?e :player/id ?pid]] db :opponent)]
        ;; Create game objects (3 cards in hand)
        (d/transact! conn [{:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card dr-eid :object/zone :hand
                            :object/owner player-eid :object/controller player-eid :object/tapped false}
                           {:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card cr-eid :object/zone :hand
                            :object/owner player-eid :object/controller player-eid :object/tapped false}
                           {:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card bf-eid :object/zone :hand
                            :object/owner player-eid :object/controller player-eid :object/tapped false}])
        ;; Create opponent library (40 cards so mill has targets)
        (d/transact! conn (vec (for [i (range 40)]
                                 {:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card dr-eid :object/zone :library
                                  :object/owner opp-eid :object/controller opp-eid
                                  :object/tapped false :object/position i})))
        ;; Game state
        (d/transact! conn [{:game/id :game-1
                            :game/turn 1
                            :game/phase :main1
                            :game/active-player player-eid
                            :game/priority player-eid}]))
      {:game/db @conn})))
```

Player starts with {:black 2} mana (enough to cast Dark Ritual which costs {B}).
Opponent player with 40-card library needed so Brain Freeze mill effect has cards to mill.

**Event 2: ::select-card**

Pure UI state, no engine call. Toggles selection (deselect if clicking same card).

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::select-card
  (fn [db [_ object-id]]
    (let [currently-selected (:game/selected-card db)]
      (assoc db :game/selected-card
             (when (not= currently-selected object-id) object-id)))))
```

**Event 3: ::cast-spell**

Guard: no-op if no card selected or cant cast.

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::cast-spell
  (fn [db _]
    (let [game-db (:game/db db)
          selected (:game/selected-card db)]
      (if (and selected (rules/can-cast? game-db :player-1 selected))
        (-> db
            (assoc :game/db (rules/cast-spell game-db :player-1 selected))
            (dissoc :game/selected-card))
        db))))
```

**Event 4: ::resolve-top**

CRITICAL: The stack has TWO types of items:
1. Triggers - queried via queries/get-stack-items (entities with :trigger/stack-order)
2. Spell objects - queried via queries/get-objects-in-zone for :stack zone

Resolution order: triggers first (higher stack-order, added after spells). When no triggers remain, resolve spell objects.

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::resolve-top
  (fn [db _]
    (let [game-db (:game/db db)
          stack-triggers (queries/get-stack-items game-db)]
      (if (seq stack-triggers)
        ;; Resolve top trigger (first = highest stack-order = most recent)
        (let [top-trigger (first stack-triggers)
              game-db' (-> game-db
                           (triggers/resolve-trigger top-trigger)
                           (triggers/remove-trigger (:trigger/id top-trigger)))]
          (assoc db :game/db game-db'))
        ;; No triggers - resolve top spell object on stack
        (let [stack-objects (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :stack)]
          (if (seq stack-objects)
            (let [top-spell (first stack-objects)
                  game-db' (rules/resolve-spell game-db :player-1 (:object/id top-spell))]
              (assoc db :game/db game-db'))
            db))))))
```

NOTE: get-objects-in-zone filters by owner. Storm copies have same owner as original (see triggers/create-spell-copy). So this works for Phase 1.

### 2. Create src/main/fizzle/subs/game.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.subs.game
  (:require
    [fizzle.db.queries :as queries]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]))
```

Layer 2 (extraction) subscriptions:
```clojure
(rf/reg-sub ::game-db (fn [db _] (:game/db db)))
(rf/reg-sub ::selected-card (fn [db _] (:game/selected-card db)))
```

Layer 3 (derived) subscriptions using sugar syntax:
```clojure
(rf/reg-sub
  ::hand
  :<- [::game-db]
  (fn [game-db _]
    (when game-db (queries/get-hand game-db :player-1))))

(rf/reg-sub
  ::mana-pool
  :<- [::game-db]
  (fn [game-db _]
    (when game-db (queries/get-mana-pool game-db :player-1))))

(rf/reg-sub
  ::storm-count
  :<- [::game-db]
  (fn [game-db _]
    (when game-db (queries/get-storm-count game-db :player-1))))

(rf/reg-sub
  ::stack
  :<- [::game-db]
  (fn [game-db _]
    (when game-db
      (let [triggers (queries/get-stack-items game-db)
            spells (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :stack)]
        (concat triggers spells)))))

(rf/reg-sub
  ::can-cast?
  :<- [::game-db]
  :<- [::selected-card]
  (fn [[game-db selected] _]
    (when (and game-db selected)
      (rules/can-cast? game-db :player-1 selected))))
```

IMPORTANT: All Layer 3 subs guard against nil game-db with (when game-db ...).
The ::stack sub combines triggers AND spell objects for unified view.

### 3. Update core.cljs

Replace placeholder with actual subscription data display for verification:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.core
  (:require
    [fizzle.events.game :as events]
    [fizzle.subs.game :as subs]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]
    [reagent.dom :as rdom]))

(defn app
  []
  (let [hand @(rf/subscribe [::subs/hand])
        mana @(rf/subscribe [::subs/mana-pool])
        storm @(rf/subscribe [::subs/storm-count])
        stack @(rf/subscribe [::subs/stack])]
    [:div {:style {:padding "20px"}}
     [:h1 "Fizzle"]
     [:p (str "Hand: " (count hand) " cards")]
     [:p (str "Mana: " mana)]
     [:p (str "Storm: " storm)]
     [:p (str "Stack: " (count stack) " items")]]))

(defn ^:dev/after-load mount-root
  []
  (rf/clear-subscription-cache!)
  (rdom/render [app] (.getElementById js/document "app")))

(defn init
  []
  (rf/dispatch-sync [::events/init-game])
  (mount-root))
```

### 4. Run make fmt to fix formatting
### 5. Run make validate

## Key Engine API Reference

```
;; Player ID: :player-1 (keyword, NOT string)
;; Opponent ID: :opponent

;; rules.cljs
(cast-spell [db player-id object-id]) -> new-db
(resolve-spell [db player-id object-id]) -> new-db
(can-cast? [db player-id object-id]) -> truthy/nil (uses when-let)

;; queries.cljs
(get-hand [db player-id]) -> [{:object/id uuid :object/card {:card/name ...} ...} ...]
(get-mana-pool [db player-id]) -> {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 2 ...}
(get-storm-count [db player-id]) -> integer
(get-stack-items [db]) -> triggers ONLY [{:trigger/id uuid :trigger/type :storm ...} ...]
(get-objects-in-zone [db player-id zone]) -> objects filtered by owner

;; triggers.cljs
(resolve-trigger [db trigger]) -> new-db (multimethod on :trigger/type)
(remove-trigger [db trigger-id]) -> new-db (retracts trigger entity)

;; cards/iggy_pop.cljs
cards/all-cards -> vector of all 3 card defs
cards/dark-ritual, cards/cabal-ritual, cards/brain-freeze -> individual defs
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/game.cljs created with 4 events: ::init-game, ::select-card, ::cast-spell, ::resolve-top
- [ ] subs/game.cljs created with 7 subscriptions: ::game-db, ::hand, ::mana-pool, ::storm-count, ::stack, ::selected-card, ::can-cast?
- [ ] core.cljs updated to dispatch ::events/init-game and display subscription data
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] Browser shows Hand: 3 cards, Mana: map with :black 2, Storm: 0, Stack: 0 items
- [ ] No console errors in browser dev tools

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO game logic in events (delegate to engine functions only)
- NO raw datascript queries in events except in ::init-game setup (use queries.cljs functions)
- NO direct atom manipulation (re-frame events only)
- NO hardcoded entity IDs (use queries to look up eids)
- NO mutable state outside re-frame app-db
- NO string player IDs (use keyword :player-1, not string p1)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases and Troubleshooting)

**Edge Case: ::cast-spell with no selected card**
- Guard: check (and selected (rules/can-cast? ...)) before calling engine
- Return db unchanged if guard fails

**Edge Case: ::resolve-top with empty stack**
- Guard: check (seq stack-triggers) and (seq stack-objects)
- Return db unchanged if nothing to resolve

**Edge Case: nil game-db in subscriptions (before init)**
- All Layer 3 subs MUST wrap in (when game-db ...)
- Returns nil if game-db not yet initialized

**Edge Case: can-cast? returns nil not false**
- rules/can-cast? uses when-let, returns nil for non-existent objects
- Treat nil as falsy in guards

**Stack has two item types (IMPORTANT)**
- Triggers: from get-stack-items (entities with :trigger/stack-order)
- Spell objects: from get-objects-in-zone for :stack zone
- These are SEPARATE queries - triggers are NOT objects
- ::resolve-top must check triggers first, then spell objects
- ::stack sub combines both for unified view

**Opponent player needed for mill**
- Brain Freeze mills opponent via :effect/target :opponent
- Need opponent player entity with :player/is-opponent true
- Need opponent library with cards so mill has targets
- Without opponent, mill effect silently fails

**If compilation fails:**
- Check namespace requires are alphabetically sorted
- Run make fmt before make validate
- Check browser console for runtime errors

title: Add lose-life and gain-life effects
design:
## Goal

Implement :lose-life and :gain-life effect types that modify player life totals. These are foundational effects needed for City of Brass (deals damage when tapped) and win/loss condition checking.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours (simple effects, follows established patterns)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Effect multimethod pattern (see :draw implementation)
- src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs:46 - :player/life already in schema
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs - Add get-life-total query

### 2. Write tests first (TDD):

**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs

| Test Name | Bug It Catches |
|-----------|----------------|
| test-lose-life-reduces-total | Basic arithmetic wrong |
| test-lose-life-zero-is-noop | Spurious state changes on no-op |
| test-lose-life-can-go-negative | Incorrect clamping at 0 |
| test-lose-life-negative-amount-is-noop | Missing input validation |
| test-lose-life-nonexistent-player-is-noop | Crash on invalid player |
| test-gain-life-increases-total | Basic arithmetic wrong |
| test-gain-life-zero-is-noop | Spurious state changes on no-op |
| test-gain-life-accumulates | State not persisting correctly |
| test-gain-life-negative-amount-is-noop | Missing input validation |
| test-gain-life-nonexistent-player-is-noop | Crash on invalid player |

**Assertions for each test:**
- Life total changes by expected amount (or stays same for no-op)
- Other game state unchanged (mana pool, hand, etc.)
- Works with :effect/target for targeting specific player

### 3. Implementation checklist:
- [ ] Add get-life-total query to db/queries.cljs:
  ```clojure
  (defn get-life-total [db player-id]
    (d/q '[:find ?life .
           :in $ ?pid
           :where [?e :player/id ?pid]
                  [?e :player/life ?life]]
         db player-id))
  ```
- [ ] Add (defmethod execute-effect :lose-life ...) to effects.cljs
- [ ] Add (defmethod execute-effect :gain-life ...) to effects.cljs
- [ ] Both read :effect/amount for how much life to change
- [ ] Both read :effect/target (default: player-id) for who is affected
- [ ] Guard: amount <= 0 returns db unchanged (no-op)
- [ ] Guard: nonexistent player returns db unchanged (no-op)
- [ ] Return db directly (not a map wrapper)

### 4. Loss condition check (separate concern):
- Do NOT set :game/loss-condition in these effects
- Loss condition will be checked separately after each action
- Keeps effects focused on single responsibility

## Success Criteria

- [ ] test-lose-life-reduces-total passes: lose 3 from 20 → 17 life
- [ ] test-lose-life-zero-is-noop passes: lose 0 → no change
- [ ] test-lose-life-can-go-negative passes: lose 25 from 20 → -5 life
- [ ] test-lose-life-negative-amount-is-noop passes: lose -5 → no change (not gain)
- [ ] test-lose-life-nonexistent-player-is-noop passes: invalid player → no crash
- [ ] test-gain-life-increases-total passes: gain 5 from 20 → 25 life
- [ ] test-gain-life-zero-is-noop passes: gain 0 → no change
- [ ] test-gain-life-accumulates passes: gain 3 twice from 20 → 26 life
- [ ] test-gain-life-negative-amount-is-noop passes: gain -5 → no change (not lose)
- [ ] test-gain-life-nonexistent-player-is-noop passes: invalid player → no crash
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (make test)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Negative Amount**
- lose-life with negative amount should NOT be treated as gain-life
- gain-life with negative amount should NOT be treated as lose-life
- Both: amount <= 0 → return db unchanged (no-op)
- Why: Prevents confusing behavior from malformed effect data

**Edge Case: Nonexistent Player**
- If player-id doesn't exist in db, return db unchanged
- Follow same pattern as :draw effect (guard via if-let on query result)
- Why: Prevents crashes from bad effect targets

**Edge Case: nil Amount**
- If :effect/amount is nil, treat as 0 (no-op)
- Use (get effect :effect/amount 0) pattern
- Why: Defensive programming against malformed effects

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow :draw effect pattern at effects.cljs:71-102
- Same guard structure: check amount, check player exists, then modify

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO setting :game/loss-condition in lose-life (separate concern)
- ❌ NO capping life at 0 (MTG allows negative life)
- ❌ NO capping life gain (no maximum life in normal rules)
- ❌ NO crashing on invalid inputs (guard clauses, return db unchanged)
- ❌ NO treating negative amount as opposite effect (lose -5 ≠ gain 5)

title: Life <= 0 triggers loss condition
design:
## Goal
When a player's life total reaches 0 or below, set game loss condition.

## Context
Epic success criterion: 'Game ends when life ≤ 0 or library empty on draw'
Currently lose-life effect reduces life but never checks if life <= 0.
Schema has :game/loss-condition but life-based loss never triggered.
Draw from empty library already sets loss condition - follow same pattern.

## Investigation Needed
- Review lose-life effect implementation
- Check how draw effect sets :game/loss-condition :empty-library
- Review :game/loss-condition field in schema

## Implementation Steps
1. RED: Write test that life reaching 0 sets :game/loss-condition
2. GREEN: Add life check to lose-life effect
3. RED: Write test that life going negative also triggers loss
4. GREEN: Verify <= 0 check works
5. RED: Write test that game displays loss when condition set
6. VERIFY: All tests pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Life reaching 0 sets :game/loss-condition :life-zero
- [ ] Life going negative also triggers loss
- [ ] Loss condition consistent with empty library pattern
- [ ] Game recognizes player has lost
close_reason: Implemented life <= 0 loss condition with tests. Loss condition :life-zero set when life reaches 0 or below via :lose-life effect.

title: Task 5: Core Rules (can-cast?, cast-spell, resolve-spell)
design:
## Goal

Implement the core rules functions that tie together mana, zones, and effects to enable the full Dark Ritual cast→resolve pipeline.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Context
Completed Tasks 1-4: Schema, mana system, zone transitions, effect interpreter.

We have all the building blocks:
- mana/can-pay?, mana/pay-mana, mana/add-mana
- zones/move-to-zone
- effects/execute-effect with :add-mana

Now we need rules.cljs to orchestrate them into can-cast?, cast-spell, resolve-spell.

## Implementation

### 1. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.engine.rules-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
            [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]
            [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]))

;; === can-cast? tests ===

(deftest can-cast-returns-false-without-mana-test
  (testing "can-cast? returns false when player lacks mana"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)]
      (is (false? (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 (:object/id ritual)))))))

(deftest can-cast-returns-true-with-mana-test
  (testing "can-cast? returns true when player has sufficient mana"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)]
      (is (true? (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 (:object/id ritual)))))))

(deftest can-cast-returns-false-wrong-zone-test
  (testing "can-cast? returns false when card not in hand (on stack)"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 2}))  ;; Extra mana for test
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          obj-id (:object/id ritual)
          ;; Move card to stack (simulating cast without using cast-spell)
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db obj-id :stack)]
      ;; Card is on stack, not in hand - should return false
      (is (false? (rules/can-cast? db' :player-1 obj-id))))))

;; === cast-spell tests ===

(deftest cast-spell-moves-to-stack-test
  (testing "cast-spell moves card from hand to stack"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 (:object/id ritual))]
      (is (= 0 (count (q/get-hand db' :player-1))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :stack)))))))

(deftest cast-spell-pays-mana-test
  (testing "cast-spell deducts mana cost from pool"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 2}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 (:object/id ritual))]
      (is (= 1 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest cast-spell-increments-storm-test
  (testing "cast-spell increments storm count"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 (:object/id ritual))]
      (is (= 1 (q/get-storm-count db' :player-1))))))

;; === resolve-spell tests ===

(deftest resolve-spell-executes-effects-test
  (testing "resolve-spell executes card effects"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (-> db
                  (rules/cast-spell :player-1 (:object/id ritual))
                  (rules/resolve-spell :player-1 (:object/id ritual)))]
      ;; Dark Ritual adds BBB
      (is (= 3 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest resolve-spell-moves-to-graveyard-test
  (testing "resolve-spell moves card from stack to graveyard"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (-> db
                  (rules/cast-spell :player-1 (:object/id ritual))
                  (rules/resolve-spell :player-1 (:object/id ritual)))]
      (is (= 0 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :stack))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :graveyard)))))))

;; === Full pipeline test ===

(deftest dark-ritual-full-pipeline-test
  (testing "Dark Ritual: cast with B, resolve to get BBB, storm = 1"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          obj-id (:object/id ritual)

          ;; Verify can cast
          _ (is (true? (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 obj-id)))

          ;; Cast it
          db-cast (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 obj-id)
          _ (is (= 0 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db-cast :player-1))))
          _ (is (= 1 (q/get-storm-count db-cast :player-1)))
          _ (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db-cast :player-1 :stack))))

          ;; Resolve it
          db-resolved (rules/resolve-spell db-cast :player-1 obj-id)]
      (is (= 3 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db-resolved :player-1))))
      (is (= 0 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db-resolved :player-1 :stack))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db-resolved :player-1 :graveyard)))))))
```

### 3. Key implementation details

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.rules
  "Core game rules for Fizzle.

   Orchestrates mana, zones, and effects into casting operations.
   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db"
  (:require [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
            [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]
            [fizzle.engine.effects :as effects]))

(defn can-cast?
  "Check if a player can cast a card.
   
   Requires:
   - Card is in player's hand
   - Player has sufficient mana
   
   Returns false if object doesn't exist."
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [obj (q/get-object db object-id)]
    (when obj
      (let [card (:object/card obj)
            cost (:card/mana-cost card)]
        (and (= :hand (:object/zone obj))
             (mana/can-pay? db player-id cost))))))

(defn- increment-storm
  "Increment a player's storm count."
  [db player-id]
  (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)
        current (q/get-storm-count db player-id)]
    (d/db-with db [[:db/add player-eid :player/storm-count (inc current)]])))

(defn cast-spell
  "Cast a spell from hand.
   
   - Pays mana cost
   - Moves card to stack
   - Increments storm count
   
   Caller should verify can-cast? first."
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [obj (q/get-object db object-id)
        card (:object/card obj)
        cost (:card/mana-cost card)]
    (-> db
        (mana/pay-mana player-id cost)
        (zones/move-to-zone object-id :stack)
        (increment-storm player-id))))

(defn resolve-spell
  "Resolve a spell on the stack.
   
   - Executes all effects
   - Moves card to graveyard"
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [obj (q/get-object db object-id)
        card (:object/card obj)
        effects-list (:card/effects card)]
    (as-> db db'
      (reduce (fn [d effect] (effects/execute-effect d player-id effect))
              db'
              (or effects-list []))  ;; Handle nil effects gracefully
      (zones/move-to-zone db' object-id :graveyard))))
```

npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js

## Success Criteria

- [ ] can-cast? returns false when player lacks mana
- [ ] can-cast? returns true when player has sufficient mana
- [ ] can-cast? returns false when card not in hand (on stack)
- [ ] cast-spell moves card from hand to stack
- [ ] cast-spell deducts mana cost from pool
- [ ] cast-spell increments storm count
- [ ] resolve-spell executes card effects (adds BBB)
- [ ] resolve-spell moves card from stack to graveyard
- [ ] Full pipeline test passes: cast with B, resolve to get BBB, storm = 1
- [ ] All 9 rules tests pass
- [ ] Verification command passes: npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Non-existent object-id**
- get-object returns nil for invalid object-id
- can-cast? wrapped in (when obj ...) to return nil/false gracefully
- cast-spell/resolve-spell: Caller should verify can-cast? first per contract
- Design decision: No defensive checks in cast-spell/resolve-spell (per anti-pattern "caller checks can-cast?")

**Edge Case: Card with nil effects**
- Dark Ritual has effects, but future cards might not
- resolve-spell uses (or effects-list []) to handle nil gracefully
- reduce on empty vector is no-op, safe

**Edge Case: Card with nil mana cost (free spell)**
- mana/can-pay? returns true for nil/empty cost (already tested in mana_test)
- mana/pay-mana handles nil/empty cost as no-op (already tested)
- No special handling needed in rules.cljs

**Why wrong-zone test requires zones/move-to-zone**
- Initial state has card in hand only
- Must move card to stack to test can-cast? returns false for non-hand cards
- Test explicitly calls zones/move-to-zone (not cast-spell) to isolate the can-cast? behavior
- Catches bug: can-cast? not checking :object/zone

**Function Contract: Caller Responsibility**
- can-cast? is safe to call with any inputs (returns false/nil gracefully)
- cast-spell and resolve-spell assume can-cast? was verified
- This matches MTG rules: you can't cast if not legal, validation happens before cast

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO validation/throwing in cast-spell (caller checks can-cast?)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in rules (effects are data-driven)
- ❌ NO side effects (purity: rules transform db)
- ❌ NO defensive nil checks in cast-spell/resolve-spell (violates contract)
